<!-- #Note: Báo cáo audit Web 2.0 — máy sinh từ workflow (read-only). | WEB2.0. -->

# 🔍 Web 2.0 — Rà Soát Toàn Diện (2026-06-20)

Rà soát **tĩnh, read-only** toàn bộ Web 2.0: Cloudflare worker, Render routes/services, Firebase touchpoints, ~90 shared module, mọi trang (gồm native-orders/so-order/live-chat). **Không gọi mạng, không gửi gì tới FB/Pancake/Zalo.** TPOS & Web 1.0 loại trừ. Mỗi lỗi nghi ngờ được 1 agent kiểm chứng độc lập (adversarial); chỉ lỗi `real=true` đưa vào đây.

## Tổng quan

- **Đơn vị audit**: 56 (Infra/Routes/Services/Shared/Pages)
- **Lỗi đã xác nhận** (đã khử trùng lặp): **121** — 🔴 critical 6 · 🟠 high 43 · 🟡 medium 39 · ⚪ low 32
- (Tổng verdict real trước khử trùng: 122 — nhiều lỗi auth bị nhiều agent xác nhận trùng.)

> ⚠️ Đây là phát hiện review tĩnh (độ tin cậy cao, đa số `confidence=high`) — vẫn nên đọc lại code tại `file:line` trước khi sửa.

## 🚨 Lỗi đã xác nhận (sắp theo mức độ)

### 🔴 CRITICAL (6)

1. **[high]** In render.com/routes/web2-returns.js the POST '/' handler, thu_ve_1_phan branch (lines 626-647), builds items entirely from req.body.items and computes walletCredit = totalAmount = Σ(Number(it.price) × Number(it.quantity)) with NO server-side cross-check. Key facts verified: 1) No price validation: items.price/quantity are taken verbatim from the client; the branch never calls \_resolveSourceOrder, and sourceOrderCode is optional (line 628, falls back to null) — only stored for traceability, never used to validate the credit. Contrast with the khong_nhan_hang branch (line 620-625) which resolve
    - **Fix:** Two changes, both needed: (A) Add auth: apply requireWeb2AuthSoft to the writes, matching siblings. In web2-returns.js: `const { requireWeb2AuthSoft } = require('../middleware/web2-auth');` then `router.post('/', requireWeb2AuthSoft, async ...)`, plus the same on `/:code/approve` and DELETE `/:code`. Derive \_user from req.web2User (set by the middleware) instead of trusting body. (B) Validate the
2. **[high]** render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js (2034 lines) applies NO authentication anywhere: `grep` for `web2-auth|requireWeb2Auth|router.use|x-web2-token|WEB2_AUTH` returns zero matches, and server.js:911 mounts it bare (`app.use('/api/web2-zalo', web2ZaloRoutes)`) with no wrapper.     - **Fix:** At the top of render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js add `const { requireWeb2AuthSoft, requireWeb2Admin } = require('../middleware/web2-auth');` then `router.use(requireWeb2AuthSoft);` immediately after `const router = express.Router();`. This gates all reads/writes (returns 401 when WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 and no valid x-web2-token; soft-warns otherwise, matching peer behavior). Additionally harden the destr
3. **[high]** The entire web2-fb-posts router is unauthenticated. None of its mutating endpoints are gated: POST /connect (285), POST /disconnect (314), POST /refresh-pages (326), POST /publish (371), POST /delete (541), POST /post-edit (563), POST /draft (829), DELETE /draft/:id (863), POST /ad-entry (718), DELETE /ad-entry/:id (798). 4. **[high]** render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js (2033 lines, 44 routes) applies zero authentication: it never requires ../middleware/web2-auth, has no router.use, no requireWeb2AuthSoft/Admin, and never references req.web2User (grep returns NONE). server.js mounts it at line 911 via app.use('/api/web2-zalo', web2ZaloRoutes) with no auth wrapper, and server.js applies no global web2-auth middleware (auth is per-router in this codebase). Additionally gate the destructive account-management routes with
5. **[high]** The entire router render.com/routes/web2-fb-posts.js applies no authentication on any endpoint. 6. **[high]** render.com/routes/web2-fb-posts.js declares all 16 routes via bare `router.get/post/delete(...)` with NO auth middleware. The mutating endpoints are fully unauthenticated: POST /connect (285) stores an attacker-supplied FB user+page token, POST /publish (371) posts to the shop's real customer-facing FB pages using stored page tokens, POST /delete (541) deletes live posts, POST /post-edit (563) edits live captions, POST /disconnect (314) runs `DELETE FROM web2_fb_post_tokens` wiping the connection. Verified there is NO global write-auth layer: server.js global middleware (lines 97-150) only set
    - **Fix:** In render.com/routes/web2-fb-posts.js, add per-route Web 2.0 auth middleware matching the gated peers. Import at top: `const { requireWeb2Auth, requireWeb2Admin, requireWeb2AuthSoft } = require('../middleware/web2-auth');`. Then: apply `requireWeb2Admin` to the high-risk credential/destructive routes — POST /connect (285), POST /disconnect (314), POST /publish (371), POST /delete (541), POST /post

### 🟠 HIGH (43)

1. **[high]** defect. cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/token-handler.js lines 42-45 hardcode plaintext TPOS credentials: CREDENTIALS_BY_COMPANY = { 1: {username:'nvktlive1', password:'Aa@28612345678', ...}, 2: {username:'nvktshop1', password:'Aa@28612345678', ...} }. The file is git-tracked and committed (commit ced418943 'feat(worker): proxy auth for /api/token — credentials server-side only'), so the working password lives in git history.\n\nThis is a genuine exposed production secret, not test data — these are real TPOS (production ERP) account passwords. Worse, the IDENTICAL credential is duplicated a
    - **Fix:** 1) Rotate the TPOS password immediately (it is in git history and shipped to browsers). 2) In the worker: thread env into the handler — change worker.js line 111 to `return handleTokenRequest(request, env);`, update handleTokenRequest(request, env) signature, and replace the hardcoded object with values read from env (e.g. env.TPOS_PASSWORD_LIVE / env.TPOS_PASSWORD_SHOP set via `wrangler secret pu
2. **[high]** unauthenticated open-proxy / SSRF in two reachable Worker endpoints. `/api/proxy` -> handleGenericProxy (proxy-handler.js:68-124): targetUrl is read verbatim from the `?url=` query param (line 69), then fetched via fetchWithRetry (line 115) with NO allowlist, NO scheme check, NO private-IP block. Worse, the caller also controls the HTTP method, the request body, and ARBITRARY request headers via `?headers=<JSON>` (lines 103-111) — so the attacker can inject Authorization/Cookie/etc. to any target.     - **Fix:** In handleGenericProxy (proxy-handler.js) and handleImageProxy (image-proxy-handler.js), validate `?url=` before fetching: 1) Parse with `new URL(targetUrl)`; reject anything where protocol is not 'http:' or 'https:'. 2) Enforce a hostname allowlist of the only origins these endpoints legitimately need (tomato.tpos.vn, _.pancake.vn, content.pancake.vn, graph.facebook.com / _.fbcdn.net, \*.onrender.c

15. **[high]** `render.com/routes/web2-fb-posts.js` applies NO auth middleware on ANY route. POST /connect (285-311) takes an attacker-supplied `req.body.token`, exchanges it, fetches /me + pages (with page access tokens), and persists via saveToken keyed by user_id with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE. loadToken (129-138) always reads `ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1`, so whichever row was written most recently IS the active shop connection — an attacker who connects their own FB token (different user_id => new row, or same user_id => overwrite) becomes the live connection used by /status, /publish, /list, /engageme

- **Fix:** Treat the token store and all mutations as authenticated state. 36. **[high]** The sidebar's escapeHtml() (web2-sidebar.js:344-349) uses the `div.textContent = s; return div.innerHTML` round-trip, which escapes &, <, > but does NOT escape double-quote (") or single-quote ('). This is correct, documented browser behavior: HTML text-node serialization does not escape quotes. The escaped `username` and `displayName` values are then interpolated into HTML ATTRIBUTE context at lines 572 (`title="${username}"`) and 574 (`title="${displayName} (${username})"`), both double-quote delimited. A value containing a double-quote breaks out of the attribute — e.g. displayName/username

- **Fix:** Replace the sidebar-local escapeHtml (web2-sidebar.js:344-349) with the canonical 5-char escaper. Minimal change: `function escapeHtml(s){ if (window.Web2Escape) return window.Web2Escape.escapeHtml(s); if (s==null) return ''; return String(s).replace(/&/g,'&amp;').replace(/</g,'&lt;').replace(/>/g,'&gt;').replace(/"/g,'&quot;').replace(/'/g,'&#39;'); }` — this escapes " and ' so attribute-context

1. **[high]** lost-update race confirmed by reading both endpoints in render.com/routes/web2-products.js.\n\nPATCH /:code (671-743): builds `stock = $N` where `$N` is the client's literal `req.body.stock` (an ABSOLUTE set). It takes `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` at line 710, but the only guard (720-736) checks `newStock < pendingQty`; it never compares the locked DB stock against the value the client originally read. The by-phone reset handler logs `phone=${variants.join('|')}` to console on every call, and `variants` (built at lines 232-234 from the request body phone) contains the full customer phone number plus its normalized forms. So a real customer phone (PII) is written in cleartext to Render server logs. The defect is real and triggers on every non-dryRun (and dryRun) call. HOWEVER I reassessed severity DOWN from high to low: (1) the endpoint is admin-only, gated by authOk() requiring the x-admin-secret/CLEANUP_SECRET header (lines 24-27, 219) — not a public o

- **Fix:** Mask the phone in the log line. Replace lines 299-301 with: `const maskedPhone = digits.length >= 4 ? '****' + digits.slice(-4) : '****'; console.log(`[web2-wallet-reset/by-phone] ${dryRun ? 'DRY ' : ''}phone=${maskedPhone} → ${JSON.stringify(steps)}`);` — logs only the last 4 digits plus the per-step rowCounts already in `steps`, never the full variants array.

15. **[high]** at render.com/routes/web2-products.js:948-967. The adjust-stock UPDATE uses `SET stock = GREATEST(0, stock + $1)`, so any oversell (stock+delta < 0) is clamped to exactly 0 and the absorbed negative magnitude is permanently lost. The response's results[].stock returns the clamped value (0), and the warning string does not include the pre-clamp result, so the caller cannot recover how much was over-sold. This causes inventory to drift positive vs. reality. The issue is REAL.\n\nHowever, the report overstates one point: it claims the caller "cannot reliably detect the oversell." In fact the warn

- **Fix:** Surface the requested vs. applied delta (or pre-clamp result) so callers can compute exact oversell. Change the UPDATE to also return the unclamped value, e.g.:\n\n const r = await client.query(\n `UPDATE web2_products\n SET stock = GREATEST(0, stock + $1), updated_at = $2\n WHERE code = $3\n RETURNING code, stock, (stock) AS applied_stock, (\n SELECT stock + $1 FROM web2_products WHERE code = $3\

16. **[high]** but lower severity than reported. In render.com/routes/web2-variants.js, POST `/` (lines 330-336) calls `_suggestShortCode(pool, value, groupName)` when the client omits shortCode, then INSERTs (lines 346-359). `_suggestShortCode` (lines 136-187) does a plain `SELECT value, group_name, short_code FROM web2_variants WHERE short_code IS NOT NULL` against the pool with NO transaction, NO `FOR UPDATE`, NO advisory lock, and builds an in-memory `usedCodes` set to pick a free code. The table has a partial UNIQUE index `idx_web2_variants_short_code` (lines 65-67), so a duplicate short_code INSERT rai

- **Fix:** In POST `/` (web2-variants.js ~line 362), distinguish auto-suggested codes from user-supplied ones and retry on short_code conflict instead of surfacing 409. Track whether the code was auto-suggested (e.g. `const wasAutoSuggested = !b.shortCode;`). In the catch block, when `err.code === '23505'` and `err.detail` includes 'short_code' AND `wasAutoSuggested`, loop a few times: re-call `\_suggestShort

17. **[high]** the code at render.com/routes/web2-users.js:783: the opportunistic expired-session cleanup `pool.query('DELETE FROM web2_user_sessions WHERE expires_at < $1', [now]).catch(() => {})` is indeed fire-and-forget with errors fully swallowed. The existing setInterval (line 94) only purges the in-memory `_loginFails` rate-limit map, NOT the DB sessions table, so there is no other periodic DB cleanup. So the two factual observations are accurate.\n\nHowever the "DATA-LOSS" framing is wrong and the severity is genuinely low (the auditor's own rating and "harmless for correctness" note are correct):\n\

- **Fix:** Minimal: log the swallowed error instead of discarding it — `pool.query('DELETE FROM web2_user_sessions WHERE expires_at < $1', [now]).catch((e) => console.warn('[WEB2-USERS] session cleanup failed:', e.message));`. Optional hardening: move the DELETE into the same setInterval already present at line 94 (it has a pool in scope via the module/app.locals) or a cron so cleanup is decoupled from login

18. **[high]** but lower severity than reported. In render.com/routes/web2-users.js the single-flight guard for ensureTables (line 339-341, 406) is a plain module-level boolean `tablesReady` with NO in-flight promise lock and NO transaction/advisory lock (verified: zero pg_advisory/BEGIN/ON CONFLICT in the function). On a cold start two concurrent requests can both read `tablesReady===false` and both run the body. The seed at lines 393-405 is a genuine check-then-insert TOCTOU: `SELECT COUNT(*)` (394) then, if `n===0`, `INSERT ... VALUES ('admin', ...)` (398-403). The table has `username VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL

- **Fix:** Two-part fix as the auditor suggests, both small: (1) Cache an in-flight promise to make ensureTables truly single-flight: `let _ensurePromise = null; function ensureTables(pool){ if (tablesReady) return Promise.resolve(); return (_ensurePromise ||= doEnsure(pool).then(()=>{tablesReady=true;}).catch(e=>{ _ensurePromise=null; throw e; })); }` moving the current body into `doEnsure`. (2) Make the se

19. **[high]** The mechanism is real but the trigger is an edge case, so I downgrade severity from the reported "high" to "low". VERIFIED FACTS: - db/web2-zalo-schema.js:185 — the ONLY dedup on web2_zalo_messages is the PARTIAL unique index `uq_web2_zalo_msg_id ON (account_key, msg_id) WHERE msg_id IS NOT NULL`. There is no fallback unique constraint on cli_msg_id. So a row with msg_id NULL has ZERO dedup protection. - web2-zalo.js:274-298 — \_persistIncoming uses `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING id` and derives `isNew = ins.rowCount > 0`, then `unreadDelta = isNew && direction==='in' ? 1 : 0`, applied via `

Simplest minimal fix in \_persistIncoming (web2-zalo.js): gate the unread increment on a non-null msg_id, so id-less messages never inflate the counter even if re-delivered: const isNew = ins.rowCount > 0; // Chỉ cộng unread khi tin có msg_id (đã được dedup bởi uq_web2_zalo_msg_id). // Tin thiếu msg_id KHÔNG có c

20. **[high]** The reported defect is real and the mechanism is exactly as described. In POST /refresh (render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:856-872), each chunk callback calls fetchJt with a single retry (line 862-866); if BOTH attempts throw (timeout/HTTP error/jtexpress block), the exception propagates before reaching \_upsertTracked at line 867, so Promise.allSettled records the result as 'rejected' -> fail++ and NO write happens. I verified \_upsertTracked (line 376-413, INSERT...ON CONFLICT...SET last_fetched_at=EXCLUDED.last_fetched_at at line 395) is the ONLY place last_fetched_at is ever written — al

- **Fix:** In the chunk handler, bump the timestamp even on failure so failing codes round-robin to the back. 21. **[high]** but low-impact. The defect described at web2-wallet-service.js:122-136 genuinely exists. `emitAfterCommit` registers the SSE wallet emit on `client._afterCommit` ONLY when that array is present; otherwise it falls back to `process.nextTick(doEmit)`. The `_afterCommit` array is created exclusively by `withTransaction` (db/with-transaction.js:67), so any caller that passes a raw PoolClient obtained from its own `pool.connect()` (outside `withTransaction`) takes the nextTick path. Two such callers actually exist and pass a raw client into the wallet service: 1. services/web2-sepay-matching.js:920

- **Fix:** Make the two raw-client callers mirror `withTransaction`'s post-COMMIT hook so `emitAfterCommit` takes the deferred path. In both `resolveWeb2PendingMatch` (web2-sepay-matching.js) and `revertMatchAudit` (web2-match-audit.js): 1. Right after `const client = isPool ? await db.connect() : db;` add: `if (!Array.isArray(client._afterCommit)) client._afterCommit = [];` (When `db` is already a client fr

22. **[high]** The race itself is REAL and reachable, but the reporter's specific "listener leak" mechanism is partly inaccurate. Reachability confirmed: in server.js:907-911, restoreSessions() runs via .then() and is NOT awaited before app.use('/api/web2-zalo', ...) on the very next line. So the Zalo routes are mounted (and served) while restoreAll() is still iterating accounts. restoreAll (zca:765-779) does `await loginWithCredentials` per account, and a manual POST /accounts/:key/reconnect (route:560) or /login-cookie (route:588) can arrive for the same accountKey during that window. 23. **[high]** but lower severity than reported. In web2/shared/page-builder.js removeRecord (614-658), `idx` (623) and `snap` (624) are captured synchronously, then Web2Optimistic.run (web2-optimistic.js:74-105) runs apply() synchronously and fires api.remove(code) async. rollback() (633-640) runs ONLY if that network call rejects, and it splices `snap` at `Math.min(idx, STATE.records.length)` into whatever `STATE.records` currently is, plus does `STATE.total += 1`. The SSE handler (696-707) debounces 600ms then calls load(), which REASSIGNS STATE.records to a fresh array (343) and overwrites STATE.total (3

- **Fix:** In the rollback closure (page-builder.js:633-640), do not reuse the stale captured `idx`/total math. Because a failed delete means the record still exists server-side, the simplest robust fix is to let load() resync as the source of truth: const rollback = () => { if (!snap) return; if (STATE.records.some((x) => x.code === snap.code)) return; // reload already restored it load(); // authoritative

24. **[high]** against web2/shared/web2-ck-assign-picker.js. The debounce timer `t` (line 145, declared as a closure-local inside ensureDom; armed at line 148 via `t = setTimeout(load, 350)`, cleared only at line 147 on the NEXT keystroke) is genuinely never cleared by close() (lines 157-161, which only sets `_el.hidden = true` and `_ctx = null`). So the interleaving holds: type in search -> close within 350ms -> the pending timer still fires -> load() runs. The stale load() DOES execute: it writes 'Đang tải…' into the hidden #w2capList and issues a wasted GET to /api/web2/balance-history. This is real but m

- **Fix:** Lift the debounce timer to module scope so close() can cancel it, and (optionally) early-return from load() when hidden. Concretely: replace the closure-local `let t = null;` inside ensureDom with a module-level `let _searchTimer = null;` (alongside \_el/\_ctx near line 90). Update the oninput handler (lines 146-149) to use `_searchTimer`. In close() (lines 157-161) add `if (\_searchTimer) { clearTim

25. **[high]** The mechanism is technically correct. In web2-bill-service.js:514-560, `_printFrame` is a module-level singleton iframe and the `printed` guard (line 526) is a per-call local, not per-document. `go` never runs synchronously — it fires via `win.onload` (line 547) or the double-rAF (lines 554-555). So if two `_printViaIframe` calls run synchronously back-to-back, call B's `doc.open()/write()/close()` (lines 549-551) clobbers call A's document before A's `go` fires, and `win.onload = goB` overrides A's onload handler. A's surviving double-rAF closure then calls `win.print()` on B's content → bill

- **Fix:** Defense-in-depth (cheap, worth doing). Simplest: create a fresh iframe per print job and remove it after print, eliminating the shared-document clobber entirely. Minimal change in `_printViaIframe`: always `const f = document.createElement('iframe')` (drop the `_printFrame` reuse), append, write, print in `go`, then `setTimeout(() => f.remove(), 1000)` after print. Alternatively serialize: keep a

- **Fix:** Add an in-flight guard to PS.startCamera so concurrent starts serialize: at the top, `if (state._starting) return; state._starting = true;` and wrap the existing body in try/finally that resets `state._starting = false;`. 27. **[high]** PARTIALLY REAL — defect exists but the reporter's stated impact is overstated. 28. **[high]** PARTIALLY REAL, downgraded to low. The structural claim is correct: the server PATCH echoes `_notify('update', code)` after commit (web2-products.js:905) to ALL clients including the originating tab, with NO self-echo suppression (no client request id). The SSE handler (web2-products-app.js:87-94) `await`s `_updateRowsBatch([code])`, which fetches the row and unconditionally calls `_updateRowInPlace` (web2-products-render.js:239-254). 29. **[high]** defect at web2/variants/js/web2-variants-app.js:345-387 (toggleActive). The function captures `idx = STATE.variants.findIndex(...)` once at line 347 and reuses that stale index inside apply (line 354), onSuccess (line 362), and rollback (line 368). 31. **[high]** defect in /Users/mac/Desktop/n2store/web2/video-beauty/js/video-beauty.js:72-99. previewLoop() reschedules via v.requestVideoFrameCallback(previewLoop) (line 77) WITHOUT storing the returned handle, and stopPreview() only calls cancelAnimationFrame(state.\_raf) (line 98), which cancels the rAF fallback path but NOT the rVFC path used on Chrome/Edge. ---

## 📚 Chi tiết theo khu vực (mọi unit)

## Infra

### infra:admin-routes

Five Express routers mounted under /api/admin (server.js:788-792), all Web 2.0 infra/maintenance endpoints operating on web2Db. admin-web2-data-reset backs up + TRUNCATEs product/order/PBH/cart/wallet tables (targets: products/inventory/web2-all/ck) plus one-off rename-to-nj and cleanup-dead helpers. admin-web2-wallet-reset backs up + wipes wallet/matching tables and rematches SePay rows, with a by-phone surgical reset for test cleanup. The three import routers are one-time migrations that backfill web2_customers from TPOS OData, Pancake INBOX conversations, and Web 1.0 customers. Every destructive endpoint is gated by an x-admin-secret / ?secret= check against CLEANUP_SECRET and refuses to run when web2Db === chatDb (so it cannot touch Web 1.0). web2_records is correctly protected from full truncation.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** Routers are required+mounted at boot in server.js:788-792 under /api/admin; no cron/interval — all endpoints are request-driven.; ADMIN_SECRET = process.env.CLEANUP_SECRET read once at module load (admin-web2-data-reset.js:20, admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:23, admin-web2-import-customers.js:17, admin-web2-import-pancake-customers.js:18, admin-web2-import-fb-links.js:19).; Global middleware chain that wraps every admin call: security headers (server.js:97), CORS allowlist incl x-admin-secret header (server.js:107-135), express.json 100mb (server.js:138), and a request logger that prints req.url for every non-health request (server.js:145-150).; admin-web2-wallet-reset web2-rematch-all lazy-requires ../services/web2-sepay-matching and ../../shared/node/fetch-utils.cjs per request (lines 154-155).; admin-web2-import-customers lazy-requires ../services/tpos-token-manager.getToken() per request (line 65).

| Control                                       | Handler                                                                               | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| POST /api/admin/web2-data-reset               | router.post web2-data-reset (admin-web2-data-reset.js:143)                            | authOk gate; validates mode in {backup,wipe} and confirm:'YES-RESET' for wipe; refuses if web2Db===chatDb; picks table allowlist by target (products\|inventory\|web2-all\|ck); for each table CREATE TABLE <t>_bak_<ts> AS TABLE <t>; if mode=wipe TRUNCATE ... RESTART IDENTITY (CASCADE only for inventory). Returns steps. Destructive, idempotent backup-then-truncate.  |
| GET /api/admin/web2-data-reset/status         | router.get web2-data-reset/status (admin-web2-data-reset.js:122)                      | authOk gate; returns per-table existence+COUNT(\*) for target tables. Read-only.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| POST /api/admin/web2-rename-to-nj             | router.post web2-rename-to-nj (admin-web2-data-reset.js:231)                          | authOk gate; refuses if web2Db===chatDb; one transaction: DELETE test orders fb_user_id LIKE 'TEST-NJ-%', remap native_orders NW-→NJ- codes + fast_sale_orders number/source_code + pbh_fulfillment_logs. dryRun rolls back. Mutating, transactional.                                                                                                                         |
| GET /api/admin/web2-tables                    | router.get web2-tables (admin-web2-data-reset.js:346)                                 | authOk gate; lists all public tables + sizes from pg_tables. Read-only.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| POST /api/admin/web2-cleanup-dead             | router.post web2-cleanup-dead (admin-web2-data-reset.js:362)                          | authOk gate; confirm:'YES-CLEANUP'; refuses if web2Db===chatDb; DROP every table matching %_bak_%, DELETE FROM web2_records WHERE entity_slug IN ('deliveryzone','printer') (per-slug, correct), VACUUM ANALYZE web2_records. Destructive.                                                                                                                                    |
| POST /api/admin/web2-wallet-reset             | router.post web2-wallet-reset (admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:54)                         | authOk gate; mode in {backup,reset-rematch,full-wipe}; confirm:'YES-RESET' for non-backup; refuses if web2Db===chatDb; backups then TRUNCATE derived (or ALL for full-wipe) RESTART IDENTITY; reset-rematch also UPDATE web2_balance_history SET assignment cols NULL WHERE TRUE. Destructive.                                                                                |
| POST /api/admin/web2-rematch-all              | router.post web2-rematch-all (admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:144)                         | authOk gate; refuses if web2Db===chatDb; keyset-paginates web2_balance_history transfer_type='in' id>afterId LIMIT batch(<=500); calls sepayMatching.processWeb2Match per row (per-row try/catch swallows errors). Returns lastId+done. Mutating.                                                                                                                             |
| GET /api/admin/web2-wallet-reset/backups      | router.get web2-wallet-reset/backups (admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:193)                 | authOk gate; lists information*schema tables LIKE %\_bak*%. Read-only. NOTE: does NOT refuse when web2Db===chatDb (would read chatDb backups), low risk.                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| POST /api/admin/web2-wallet-reset/by-phone    | router.post web2-wallet-reset/by-phone (admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:218)               | authOk gate; confirm:'YES-RESET'; validates digits.length>=9 (loose); builds phone variants; DELETEs from wallet_transactions/adjustments/customer_wallets + fast_sale_orders(partner_phone) + native_orders(phone), UPDATE unlink web2_balance_history + web2_payment_signals — all WHERE col = ANY(variants). NOTE: does NOT refuse web2Db===chatDb. Destructive per-phone. |
| POST /api/admin/web2-import-customers         | router.post web2-import-customers (admin-web2-import-customers.js:55)                 | authed gate; gets TPOS token; paginates TPOS Partner OData (TOP=200, maxPages<=1000), pre-dedupes by normalized phone in a Map, then batched (500) INSERT...ON CONFLICT(phone) DO UPDATE into web2_customers. dryRun returns counts. Bulk upsert — TPOS coupling (see bugs).                                                                                                  |
| POST /api/admin/web2-import-pancake-customers | router.post web2-import-pancake-customers (admin-web2-import-pancake-customers.js:51) | authed gate; reads JWT from chatDb pancake_accounts or env; paginates Pancake INBOX conversations per page via time cursor; gathers fb_id/phone; upserts web2_customers ON CONFLICT(phone) merging fb_psids/alt_phones. dryRun returns counts. Reads chatDb (Web1) for JWT only.                                                                                              |
| POST /api/admin/web2-import-fb-links          | router.post web2-import-fb-links (admin-web2-import-fb-links.js:31)                   | authed gate; requires BOTH web2Db & chatDb; read-only SELECT from Web1 customers (fb_id NOT NULL), groups by normalized phone, batched (300) upsert into web2_customers ON CONFLICT(phone). dryRun returns sample. Cross-layer read of Web1 (documented one-time migration).                                                                                                  |

**Liên kết:** render.com/server.js:788-792 (mounts all 5 routers under /api/admin), render.com/services/tpos-token-manager.js (getToken for TPOS import), render.com/services/web2-sepay-matching.js (processWeb2Match for rematch-all), shared/node/fetch-utils.cjs (fetchWithTimeout), render.com/routes/web2-balance-history (reprocess-unmatched referenced in 'next' hints), web2/admin pages / web2-data-reset admin UI consumers (documented in MEMORY reference_web2_data_wipe), Tables: web2_products, web2_variants, web2_customers, web2_customer_wallets, web2_balance_history, web2_payment_signals, native_orders, fast_sale_orders, web2_records (protected), Web1 customers + pancake_accounts (read-only via chatDb)

**SSE:** No SSE published by any of these routers. After a wipe/import, no web2:<entity> \_notify is emitted — open tabs (products, wallet, customers) will show stale data until manual refresh. Consider publishing web2:products / web2:customer-wallet / web2:customer-intents after destructive ops so subscribed tabs reload (none currently).

**console.log:** 7 — admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:299-301 console.log logs phone variants (`phone=${variants.join('|')}`) for by-phone reset — customer PII (phone numbers) written to Render logs.; admin-web2-data-reset.js:218 console.error('[web2-data-reset] error', e.message) — error text only, low risk.; admin-web2-data-reset.js:330 console.error('[web2-rename-to-nj] error') — low risk.; admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:135 console.error('[web2-wallet-reset] error') — low risk.; admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:187 console.error('[web2-rematch-all] error') — low risk.; Global logger server.js:147 logs req.url for every admin call — when secret passed via ?secret= the admin secret lands in logs (see security).

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `web1-coupling` web2-import-customers fetches live TPOS OData (tomato.tpos.vn) directly. Web 2.0 is explicitly forbidden from depending on the TPOS API. Even as a 'one-time' migration this is TPOS coupling inside a /api/admin Web 2.0 route, and the token comes from tpos-token-manager (Web 1.0 shared). — `admin-web2-import-customers.js:23,45-53,65` → Keep this strictly as a manual one-off and document it as TPOS-coupled; do not let any Web 2.0 runtime path call it. Long-term, run the import as an offline script rather than a live mounted route.
- 🟡 `data-loss` Documented intent (data-reset.js:58-59) says PBH shadow slug 'fastsaleorder-invoice' in web2_records should be wiped per-slug via delete-all during target='web2-all', but the wipe path only TRUNCATEs the fixed WEB2_ALL_TABLES and NEVER deletes that slug from web2_records. So a 'web2-all' reset leave — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:58-59 vs 191-201` → Either implement the documented per-slug DELETE FROM web2_records WHERE entity_slug='fastsaleorder-invoice' inside the web2-all branch, or remove the misleading comment so no one adds web2_records to
- 🟡 `race` Backup table name uses minute-resolution timestamp (tsTag = YYYYMMDD*HHMM). Two concurrent wipes (or a retry within the same minute) collide on <t>\_bak*<ts>; the code does DROP TABLE IF EXISTS then CREATE TABLE AS, so a second concurrent call can DROP the first call's just-created backup before/afte — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:107-111,178-179; admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:40-44,89-90` → Add seconds+random suffix to tsTag, or wrap backup+truncate in a single transaction / advisory lock (pg_advisory_xact_lock) so only one reset runs at a time.
- 🟡 `race` data-reset wipe path runs backup and TRUNCATE outside a transaction (db.query on the pool, not a dedicated client/BEGIN). If the process dies or an error occurs after some TRUNCATEs but the response 500s, state is half-wiped with partial backups. The single TRUNCATE of all tables at line 200 is atom — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:167-202` → Acquire one client, BEGIN, do all CREATE TABLE AS backups + TRUNCATE, COMMIT, release — so backup+wipe are all-or-nothing (rename-to-nj already does this correctly).
- 🟡 `logic` by-phone reset validates digits.length < 9 → rejects, i.e. accepts 9-or-more digit numbers and slices last 10. A 9-digit input becomes '0'+last9, and variants include digits.slice(-10) which for an over-long fb_id-like numeric string could over-match unrelated rows. The project standard is exactly / — `admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:223-234` → Validate /^0\d{9}$/ on the normalized phone before building variants; reject anything that is not exactly a 10-digit VN phone.
- 🟡 `data-loss` web2-wallet-reset/by-phone and /backups do NOT guard against web2Db===chatDb (unlike the other endpoints). If WEB2_DATABASE_URL is unset, by-phone would DELETE from native_orders/fast_sale_orders/customer_wallets on the Web 1.0 chatDb pool — directly violating the Web1/Web2 isolation rule and deleti — `admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:218-221 (no db===chatDb check), 193-195` → Add the same `if (db === req.app.locals.chatDb) return 400` guard used by web2-wallet-reset and rematch-all to the by-phone and backups handlers.
- ⚪ `race` rematch-all keyset pagination is safe per-call, but it lazy-require's and calls sepayMatching.processWeb2Match for each row with a per-row try/catch that silently swallows ALL errors (comment 'skip lỗi từng row'). A systemic failure (e.g. matching service down) returns success with matched=0 and loo — `admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:167-178` → Collect and return error counts/samples instead of fully swallowing; at minimum console.error the first N row errors.
- ⚪ `race` Import routers fetch external pages (TPOS/Pancake) in a long loop then do a long upsert loop with no overall timeout or concurrency lock. Two concurrent invocations of the same import would double-process and race on ON CONFLICT upserts; also a partial failure mid-loop returns 500 with whatever was — `admin-web2-import-customers.js:131-170, admin-web2-import-pancake-customers.js:1` → Guard with an advisory lock so only one import runs at a time; upserts are idempotent so partial reruns are safe, but document that and return a resumable cursor.
- ⚪ `logic` web2-import-pancake-customers parses conversation timestamps via new Date(s + 'Z') (line 88) assuming Pancake values lack the trailing Z — correct per project rule — but only updated_at/inserted_at are normalized; if a value already has 'Z' or an offset, appending 'Z' yields an Invalid Date (NaN), t — `admin-web2-import-pancake-customers.js:86-91,113-117` → Guard: only append 'Z' when the string has no timezone designator (regex /[Z+-]\d\d:?\d\d$|Z$/); fall back to 0 on NaN (already done) — make the append conditional.
- ℹ️ `other` web2-wallet-reset reset-rematch issues UPDATE ... WHERE TRUE on the entire web2_balance_history table (full-table rewrite). Functionally intended, but on a large SePay log this is a heavy unindexed full update with no batching. — `admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:112-122` → Acceptable for an admin one-off; consider batching by id range if the table grows large.

**Security (audit):**

- 🔴 Admin secret accepted via query param (req.query.secret) on EVERY endpoint, and the global request logger at server.js:145-150 logs req.url for all non-health requests. Any call using ?secret=<CLEANUP_SECRET> writes the destructive-wipe admin secret into Render application logs in plaintext, where i — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:22, admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:25, admin-web2-import-cu` → Drop the ?secret= query fallback; require the x-admin-secret HEADER only. If query support is unavoidable, redact secret/token query params in the server.js logger before console.log.
- 🟠 by-phone reset logs raw customer phone numbers to console (PII in logs). — `admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:299-301` → Log only a masked phone (last 4 digits) and rowCounts, not the full variants array.
- 🟡 Timing-unsafe secret comparison (p === ADMIN_SECRET) allows theoretical timing side-channel on the admin secret. — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:23, admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:26, admin-web2-import-cu` → Use crypto.timingSafeEqual with equal-length buffers for the secret check.
- 🟡 No rate limiting on any destructive admin endpoint; combined with the secret-in-URL leak this makes brute force / replay easier. CORS allows x-admin-secret header from the fixed origin allowlist only, but server-to-server callers bypass CORS entirely. — `render.com/server.js:788-792 (no limiter middleware)` → Add an express-rate-limit to /api/admin and/or restrict these routes to an internal network/IP allowlist.
- ⚪ Table names are string-interpolated into SQL ("${t}", ${table}, ${SEPAY_LOG}). All values come from hardcoded constant allowlists so this is not currently injectable, but the pattern is fragile if a future caller-controlled value is added. — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:134,175,178-179,200; admin-web2-wallet-reset.js:86,89-9` → Keep table names from constant allowlists only; add an assertion that any table name matches /^[a-z0-9_]+$/ before interpolation as defense-in-depth.
- ⚪ web2_records correctly excluded from full TRUNCATE (data-reset.js:62-90 WEB2_ALL_TABLES has no web2_records; cleanup-dead deletes only per-slug orphans at line 384). This matches the multi-tenant requirement — noted as confirmed-good, not a bug. — `admin-web2-data-reset.js:62-90, 384` → No change. Optionally add a runtime guard rejecting 'web2_records' if it ever appears in a pickTables result.

**Ghi chú:** Positive: every destructive endpoint enforces the admin secret AND the web2Db===chatDb refusal (except by-phone & backups in wallet-reset — see bug). web2_records is correctly never full-truncated; only per-slug orphan deletes occur. Backups are taken before every wipe.; All SQL parameterizes user/data values ($1..) — only table NAMES are interpolated and those come from constant allowlists, so no injection on current call paths.; import-pancake & import-fb-links read chatDb (Web 1.0) but strictly read-only and only for JWT / fb-link backfill — a documented intentional one-time cross-layer migration, not a runtime dependency.; No GMT+7 display bugs here (server-side only); the only timestamp parsing (pancake import) correctly appends 'Z' per the project rule but unconditionally — see low bug.; Recommend moving the three import routers out of the always-mounted route table into an offline scripts/ entrypoint since they are explicitly one-time migrations and one of them (TPOS) violates the no-TPOS-in-Web2 rule.

### infra:firebase

Audit of every remaining Firebase/Firestore touchpoint inside Web 2.0 scope (web2/, web2/shared/, render.com web2-_ routes/services, realtime-sse-web2.js). Net result: Web 2.0 is effectively DRAINED of Firestore at runtime — there are NO live Firestore reads or writes, NO cost-incurring onSnapshot listeners, and NO live firebase-_-compat SDK script tags anywhere in the Web 2.0 surface. Realtime is 100% SSE (realtime-sse-web2.js, topics web2:\*), and data lives in Postgres web2Db. Every firebase/firestore string that grep finds in this scope is one of: (a) a historical migration comment, (b) dead unused constants, (c) a stale/misleading DB-badge marker, or (d) one-off manually-invoked migration/wipe CLI scripts (not boot/cron). The only residual items to clean are dead SDK constants, one misleading meta marker that renders a false 'Firebase 2.0' badge, and stale comments. The Pancake-token-at-boot read that older docs attribute to Firestore is now served from Postgres pancake_accounts ('Replaces Firestore pancake_tokens/accounts').

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** SERVICE realtime-sse-web2.js: pure in-process SSE hub (Map<topic,Set<Response>>) + web2WalletEvents EventEmitter listener (web2:wallet:update) wired in server.js — ZERO Firebase/Firestore at boot (grep firebase|firestore = 0 matches in 512 lines).; SERVICE web2-pancake-creds.js: AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt of Pancake identity/password using PANCAKE*CREDS_KEY env — ciphertext stored in Postgres, NO Firestore.; SERVICE web2-wallet-service.js / web2-wallet-isolation.js: 0 firebase/firestore references; SePay->wallet flow is Postgres + in-process EventEmitter only.; ROUTE web2-live-relay.js: 0 firebase references; proxies relay config to external web2-realtime Pancake-WS service; token source is Postgres pancake_accounts.; PAGE web2/customer-wallet/index.html: loads Web2DbBadge (shared) which on DOMContentLoaded reads <meta web2-db> and (incorrectly) renders 'DB Firebase 2.0' badge — see bugs. Page scripts are Web2 backend only (header line 1 + line 301 declare NO Firebase/Firestore).; SHARED web2-products-cache.js + web2-variants-cache.js: define FIRESTORE_COLLECTION/FIRESTORE_DOC constants but never use them; realtime now via web2-sse-bridge only (Firestore tickle removed 2026-05-29, \_ensureFirestore() removed 2026-06-13).; SHARED web2-sse-bridge.js: EventSource bridge that REPLACED Firestore onSnapshot tickle listeners — no firebase.; ONE-OFF scripts (NOT boot/cron): render.com/scripts/web2-firestore-wipe.js + migrations/045_migrate_firestore_to_pg.js + migrate-firebase-to-postgres.js use firebase-admin via FIREBASE*\* env vars; only run when manually invoked (node ...).

| Control                                                                                                                      | Handler                                                                                                                                            | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Web2DbBadge.mount() / \_resolveType() — web2/shared/web2-db-badge.js:89-97,122-134                                           | reads document.head meta[name=web2-db], maps content -> BADGE_CONFIG (render\|firebase\|both), appends <span class=w2-db-badge> to first <main h1> | Pure DOM render of an informational badge. With content='firebase' it shows '🔥 DB Firebase 2.0' + tooltip 'Trang dùng Firestore web2\_\* collection'. Label is purely cosmetic — does NOT trigger any Firestore connection. Bug is the WRONG label on customer-wallet (see bugs). |
| realtime-sse-web2.js notifyClients(topic,data) (exported, wired via initializeNotifiers across ~25 web2 routes in server.js) | in-process broadcast to subscribers Set for topic + optional forward to web2-api hub                                                               | SSE push only. No Firestore write. This is the replacement for the old Firestore tickle pattern (web2-products.js:17 comment confirms).                                                                                                                                            |
| web2-pancake-creds.encrypt/decrypt/isConfigured (exported)                                                                   | AES-256-GCM with key=sha256(PANCAKE_CREDS_KEY)                                                                                                     | Encrypts Pancake creds for Postgres storage; replaces Firestore-stored tokens. Returns null safely when key/plaintext missing.                                                                                                                                                     |
| web2-products-cache.js / web2-variants-cache.js subscribe()                                                                  | Web2SSE.subscribe('web2:products' \| 'web2:variants', cb)                                                                                          | Realtime via SSE bridge only; the FIRESTORE_COLLECTION/FIRESTORE_DOC constants are inert dead code never passed to any firebase call.                                                                                                                                              |

**Liên kết:** web2/shared/web2-db-badge.js (badge marker consumer), web2/shared/web2-sse-bridge.js (Web2SSE — replaced Firestore listeners), web2/shared/web2-products-cache.js, web2/shared/web2-variants-cache.js (dead FIRESTORE\__ constants), web2/shared/web2-suppliers-cache.js, web2/shared/web2-so-order-reader.js (now read Postgres /api/web2-_ not Firestore), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (SSE hub), render.com/routes/web2-so-order.js, web2-supplier-wallet.js, web2-msg-templates.js (Postgres ledgers that replaced Firestore docs), render.com/routes/pancake-accounts.js + pancake-page-tokens.js (Postgres source for Pancake tokens, replaced Firestore pancake_tokens), render.com/scripts/web2-firestore-wipe.js (one-off cleanup, firebase-admin via env)

**SSE:** realtime-sse-web2.js PUBLISHES web2:\* topics (web2:products, web2:variants, web2:supplier-wallet, web2:so-order, web2:wallet:update, etc.) via notifyClients — replaces all former Firestore onSnapshot tickles.; web2-products-cache.js SUBSCRIBES web2:products; web2-variants-cache.js SUBSCRIBES web2:variants; web2-suppliers-cache.js SUBSCRIBES web2:supplier-wallet — all via Web2SSE bridge, no Firestore reads consumed.; No Firestore listeners remain, so there are no Firestore read-cost listeners to clean up. SSE EventSource cleanup is the bridge's concern (out of this unit's Firebase scope).

**console.log:** 15 — web2/shared/web2-products-cache.js: 9 console._ (cache/SSE debug noise, no PII/tokens) — not Firebase-related leakage; web2/shared/web2-variants-cache.js: 3 console._ (debug noise, no PII); web2/shared/web2-sse-bridge.js: 3 console._ (debug noise); render.com/scripts/web2-firestore-wipe.js: console.error on missing FIREBASE\__ env — message names the env keys but does NOT print secret values (safe)

**Bugs (audit):**

- ⚪ `logic` Misleading/stale DB marker: customer-wallet page declares <meta name="web2-db" content="firebase" />, which makes Web2DbBadge render a '🔥 DB Firebase 2.0' badge with tooltip 'Trang dùng Firestore web2\_\* collection'. This directly contradicts the SAME file's header (line 1: 'WEB2.0 customer-wallet Ph — `web2/customer-wallet/index.html:9 (consumed by web2/shared/web2-db-badge.js:35-4` → Change line 9 to <meta name="web2-db" content="render" /> so the badge reads '🐘 DB Render 2.0' and matches reality. No JS change needed.
- ⚪ `other` Dead code: FIRESTORE_COLLECTION ('web2_products_sync'/'web2_variants_sync') and FIRESTORE_DOC ('notify') constants are still declared in the two shared cache modules but are referenced nowhere (grep count = 2 each, i.e. only the definitions). \_ensureFirestore() and Firestore tickle writes were alrea — `web2/shared/web2-products-cache.js:36-37; web2/shared/web2-variants-cache.js:29-` → Delete both constant pairs. Realtime is fully covered by Web2SSE.subscribe('web2:products'|'web2:variants'). Removing them prevents future readers from re-wiring a Firestore tickle.
- ⚪ `other` Stale server comment claims so-order and supplier-wallet are '100% client-side từ Firestore web2_so_order + web2_supplier_wallet (Web 2.0)' — false since the C8 (2026-06-13) and Đợt E (2026-06-12) migrations moved both to Postgres server ledgers (/api/web2-so-order, /api/web2-supplier-wallet). The v — `render.com/server.js:833` → Update or delete the comment to say data is served from Postgres web2Db via /api/web2-so-order and /api/web2-supplier-wallet.
- ℹ️ `other` Several leftover Firestore-mentioning comments across web2/ (e.g. purchase-refund-api.js:69-214, supplier-wallet-storage.js, web2-msg-template-core.js, web2-suppliers-cache.js) are accurate as migration history but increase the false impression that Web 2.0 still reads Firestore. None are live code. — `web2/purchase-refund/js/purchase-refund-api.js:69,81,86,124,204,214; web2/suppli` → Optional cleanup pass: trim historical Firestore comments to a single 'migrated to Postgres' breadcrumb to reduce confusion. No functional risk.

**Security (audit):**

- ℹ️ One-off Firestore wipe/migration scripts (web2-firestore-wipe.js, 045_migrate_firestore_to_pg.js, migrate-firebase-to-postgres.js) initialize firebase-admin with FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID/CLIENT_EMAIL/PRIVATE_KEY read from env — NO hardcoded service-account secrets. Private key escaped-newline handling is — `render.com/scripts/web2-firestore-wipe.js:8-20; render.com/migrations/045_migrat` → Keep as-is. Optionally move these dead-path scripts to an archive/ folder so future readers don't assume Web 2.0 still touches Firestore at runtime.
- ℹ️ No Firestore-backed data path exists in Web 2.0 runtime, so the usual Firestore-rules/exposure risks (client SDK reads with permissive rules) do NOT apply here — the attack surface was eliminated by the Postgres migration. x-web2-token / WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE gating now governs all web2 writes (handled — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js; web2/* (no firebase SDK loaded)` → No action for Firebase. (Auth-header enforcement is audited under the relevant write-route units.)

**Ghi chú:** CONCLUSION: Web 2.0 has NO active Firestore touchpoint at runtime. No live firebase-app/firestore/database/storage-compat <script> tags remain in any web2 HTML (kpi, printer-settings, delivery-zone, customers, balance-history, purchase-refund all carry 'gỡ Firebase SDK' removal comments). No window.firebase / firebase.firestore() / .onSnapshot() / firebase.initializeApp / firebase-config.js usage in web2/ (grep clean).; The CLAUDE.md statement that 'web2-realtime reads Firestore pancake_tokens/accounts at boot' appears OUTDATED for this codebase state: render.com/routes/pancake-accounts.js:5 says 'Replaces Firestore pancake_tokens/accounts' and pancake-page-tokens.js:5 'Replaces Firestore pancake_tokens/page_access_tokens' — token source is now Postgres. The actual web2-realtime Pancake-WS relay service lives outside the audited file set (server.js only proxies to it via web2-live-relay), so a residual Firestore fallback there cannot be confirmed/denied from this scope; flag for the realtime-service unit to verify.; No cost-incurring Firestore listeners exist in Web 2.0 (all onSnapshot replaced by SSE), so there is no Firestore read-cost exposure from this layer.; No Web 2.0 data is written to Firestore by any live code path in scope; writes go to Postgres web2Db via /api/web2\* routes.

### infra:sse

Two independent SSE hubs sharing near-identical code: realtime-sse-web2.js (Web 2.0, mounted /api/realtime/web2, topics web2:\*, routed by the worker to the web2-api Render service) and realtime-sse.js (Web 1.0, mounted /api/realtime, bare snake_case topics, n2store-fallback service). Each keeps its own Map<topic,Set<Response>> + WeakMap metadata, 30s heartbeat, per-key disconnect cleanup, and notifyClients/notifyClientsWildcard/broadcastToAll exported and wired in server.js. The web2 hub adds an admin ring buffer (500 entries) + live admin feed (topic web2:\_admin:sse-log, gated by ?admintoken), a cross-instance forward (fallback POSTs web2 notifies to web2-api/sse/relay-notify so SePay/ck-watcher events emitted on fallback reach the real subscribers on web2-api), and a web2WalletEvents listener that fans SePay deposits out to web2:wallet:<phone>, the web2:wallet wildcard, and web2:customer-wallet as PII-stripped tickles. Hub separation itself is clean: the web2 file imports only web2WalletEvents + web2-auth middleware, no chatDb/TPOS/web2Db coupling.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** realtime-sse-web2.js boot: requires express + ../middleware/web2-auth; initializes module-level sseClients Map, clientMetadata WeakMap, recentLogs array (max 500), \_logSeq, \_forwardTarget=null; realtime-sse-web2.js:462-500 boot: try/require ../services/web2-wallet-service and registers web2WalletEvents.on('web2:wallet:update', ...) listener; logs '[SSE-WEB2] Web 2.0 wallet event subscription initialized' or warns if service missing; server.js:606 require('./routes/realtime-sse-web2'); :738 app.use('/api/realtime/web2', web2RealtimeSseRoutes); :740 app.use('/api/realtime', realtimeSseRoutes); server.js:1104-1109 if process.env.WEB2_API_FORWARD_URL set → web2RealtimeSseRoutes.setForwardTarget({url, secret: CLEANUP_SECRET}) — only set on n2store-fallback, never web2-api (prevents forward loop); server.js:881-1156 ~40 web2 route modules call initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients); :2870 app.locals.web2RealtimeSseNotify = web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients; realtime-sse.js boot: requires ../services/wallet-event-processor and registers walletEvents.on('wallet:update', ...); logs '[SSE] Wallet event subscription initialized'; Per SSE GET /sse connection: validates keys, registers res into Set per key, starts 30s setInterval heartbeat, attaches req.on('close') cleanup + req.on('error')

| Control                                                                                           | Handler                                     | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| GET /api/realtime/web2/sse?keys=web2:a,web2:b                                                     | router.get('/sse') realtime-sse-web2.js:132 | Validates: keys non-empty (400), each <=500 chars (400), <=50 keys (400). Admin keys (web2:\_admin:\*) require valid ?admintoken resolving to role=admin else dropped (or 403 if all keys admin). Writes SSE headers + 'connected' event, stores metadata, adds res to sseClients Set per key, \_pushLog connect, starts 30s heartbeat. req.on('close') clears interval, deletes res from each key Set (deletes empty key), \_pushLog disconnect. |
| notifyClients(key,data,eventType='update')                                                        | realtime-sse-web2.js:271                    | If \_forwardTarget set → \_forwardNotify cross-instance POST first (skips admin topic). Then local broadcast to sseClients.get(key); writes 'event: <eventType>' + JSON {key,data,timestamp,event}. \_pushLog notify (clientsNotified count, action, code). Returns successCount. Logs '[SSE-WEB2] No clients listening' when 0.                                                                                                                  |
| notifyClientsWildcard(keyPrefix,data,eventType)                                                   | realtime-sse-web2.js:325                    | Iterates ALL sseClients keys; matches key===prefix OR key.startsWith(prefix+':'). Sets payload.key = the actual subscribed key (so web2:wallet:\* subscriber matches). Used by wallet listener for 'web2:wallet'. No admin-topic exclusion (see bug).                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| broadcastToAll(data,eventType='broadcast')                                                        | realtime-sse-web2.js:362                    | Writes to EVERY client across all topics incl. admin subscribers. No payload.key set → bridge clients (exact-match on payload.key) silently ignore. No admin exclusion.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| web2WalletEvents.on('web2:wallet:update')                                                         | realtime-sse-web2.js:465                    | On SePay/manual deposit commit: builds tickle {action:'update',phone,ts}; notifyClients('web2:wallet:'+phone), notifyClientsWildcard('web2:wallet'), and notifyClients('web2:customer-wallet',{action,txType,ts}). PII (balance/transaction) correctly stripped — client re-fetches.                                                                                                                                                              |
| GET /sse/stats                                                                                    | realtime-sse-web2.js:384 requireWeb2Admin   | Returns {totalClients, uniqueKeys, keyStats}. Admin-gated.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| POST /sse/test                                                                                    | realtime-sse-web2.js:394 requireWeb2Admin   | notifyClients(key, data\|\|{test}, 'test'). Admin-gated. (Web 1.0 equivalent at realtime-sse.js:320 is NOT gated.)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| POST /sse/relay-notify                                                                            | realtime-sse-web2.js:407                    | Server-to-server. Gated by x-relay-secret === CLEANUP_SECRET; fail-closed 503 if env unset, 401 if mismatch. Calls notifyClients(key,data,event). This is the cross-instance forward landing endpoint on web2-api.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| GET /sse/log?since&limit                                                                          | realtime-sse-web2.js:441 requireWeb2Admin   | Returns recentLogs slice (seq>since, capped 500). Admin-gated. Bootstraps admin monitor before subscribing to live web2:\_admin:sse-log.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| setForwardTarget(target)                                                                          | realtime-sse-web2.js:49                     | Sets module-level \_forwardTarget {url,secret}; called from server.js only when WEB2_API_FORWARD_URL present.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| GET /api/realtime/sse (Web 1.0)                                                                   | realtime-sse.js:66                          | Same shape minus: NO key-count cap, NO admin-key gating, NO ring buffer, NO forward. Per-key cleanup on close.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| POST /sse/fast-sale-orders, POST /celebration, POST /celebration-config, POST /sse/test (Web 1.0) | realtime-sse.js:343/367/410/320             | All broadcast to clients with NO auth gate despite /celebration comment claiming 'Requires admin-authenticated userType'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

**Liên kết:** web2/shared/web2-sse-bridge.js (Web2SSE.subscribe singleton; exact-match payload.key), cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/proxy-handler.js renderOriginFor → /api/realtime/web2/_ to web2-api-kv04.onrender.com; Web1 /api/realtime/_ to n2store-fallback, render.com/services/web2-wallet-service.js (emits web2:wallet:update post-COMMIT via emitAfterCommit), render.com/services/wallet-event-processor.js (Web 1.0 walletEvents), render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js (requireWeb2Admin, resolveWeb2User), render.com/routes/v2/web2-balance-history.js (manual-deposit notify), render.com/server.js (mounts both hubs, wires ~40 notifiers, sets forward target), web2/customer-wallet/js/web2-customer-wallet-app.js (subscribes web2:wallet:\*, web2:fast-sale-orders, web2:native-orders)

**SSE:** Web2 hub published topics: web2:<entity> (products, variants, native-orders, fast-sale-orders, cart, notifications, wallet:<phone>, customer-wallet, balance-history, jt-tracking, etc.), web2:_admin:sse-log (admin live feed); Web2 wallet fan-out: web2:wallet:<phone>, web2:wallet (wildcard), web2:customer-wallet — all PII-stripped tickles; Web1 hub topics (bare snake_case): celebration, celebration_config, fast_sale_orders, wallet:<phone>, kpi_\*, held_products, tpos_token — strictly separate namespace; Cleanup: web2 + web1 both clear heartbeat interval and remove res from each key Set on req.on('close'); empty Sets deleted. Client bridge unsubscribe removes callback, reopens EventSource only when topic set actually changes (C16 guard), debounce 50ms; Bridge resync: on reconnect (not first connect, not topic-change) dispatches synthetic 'resync' so pages re-fetch the gap window; No server-side debounce; debounce is client-side per consuming page (typically 500-1000ms)

**console.log:** 39 — realtime-sse-web2.js:217 logs full key list per connect (topic names only, low risk); realtime-sse-web2.js:173 logs admintoken resolve error message (no token value, OK); realtime-sse-web2.js:177 logs dropped admin keys (topic names only); realtime-sse.js:474 '[SSE-WALLET] Notified N clients for wallet:<phone>' — logs raw customer phone (PII) to server logs (Web 1.0 hub); Both hubs: high-frequency connect/disconnect/notify console.log per event — log noise at scale, not PII beyond the wallet phone above

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `data-loss` Dead-topic notify: web2-balance-history manual-deposit emits notify('web2:wallet:update', {phone,action,amount}). NO client subscribes the literal topic 'web2:wallet:update' — the customer-wallet page subscribes 'web2:wallet:\*' (matched server-side via wildcard registration) and per-phone 'web2:wall — `render.com/routes/v2/web2-balance-history.js:1112-1117` → Delete the notify('web2:wallet:update', ...) block; the per-phone notify already drives the wildcard subscriber. If a list-level signal is desired, call notifyClientsWildcard('web2:wallet', {action,ts
- 🟡 `memory-leak` No max-connections cap on either hub. sseClients grows unbounded with the number of live tabs/clients; a client can also register up to 50 keys (web2) or unlimited keys (Web1 — no count cap at all). Slow/abusive clients accumulate Response objects. Web1 has neither key-length-count cap nor connectio — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse.js:66 (no key-count cap), realtime-sse-web2.js:15` → Add a global maxConnections guard (return 503 when getConnectionStats().totalClients exceeds a threshold) and port the >50-keys cap to the Web1 hub.
- 🟡 `memory-leak` Heartbeat write-after-close race / interval leak window. The 30s heartbeat writes to res inside a try/catch that clears the interval only on throw. If the socket is half-closed, res.write may NOT throw immediately (buffers), so a dead client's interval can persist until the next failed write; meanwh — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:226-232, realtime-sse.js:129-136` → Also listen on res.on('close')/res.on('finish') (not just req.on('close')) to clear the heartbeat and remove from Sets; consider a periodic sweep that drops clients whose res.writableEnded is true.
- 🟡 `race` Cross-instance forward fan-out duplicate-delivery risk. On n2store-fallback, notifyClients calls \_forwardNotify (POST to web2-api) AND broadcasts locally. If WEB2_API_FORWARD_URL were ever (mis)configured on web2-api itself, web2-api would forward back to fallback creating a loop; protection relies — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:274 + server.js:1104` → Add a self-guard: skip forwarding if \_forwardTarget.url resolves to this instance; mark relay-notify-originated broadcasts so they never re-forward (e.g. a local notifyNoForward path).
- ⚪ `logic` notifyClientsWildcard and broadcastToAll have NO exclusion for the admin topic web2:\_admin:sse-log. A broadcastToAll() or a wildcard call with a prefix that matches 'web2:\_admin' would push payloads onto the admin monitor stream, and broadcastToAll reaches admin subscribers too. Currently no caller — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:325-378` → In notifyClientsWildcard/broadcastToAll skip keys === ADMIN_LOG_TOPIC (mirror the \_forwardNotify and \_pushLog guards).
- ⚪ `logic` \_pushLog recursion guard only checks log.topic === ADMIN_LOG_TOPIC, but connect/disconnect entries (lines 219/251) set no .topic. Harmless today because those entries are not admin-topic notifies, but the guard is effectively only exercised when notifyClients is called with the admin topic key — fra — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:99-101` → Make the guard explicit: skip broadcast for any entry whose type is connect/disconnect OR whose topic is the admin topic; or always tag admin-related entries with .topic.
- ⚪ `logic` Web1 notifyClientsWildcard uses loose '/' separator semantics (key.startsWith(keyPrefix) || keyPrefix.startsWith(key+'/')). The bare key.startsWith(keyPrefix) is an unbounded prefix match — e.g. wildcard 'wallet' would also match a hypothetical 'wallet_settings' key (no boundary), over-notifying. We — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse.js:241` → Tighten Web1 to boundary-aware matching (key===prefix || key.startsWith(prefix+'/')); low impact given current Web1 key set.
- ℹ️ `other` No GMT+7 timezone bug found in scope. Both hubs use Date.now() epochs and new Date().toISOString() (UTC) for machine fields only; display formatting happens client-side. connectionId and timestamps are timezone-agnostic. — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:200` → No change.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟡 Web 1.0 hub state-changing/broadcast endpoints are completely unauthenticated. POST /sse/test, POST /sse/fast-sale-orders, POST /celebration, POST /celebration-config have NO auth middleware — the /celebration JSDoc even claims 'Requires admin-authenticated userType' but no such check exists. Any un — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse.js:320, 343, 367, 410` → Add an auth gate (admin token / x-relay-secret) to all four POST endpoints, matching the web2 hub which gates /sse/test and /sse/stats with requireWeb2Admin.
- 🟡 PII + financial data broadcast in SSE payload to a topic clients subscribe to. web2:wallet:<phone> manual-deposit notify and web2:wallet:update notify send {phone, action, amount}. While web2:wallet:\* subscription requires no auth, the wallet listener (realtime-sse-web2.js:472/491) was deliberately — `render.com/routes/v2/web2-balance-history.js:1107-1117` → Strip to {action,ts} (or {action,phone,ts}) like the SePay path; have clients re-fetch the balance. Remove the amount field from the SSE payload.
- ⚪ admintoken passed as query param appears in access logs (acknowledged tradeoff at line 154-157). resolveWeb2User is called with the token on every connect to an admin topic. — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:162-171` → Accept short-lived/one-time admin SSE tokens or rotate; ensure proxy/CDN access logs scrub ?admintoken. Acceptable as-is for an internal admin monitor but document the log-scrub requirement.
- ⚪ Customer phone (PII) written to server logs on every Web 1.0 wallet notify. — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse.js:474` → Log a masked phone or just the count.
- ℹ️ CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin: _ on the SSE endpoints. SSE carries no cookies/credentials here and topics are opaque, but combined with the unauthenticated Web1 broadcast endpoints it widens the reach of those issues. — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:190, realtime-sse.js:93` → Restrict origin to the known app origins, or at minimum keep the broadcast POST endpoints authenticated so _ origin only exposes read streams.
- ℹ️ relay-notify is correctly fail-closed (503 when CLEANUP_SECRET unset, 401 on mismatch). Good. No secret hardcoded; reads from env. — `render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js:407-419` → No change; noted as correct.

**Ghi chú:** Hub separation verified clean: realtime-sse-web2.js imports only express, web2-auth middleware, and web2WalletEvents — NO chatDb/web2Db pool, NO TPOS coupling. Web2 publishes only web2:_ topics; Web1 publishes only bare snake_case. No cross-hub notify found.; Worker routes /api/realtime/web2/_ → web2-api-kv04 origin and /api/realtime/\* → n2store-fallback (proxy-handler.js:41,57). This is why the cross-instance forward exists: SePay/ck-watcher web2 events emitted on fallback have 0 local subscribers and must be POSTed to web2-api/sse/relay-notify. url.search (incl. admintoken) is preserved through the proxy.; Wallet PII discipline is correct in the SSE listener (realtime-sse-web2.js:472/491 send tickles only). The single regression is the balance-history manual-deposit notify (bug #1) which leaks amount to a dead topic.; Web2 input validation is solid: empty-keys 400, key>500 chars 400, >50 keys 400, admin-topic gating. Web1 hub lacks the key-count cap and admin gating — port these over.; relay-notify is fail-closed and secret-gated correctly (no hardcoded secret; CLEANUP_SECRET from env).

### infra:worker

Cloudflare Worker `chatomni-proxy` is the single edge entrypoint shared by Web 1.0 and Web 2.0. worker.js routes every request through matchRoute() (routes.js) into a big switch, then a handler proxies to one of: tomato.tpos.vn (TPOS, Web 1.0 only), pancake.vn / pages.fm, graph.facebook.com, api.deepseek.com, my.sepay.vn, n2store-fallback.onrender.com (Web 1.0 Render), or web2-api-kv04.onrender.com (Web 2.0 Render). Web 2.0 origin selection is a SECOND independent classifier: proxy-handler.js `isWeb2Path()`/`renderOriginFor()`. All /api/web2\* and /api/realtime/web2 paths resolve to web2-api; the /api/web2- dash-guard (routes.js:391) and isWeb2Path `/api/web2` prefix (proxy-handler.js:29) both prevent fallthrough to the TPOS catch-all. CORS is origin-aware (credentialed allowlist), token cache is in-memory per-username, and the worker forwards x-web2-token / x-admin-secret transparently via new Headers(request.headers).

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** Worker boot: ES module default export { fetch }. No cron/interval. Module-scope mutable singletons created once per isolate: token-cache.js:13 tokenCacheMap (Map username->token), header-learner.js:12 dynamicHeaders (tposappversion), pancake Cache API (caches.default) for conversations.; Per-request: worker.js:73 fetch() -> routeRequest(): OPTIONS short-circuit to corsPreflightResponse(request) (worker.js:87); WebSocket upgrade check for /ws/pancake (worker.js:97); matchRoute(pathname) (worker.js:105); switch dispatch (worker.js:108-313); addCorsHeaders(response, request) wraps all non-WS responses (worker.js:81).; No frontend scripts/CSS — this is a service. Render origin constants loaded at module scope: proxy-handler.js:19 FALLBACK_ORIGIN, :20 WEB2_API_ORIGIN.

| Control                                                        | Handler                                                                   | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| -------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| matchRoute(pathname) (routes.js:202)                           | routes.js:202-397                                                         | Pure classifier: exact matches first, then prefix tests in priority order. Critical web2 ordering: /api/web2/products\|variants (246-256) -> WEB2_PRODUCTS/VARIANTS; /api/web2-returns (257) -> WEB2_RETURNS; /api/web2/ (259) -> WEB2_GENERIC; then later /api/web2- dash catch (391) -> WEB2_GENERIC; finally /api/ (394) -> TPOS_GENERIC. Returns a route key or null.           |
| WEB2\_\* routes (worker.js:194-233)                            | handleCustomer360Proxy (proxy-handler.js:552)                             | Strips /api/ prefix, builds targetUrl = renderOriginFor(pathname)+/api/+apiPath. renderOriginFor->isWeb2Path returns true for /api/web2\* -> WEB2_API_ORIGIN (web2-api-kv04). Forwards method+ALL headers (new Headers(request.headers)) incl x-web2-token/x-admin-secret + body, fetchWithRetry(3,1000,15000), proxyResponseWithCors. GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE all retried (see bug). |
| REALTIME /api/realtime/\* (worker.js:250)                      | handleRealtimeProxy->handleRenderFallbackProxy (proxy-handler.js:323,419) | targetUrl=renderOriginFor(pathname)+pathname+search. /api/realtime/web2* matched by isWeb2Path:41 -> web2-api SSE hub; bare /api/realtime/* -> fallback (Web1 hub). SSE (Accept text/event-stream OR path endsWith /sse) uses plain fetch (no retry/timeout) to keep stream open; else fetchWithRetry. Strips host/cf-connecting-ip/cf-ray.                                         |
| WEB2_VIENEU_REGISTRY/JT/FB_POSTS/ZALO/etc dash routes          | handleCustomer360Proxy                                                    | All dash-prefixed web2 routes (web2-supplier-wallet, web2-so-order, web2-live-comments, web2-zalo, web2-jt-tracking, web2-fb-posts, web2-vieneu-registry, livestream, livestream-images) classified explicitly then proxied to web2-api. Any UNlisted /api/web2-X also caught by routes.js:391 dash-guard.                                                                          |
| TPOS_GENERIC catch-all /api/\* (worker.js:304)                 | handleTposGeneric (tpos-handler.js:306)                                   | targetUrl=tomato.tpos.vn/+apiPath. Forwards new Headers(request.headers) + overrides Origin/Referer to tomato. POST/PUT/PATCH = fetchWithTimeout 60s NO retry (avoids dup writes); GET/DELETE = fetchWithRetry. WEB 1.0 ONLY. No web2 path can reach here given the two guards.                                                                                                     |
| TOKEN POST /api/token (worker.js:110)                          | handleTokenRequest (token-handler.js:58)                                  | JSON {companyId} -> server-side TPOS creds injected (CREDENTIALS_BY_COMPANY) or legacy form passthrough. Cache per-username (token-cache.js). On miss POST to TPOS token endpoint with clean headers. Returns access_token. Web 1.0 only.                                                                                                                                           |
| PANCAKE_OFFICIAL_V2 conversations GET (pancake-handler.js:139) | handlePancakeOfficialV2                                                   | Edge-caches pages/{id}/conversations GET in caches.default for 60s, key includes page_access_token in URL.
