Peeks body for error_code before caching. cache.put async non-blocking.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| SEPAY_DASHBOARD POST /api/sepay-dashboard (worker.js:164)      | handleSepayDashboard (sepay-dashboard-handler.js:254)                     | Logs into my.sepay.vn with email/password from body (or GET query!), scrapes dashboard/invoices/plans HTML via regex, returns parsed plan/quota/expiry. Web1 admin tooling.                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| addCorsHeaders(response,request) (worker.js:81)                | cors-headers.js:155 + buildCorsHeaders:33                                 | Echoes specific Origin + Allow-Credentials:true when origin matches CREDENTIALED_ORIGIN_PATTERNS (nhijudy.store, github.io, _.pages.dev, localhost, 127.0.0.1), else Allow-Origin:_. Allow-Headers whitelist includes X-Web2-Token.                                                                                                                                                 |

**Liên kết:** render.com web2-api service (https://web2-api-kv04.onrender.com) — all /api/web2*, /api/native-orders, /api/fast-sale-orders, /api/reconcile, /api/wallet-deposits, /api/purchase-refund, /api/livestream*, /api/realtime/web2*, render.com n2store-fallback service (https://n2store-fallback.onrender.com) — Web 1.0 /api/sepay, /api/customers, /api/chat, /api/realtime (bare), /api/v2/* core, image resize, render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js — Web 2.0 SSE hub proxied via REALTIME route, tomato.tpos.vn — TPOS_GENERIC, TPOS_REST, token, exports, order-lines (Web 1.0), pancake.vn / pages.fm / content.pancake.vn / graph.facebook.com — pancake + facebook + avatar handlers, shared/universal/cors-headers.js, shared/universal/fetch-utils.js — shared with all services, web2/shared/web2-auth.js WEB2_CONFIG — frontend source of WORKER_URL pointing at this worker

**SSE:** Worker does not subscribe/publish SSE itself — it transparently PROXIES SSE. /api/realtime/web2/sse (Web2 hub topics web2:<entity>) and /api/sepay-home/_/stream and /facebook/comments/stream and any _/sse path use plain fetch() with no timeout so the long-lived stream isn't aborted (proxy-handler.js:440-446, 216-219).; No debounce here (that lives in page clients). SSE detection is Accept: text/event-stream OR pathname.endsWith('/sse') (proxy-handler.js:423) and endsWith('/stream') for sepay-home (proxy-handler.js:214). Note: a web2 SSE endpoint NOT ending in /sse and whose client omits Accept:text/event-stream would be retried/timed-out as a normal request (see bugs).

**console.log:** 180 — sepay-dashboard-handler.js:279 console.log('[SEPAY-DASHBOARD] Starting login for:', email) — logs the SePay account email (PII/credential identifier) to Worker logs.; sepay-dashboard-handler.js:322 console.log('[SEPAY-DASHBOARD] Done. Dashboard:', JSON.stringify(data.dashboard)) — logs scraped billing/plan data.; proxy-handler.js:152 / :208 console.log('[SEPAY-PROXY] Authorization header forwarded') — confirms presence of auth header (no value, low risk).; token-handler.js:100/105/142, token-cache.js:59 — log cacheKey which equals TPOS username (not the token value); acceptable but is an account identifier.; proxy-handler.js:77/113/138/196/268 etc — verbose [PROXY]/[SEPAY-PROXY] target-URL logging; query strings may contain access_token/jwt (e.g. pancake access_token in /api/pancake/\* search, fb-avatar token param) -> tokens can land in logs via targetUrl logging (pancake-handler.js:35,240; image-proxy fb-avatar logs full pancakeAvatarUrl incl ?access_token at :117).

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `tpos-coupling` DUAL-CLASSIFIER DRIFT (latent TPOS-leak / wrong-origin risk). Routing correctness depends on TWO independent classifiers agreeing: matchRoute() (routes.js) decides the HANDLER, and isWeb2Path()/renderOriginFor() (proxy-handler.js) decides the ORIGIN. They are maintained by hand and already diverge i — `cloudflare-worker/modules/config/routes.js:259,391 vs cloudflare-worker/modules/` → Make renderOriginFor the single origin authority and assert in handleCustomer360Proxy that isWeb2Path(pathname) matches the route's expected layer; add a unit test that every WEB2\_\* route in routes.js
- 🟡 `bug` Non-idempotent web2 mutations are RETRIED. handleCustomer360Proxy (used by ALL /api/web2\* writes incl native-orders/fast-sale-orders/purchase-refund/wallet-deposits) always uses fetchWithRetry(3,...) regardless of method. On a transient 5xx/429 or timeout during a POST/PATCH/DELETE the Worker re-sen — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/proxy-handler.js:565-578 (and handleRenderFal` → Branch on method like handleTposGeneric: POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE -> fetchWithTimeout (no retry) or only retry when an idempotency key (X-Idempotency-Key already whitelisted) is present and the upstream
- 🟡 `race` Body re-read on retry is fine (arrayBuffer captured once), but fetchWithRetry caps total time poorly for SSE-ish web2 endpoints that are NOT detected as SSE. handleRenderFallbackProxy only treats Accept:text/event-stream OR path endsWith /sse as streaming. A web2 long-poll/stream endpoint whose clie — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/proxy-handler.js:422-459` → Also treat known streaming web2 paths (e.g. /api/realtime/web2/sse already matches; but verify /api/events, /api/tpos-events) and add a query flag ?stream=1 fallback. Confirm no web2 stream endpoint r
- 🟡 `bug` Stale token-cache across credential changes / per-isolate inconsistency. token-cache.js tokenCacheMap is per-Worker-isolate in-memory. Multiple isolates hold independent caches; a token revoked upstream is still served from a warm isolate for up to expires_in - 5min. Also extractUsernameFromBody fal — `cloudflare-worker/modules/utils/token-cache.js:13,20-31; token-handler.js:23-39,` → For legacy passthrough without an explicit username, do NOT cache under '\_default' (skip cache or derive key from a hash of the full body). Accept the cross-isolate staleness or move token cache to KV
- ⚪ `race` Pancake conversations edge-cache key omits the Authorization/JWT identity except via page_access_token query param. If two users hit the same pages/{id}/conversations with token in a HEADER (not query) the cache key is identical and user A could be served user B's cached conversation list. Code comm — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/pancake-handler.js:148-160` → Only enable the 60s cache when access_token/page_access_token is present in the query string; otherwise bypass cache (private data).
- ⚪ `perf` proxyResponseWithCors reads response.\_retryCount but fetchWithRetry sets it on the Response object; for SSE responses returned via plain fetch \_retryCount is undefined (falsy) — fine. However addCorsHeaders runs a SECOND time in worker.js:81 over an already-CORS'd proxy response, re-setting Allow-Or — `worker.js:81 + proxy-handler.js (every proxyResponseWithCors call) + cors-header` → Pass request into proxyResponseWithCors once (it accepts an optional request arg) and drop the outer addCorsHeaders, or vice-versa, to have a single CORS application point.
- ⚪ `logic` header-learner dynamicHeaders.tposappversion is module-global mutable shared across all requests in an isolate with a 1h cooldown; concurrent requests can race learnFromResponse but writes are last-wins on a string (benign). The risk is a one-time wrong version learned from a malformed-but-format-va — `cloudflare-worker/modules/utils/header-learner.js:49-72` → Acceptable; optionally validate against known-good prefix.
- ℹ️ `web1-coupling` /api/v2/_ split between Web1 core and Web2 piggy-back is encoded as a regex in isWeb2Path:43-48 (notifications|audit-log|supplier-aging|dashboard-kpi|smart-match|inventory-forecast|supplier-360|cart|kpi|web2-). This list must be kept in sync with render server mounts; a new Web2 /api/v2 feature not — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/proxy-handler.js:43-48` → Prefer the /api/v2/web2-_ prefix convention exclusively for new Web2 v2 routes (already covered by the web2- alternative) and avoid bare-name piggy-backs.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 Plaintext TPOS credentials (usernames + passwords) hardcoded in source: CREDENTIALS_BY_COMPANY with password 'Aa@28612345678' for nvktlive1/nvktshop1. Committed to git, readable by anyone with repo access. (Web 1.0 scope but lives in this unit.) — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/token-handler.js:42-45` → Move to Worker secret bindings (env via wrangler secret) and read from env in handleTokenRequest; rotate the exposed password since it is in git history.
- ⚪ Hardcoded imgbb API key in source. Free-tier anonymous key but still a credential in git. — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/image-proxy-handler.js:257` → Move to env binding; rotate.
- 🟡 SePay dashboard login accepts email+password via GET query params (url.searchParams), which land in CF/Render access logs and browser history. Also logs the email. — `cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/sepay-dashboard-handler.js:266-271, :279` → Reject GET (POST-only), and remove the email from the log line.
- 🟡 Tokens leak into logs via target-URL logging: pancake access_token (query param) and fb-avatar ?access_token are printed in full ([PANCAKE-DIRECT]/[PANCAKE] target URL, [FB-AVATAR] full pancakeAvatarUrl). [PROXY] also logs arbitrary targetUrl from user-supplied ?url=. — `pancake-handler.js:35,240; image-proxy-handler.js:117; proxy-handler.js:77` → Redact token/jwt/access_token query params before logging (strip or mask).
- 🟠 Open proxy / SSRF: /api/proxy?url=<any> fetches ANY URL server-side with no allowlist; /api/image-proxy?url=<any> likewise. An attacker can use the Worker to fetch arbitrary internal/external endpoints and reflect responses (with CORS \*). — `proxy-handler.js:68-124; image-proxy-handler.js:24-91` → Restrict ?url= to an allowlist of hostnames (tpos/pancake/fb/render), or require auth. At minimum block private IP ranges and non-http(s).
- ⚪ x-web2-token / x-admin-secret are forwarded correctly (new Headers(request.headers) preserves them), but the Worker performs NO auth itself — it trusts the upstream Render service to enforce WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE. This is acceptable but means a destructive web2 admin route depends entirely on web2-api v — `proxy-handler.js:428,569 (header passthrough)` → Keep; ensure web2-api enforces x-admin-secret on /api/admin/web2-\* (out of scope here).
- ℹ️ CORS Allow-Credentials echoes any \*.pages.dev origin (regex /^https:\/\/[a-z0-9-]+\.pages\.dev$/). Any Cloudflare Pages site can make credentialed requests. — `shared/universal/cors-headers.js:18` → Narrow to the project's specific pages.dev subdomain if credentialed flows matter.

**Ghi chú:** FOCUS VERDICT: No web2 path currently leaks to the TPOS catch-all. Two guards protect it: routes.js:391 (/api/web2- dash -> WEB2_GENERIC) added in commit 83b5d75c0, and isWeb2Path:29 (/api/web2 prefix -> web2-api origin). /api/realtime/web2 -> web2-api via isWeb2Path:41. TPOS_GENERIC (worker.js:304, tpos-handler.js) is reached only by /api/\* that is neither web2 nor any explicit Render route.; x-web2-token and x-admin-secret ARE forwarded (handlers use new Headers(request.headers); handleRenderFallbackProxy only deletes host/cf-connecting-ip/cf-ray). So WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE writes are not silently dropped by the worker.; The worker performs zero DB access and holds no pool — pool mixups (web2Db vs chatDb) are out of scope here; the only Web1/Web2 boundary is origin selection (isWeb2Path).; No GMT+7 timezone logic in the worker except sepay-dashboard-handler.js:315/319-320 using new Date().toISOString()/getMonth() (Web1 billing display) — runs in CF (UTC) not Render; month label could be off near midnight UTC vs +7, minor and Web1-only.; Recommend adding an automated test asserting matchRoute(route)+renderOriginFor agree for the full ROUTES table to prevent the dual-classifier drift (high bug).

## Routes

### routes:comms (Web 2.0 — comment ingest + bulk message-send queue + unread/intents/templates/quick-replies)

Seven Web 2.0 Express routers (all on pool web2Db||chatDb, all SSE on the web2: hub) covering the livestream-comment store + realtime ingest (web2-live-comments.js), the refresh-safe bulk FB message-send job/items queue (web2-msg-send.js, consumed by services/web2-msg-send-worker.js), the WS-relay config proxy (web2-live-relay.js), and CRUD for message templates / quick replies plus read-only unread-counter and customer-intent flag lists. The hot path is POST /ingest: the live-chat WS relay pushes Pancake conversation events (gated by x-relay-secret===CLEANUP_SECRET), which are mapped to comment rows, upserted via ON CONFLICT, broadcast on web2:live-comments, and (for truncated "…" snippets) reconciled to full text via the poller — which correctly requires the customer UUID (conv.customers[0].id) for the Pancake messages API. The message-send queue creates a job+items transactionally; a background worker claims pending items FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and sends through the CF worker, marking 24h-window failures needs_extension for the browser extension to drain. The unit is the comms backbone for native-orders bulk-send and the live-chat comment column.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** BOOT (server.js): web2-msg-send.ensureSchema(web2Db) creates web2_msg_send_jobs/\_items + 5 indexes (line 850); web2-unread tracker.ensureSchema (line 869); all 7 routers mounted and initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients) wired (web2-live-comments 1014, msg-templates 897, quick-replies 881, customer-intents 1048, msg-send 1087); BOOT: services/web2-msg-send-worker.start() — setInterval tick every WEB2_MSG_WORKER_INTERVAL_MS (4000ms, unref'd), setImmediate first tick; disabled via WEB2_MSG_WORKER_DISABLED=1; uses web2Pool for jobs + chatPool for pancake_accounts/PATs (cross-pool, documented); BOOT: services/web2-livestream-poller.start() — background poll DISABLED (event-driven only via WS relay → /ingest → pollPostNow); only inits deps + runs reconcileRecentTruncated once ~25s after boot; LAZY (first request per table): web2-live-comments.ensureTables runs CREATE TABLE web2_live_comments/web2_migrations/web2_live_saved + two one-time TZ migrations (w2lc_tz_fix_20260611, w2lc_tz_fix2_20260611) guarded by web2_migrations marker; ensureCampaignTables/ensurePollerTable on first relevant route; LAZY: web2-msg-templates ensureTables (WeakSet-gated per pool) + \_seedIfEmpty (4 default templates); web2-quick-replies ensureTables (WeakSet-gated) + one-time READ-ONLY seed copying rows from Web 1.0 chatDb.quick_replies into web2_quick_replies when empty

| Control                                                                                                         | Handler                                             | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| POST /api/web2-live-comments/ingest (relay WS push)                                                             | router.post('/ingest') web2-live-comments.js:320    | Gated x-relay-secret===CLEANUP*SECRET (empty secret in dev → warn+pass). Filters suppressed convs (boost-marked TTL or page-authored), maps each conv→comment with id=`${conv.id}*${message_count\|\|updated_at}`, upsertComments (ON CONFLICT id DO UPDATE), \_notify('realtime',postId) per distinct postId. For snippets ending in …/... fires poller.reconcileFullText(pageId,postId,convId,rowId,custUuid) fire-and-forget passing conv.customers[0].id as the required UUID. |
| POST /api/web2-live-comments/boost-mark                                                                         | router.post('/boost-mark',requireWeb2AuthSoft) :406 | Marks convIds in-memory _boostMarks (TTL 20min default) so future ingests skip them, AND DELETEs already-ingested rows via id=$1 OR starts_with(id,$1\|\|'_'); \_notify('reconcile') if purged. In-memory map is per-process (lost on restart, not shared across instances).                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| POST /api/web2-live-comments/poll-now                                                                           | router.post('/poll-now',requireWeb2AuthSoft) :444   | Validates posts, caps to 10, calls poller.pollPostNow(pageId,postId,{immediate:true}) in Promise.all; returns saved count. Each post fans out to up to 50 pages of conversations + N message fetches on pancake.vn.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| POST /api/web2-live-comments/bulk                                                                               | router.post('/bulk',requireWeb2AuthSoft) :478       | upsertComments(req.body.comments); deliberately does NOT \_notify (avoid reload→re-save loop). Returns saved count.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| GET /api/web2-live-comments/?postIds=&pageIds=&campaignId=&since=&sinceUpdated=&limit=                          | router.get('/') :495                                | Parameterized WHERE; delta cursor via updated_at>=sinceUpdated (correct); legacy since uses created_time>=since (lossy for late multi-post). ORDER BY created_time DESC LIMIT min(limit,5000).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| GET /stats, GET /posts, GET /page-posts, GET/POST/DELETE /campaigns, POST /campaigns/:id/assign, POST /unassign | web2-live-comments.js:538-767                       | Campaign CRUD + post-assign; page-posts calls poller.listLivePostsForAssign() and persists titles best-effort. Assign cross-types campaign_id (BIGINT id stringified to match web2_live_comments.campaign_id VARCHAR).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| POST/GET/DELETE /api/web2-live-comments/saved* + /poller-pages*                                                 | web2-live-comments.js:783-919                       | web2_live_saved CRUD (Lưu Live) and poller page config (enable/disable/delete) — each mutation \_notify('poller-pages'). All writes requireWeb2AuthSoft; reads open.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| POST /api/web2/msg-send/                                                                                        | router.post('/') web2-msg-send.js:154               | BEGIN; INSERT job(state running); loop INSERT items skipping any missing message/pageId/convId; if inserted==0 ROLLBACK 400; UPDATE total=inserted; COMMIT; \_notify web2:bulk-send created. NO requireWeb2AuthSoft on this write route — worker then actually sends FB messages to real customers.                                                                                                                                                                                |
| GET /api/web2/msg-send/active \| /:id \| /:id/extension-items                                                   | web2-msg-send.js:229,273,289                        | Reattach job after refresh; \_counts aggregates item states. extension-items returns needs_extension rows (limit≤50) for the browser extension to drain — payload includes customer_name + fb_user_id + message (PII over the wire), no auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| POST /api/web2/msg-send/:id/items/:itemId/claim-ext                                                             | web2-msg-send.js:309                                | Conditional UPDATE state=ext_inflight WHERE state='needs_extension' — atomic double-send guard (only first claimer wins). NO auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /api/web2/msg-send/:id/items/:itemId/result                                                                | web2-msg-send.js:327                                | UPDATE state=done\|error, attempts+1 WHERE id AND job_id — NO state guard, NO auth → a duplicate/retried result POST re-runs and can flip a done item, double-increment attempts; then \_recomputeAndNotify (full GROUP BY + job-state write + SSE).                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| POST /api/web2/msg-send/:id/cancel                                                                              | web2-msg-send.js:348                                | UPDATE items pending/needs_extension/ext_inflight → cancelled, then recompute. NO auth; does not cancel items already 'sending' (worker may still deliver them).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| GET/POST/DELETE /api/web2-msg-templates                                                                         | web2-msg-templates.js:115,132,181                   | Template CRUD, all requireWeb2AuthSoft; POST handles create/update via b.id; \_notify web2:msg-templates.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/web2-quick-replies                                                                     | web2-quick-replies.js:108,124,156,196               | Quick-reply CRUD on web2_quick_replies; writes requireWeb2AuthSoft, GET open; \_notify web2:quick-replies. POST sort_order via subquery MAX+1 (race window).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| GET /api/web2/unread, /stats, POST /reconcile                                                                   | web2-unread.js:28,58,75                             | Read-only unread list/stats from web2_unread_messages; POST /reconcile triggers services/web2-unread-reconcile.reconcileAll(web2Db,chatDb,notify) — reconcile (can delete rows) with NO auth gate.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| GET /api/web2/customer-intents, /stats, POST /:id/done                                                          | web2-customer-intents.js:41,77,90                   | List/stats flags; POST /:id/done sets status=done — NO auth gate; done_by from body.userName/by defaulting '(ẩn danh)'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| GET /api/web2-live-relay/pages, POST /connect                                                                   | web2-live-relay.js:45,55                            | Server-to-server proxy to web2-realtime service adding x-relay-secret; /connect requireWeb2AuthSoft sets enabled page set + reconnects per-page WS.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

**Liên kết:** services/web2-livestream-poller.js — reconcileFullText/pollPostNow/listLivePostsForAssign/reconcileRecentTruncated (customer_id UUID requirement lives here), services/web2-msg-send-worker.js — consumes web2_msg_send_items, sends via CF worker /api/pancake-official, mints PAT from chatDb pancake_accounts, services/web2-unread-tracker.js (ensureSchema) + services/web2-unread-reconcile.js (reconcileAll), middleware/web2-auth.js — requireWeb2AuthSoft / WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE / token hashing, routes/realtime-sse-web2.js — notifyClients hub for all web2: topics, routes/native-orders.js — snapshotKpiBase called by worker on successful 'Chốt đơn' send; native-orders UI is the bulk-send producer, web2/live-chat + comments-mobile pages — consumers of web2:live-comments + GET /, web2-realtime service (WEB2_REALTIME_URL) — relay target of web2-live-relay, Cloudflare worker chatomni-proxy — forwards /api/web2/_ and /api/pancake_ / /api/pancake-official\*

**SSE:** PUBLISH web2:live-comments — \_notify(action,postId) on /ingest ('realtime'), boost-mark purge ('reconcile'), poller-pages mutations ('poller-pages'); reconcileFullText publishes 'reconcile'. /bulk intentionally does NOT publish. Client side debounces 500-600ms (per project convention).; PUBLISH web2:bulk-send and web2:bulk-send:<jobId> — \_recomputeAndNotify on every item result/cancel; worker debounces recompute 500ms per job (\_scheduleRecompute). Two notifies per recompute (per-job + list).; PUBLISH web2:msg-templates (create/update/delete), web2:quick-replies (create/update/delete), web2:customer-intents (done).; web2-unread + web2-live-relay do not publish directly (unread published by tracker/reconcile service; relay is a proxy).; No EventSource is opened server-side; these routers only publish. No listener-cleanup concerns in this unit (cleanup is on the client pages). In-memory \_boostMarks map (TTL-based, bounded by delete-on-expiry) is per-process and never persisted.

**console.log:** 16 — web2-live-comments.js:329 — warns CLEANUP_SECRET not set on /ingest (operational, acceptable but signals an auth-disabled state in logs); No PII/token logging found in the 7 in-scope route files; all console.\* log e.message or operational markers only. NOTE (coupled service, out of scope file): services/web2-msg-send-worker.js \_mintPAT line 132 puts the Pancake account token in the URL query string — not logged here but a token-in-URL pattern that can leak via upstream access logs

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `logic` Two different dedupe-key schemes write the SAME comment as TWO rows. WS-direct /ingest uses id=`${conv.id}_${message_count||updated_at}` (_mapWsConvToComment), but the poller path (pollPostNow→_fetchConversationComments) uses id=`${postId}_${messageId}`. conv.id ≠ postId and message_count ≠ messageI — `web2-live-comments.js:299 (`${conv.id}\_${seq}`) vs services/web2-livestream-poll` → Unify on the stable Pancake message id: in /ingest derive id from the WS message id when present, or have the poller key by conv.id+seq. At minimum guarantee one canonical key and have reconcile rewri
- 🟠 `race` POST /:id/items/:itemId/result has NO state guard (WHERE id=$1 AND job_id=$2 only) and NO auth. The browser-extension drain can legitimately retry; a duplicate result POST re-UPDATEs the row, double-increments attempts, and can overwrite a previously 'done' item to 'error' (or vice-versa), then re-b — `web2-msg-send.js:327-345` → Add state guard: UPDATE ... WHERE id=$1 AND job_id=$2 AND state IN ('ext_inflight','needs_extension') and return claimed=rows.length>0; only recompute when a row actually changed.
- 🟡 `race` \_recomputeAndNotify is read-modify-write (SELECT GROUP BY → derive job state → UPDATE job) with no locking. Concurrent extension /result calls plus the worker's debounced recompute can interleave so the job's derived state/finished_at reflects a stale snapshot. Usually self-heals on the next event, — `web2-msg-send.js:110-151` → Recompute in a single statement (UPDATE job ... FROM (SELECT counts)) or wrap SELECT+UPDATE in a transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the job row.
- 🟡 `logic` \_mapWsConvToComment dedupe seq falls back to conv.updated_at when message_count is null. updated_at changes on non-comment events too, so the same comment can get different ids across events (duplicates) OR two comments in the same ms can collide to one id (loss). The dedupe key is not derived from — `web2-live-comments.js:299` → Prefer a real per-message id from the WS payload; only fall back to message_count; avoid updated_at as an identity component.
- 🟡 `logic` createdTime for WS-direct ingest uses conv.updated_at (the conversation's latest-activity timestamp), not the comment's own inserted_at. For a returning commenter, every new comment row is stamped with the conversation's updated_at, which can differ from the true comment time and breaks created_time — `web2-live-comments.js:309` → Use the message-level timestamp when present; fall back to conv.updated_at only if no per-message time is available.
- 🟡 `data-loss` GET / legacy cursor `created_time >= since` is still active alongside the correct sinceUpdated cursor. The code's own comments document that a created_time-based delta drops late multi-post comments; any client still passing ?since silently misses comments. — `web2-live-comments.js:516` → Deprecate/remove the since param (or use it only as a non-cursor lower bound on a full reload) and ensure all clients use sinceUpdated.
- 🟡 `race` web2-quick-replies POST computes sort_order via a sub-SELECT MAX(sort_order)+1 inside the INSERT. Two concurrent creates can read the same MAX and produce duplicate sort_order values (no unique constraint), causing nondeterministic ordering. — `web2-quick-replies.js:131-146` → Low impact (tie-broken by id in ORDER BY); consider a sequence or accept duplicates explicitly.
- 🟡 `logic` POST /cancel does not touch items already in state 'sending' (only pending/needs_extension/ext_inflight). An item the worker has already claimed will still be delivered to the customer after the user cancels, with no abort path. 'Cancel job' is silently violated for in-flight items. — `web2-msg-send.js:348-363` → Document that 'sending' items cannot be cancelled, or add a job-level cancel flag the worker checks immediately before the actual send call.
- 🟡 `race` boost-mark suppression state (\_boostMarks Map) is in-process only. Render can run multiple instances and restarts; a /ingest hitting a different instance won't see the boost mark and will store the spam comment, and all marks are lost on restart. The DELETE only cleans rows already ingested at the t — `web2-live-comments.js:268-289,406-439` → Persist active boost marks (small table with expiry) so all instances honor them, or rely on the page-authored heuristic (from.id===page_id) which is already instance-independent.
- ⚪ `web1-coupling` The message-send WORKER (coupled to web2-msg-send.js via \_recomputeAndNotify) reads Web 1.0 chatDb tables pancake_accounts / pancake_page_access_tokens. This is the documented exception, but it means a Web 2.0 send job hard-depends on Web 1.0 credential tables; a chatDb outage breaks Web 2.0 sending — `services/web2-msg-send-worker.js:86,117,144 (consumer of web2-msg-send.js)` → Keep per project policy; confirmed the worker only reads (it does) and the web2-msg-send route never writes chatDb.
- ⚪ `logic` web2-customer-intents POST /:id/done reads done_by from req.body.userName||by defaulting '(ẩn danh)' with no auth → the audit trail of who resolved an intent is unverified and spoofable. — `web2-customer-intents.js:90-100` → Derive done_by from req.web2User (after adding requireWeb2AuthSoft) instead of trusting the body.
- ⚪ `perf` /ingest reconcile loop calls poller.reconcileFullText fire-and-forget for every truncated conv on every event; reconcileFullText has an in-flight guard keyed by rowId, but high comment volume can still spawn many concurrent Pancake message fetches with no global concurrency cap, risking Pancake rate — `web2-live-comments.js:366-391` → Cap concurrent reconciles with a small semaphore/queue and/or coalesce by conv within a short window.
- ℹ️ `logic` campaign_id type mismatch is handled by stringifying the BIGINT campaign id when writing web2_live_comments.campaign_id (VARCHAR) and in the campaigns JOIN (cc.campaign_id = c.id::text). Works but fragile — any future query comparing campaign_id without the cast will silently return zero rows. — `web2-live-comments.js:622-623,742` → Standardize campaign_id column type or centralize the cast in a helper.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 Several state-changing write routes have NO auth middleware at all (not even requireWeb2AuthSoft), so WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE cannot protect them: POST /msg-send/ (create job), /:id/result, /:id/cancel, /:id/items/:itemId/claim-ext, web2-unread POST /reconcile, web2-customer-intents POST /:id/done. An una — `web2-msg-send.js:154,309,327,348; web2-unread.js:75; web2-customer-intents.js:90` → Add requireWeb2AuthSoft (or requireWeb2Auth for msg-send create) to all these handlers so WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 closes them; gate POST /reconcile behind admin/x-admin-secret.
- 🟠 /ingest auth is a shared static secret (CLEANUP_SECRET) and, when the env var is unset, the route accepts ANY caller (warn + pass). Body is unauthenticated customer data written straight to DB and broadcast over SSE. If CLEANUP_SECRET is ever empty in prod, anyone can inject/forge livestream comment — `web2-live-comments.js:320-330` → Fail closed (401) when secret is unset in production (gate the dev bypass behind NODE_ENV!=='production'); consider a dedicated RELAY_SECRET separate from the cleanup/admin secret.
- 🟡 extension-items endpoint returns full PII (customer_name, fb_user_id, message body, error) and has no auth; result/claim endpoints are also open. Customer message content and FB user ids are exposed to any caller who can reach the job UUID. — `web2-msg-send.js:289-306` → Require auth; an unguessable job UUID should not be the only control protecting PII.
- ⚪ PII broadcast surface is well-contained: comment SSE payloads are minimal ({action,postId,ts}); msg-send SSE payloads carry only counters. Good — clients re-fetch. Verify the out-of-scope reconcile service notify also stays id-only. — `web2-live-comments.js:33-44; web2-msg-send.js:147-149` → Keep payloads id-only; no change needed in these files.
- ℹ️ SQL is consistently parameterized across all 7 files ($n placeholders, ANY($1), identifier list in /ingest built from fixed offsets). No string-concatenated user input into SQL. starts*with(id,$1||'*') in boost-mark correctly avoids LIKE wildcard injection. The only dynamic SQL (worker recovery INTE — `web2-live-comments.js:423; throughout` → No action.

**Ghi chú:** Positive: the truncated-comment (customer_id UUID) bug is correctly handled — /ingest passes conv.customers[0].id down to reconcileFullText, and the poller hard-requires custId before calling the Pancake messages API (returns early if missing), matching MEMORY reference_pancake_messages_customer_id.; Positive: GMT+7 handling is correct — parseUtcTs appends 'Z' to naive Pancake timestamps before new Date(), and two marker-gated idempotent migrations (web2_migrations) repair the earlier -7h created_time drift; migrations correctly ROLLBACK+throw on error.; Positive: claim-ext is a proper atomic double-send guard (conditional UPDATE WHERE state='needs_extension'); the worker uses FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for claiming pending items — both are sound concurrency patterns.; Positive: ensureTables uses WeakSet keyed per-pool in templates/quick-replies (avoids the boolean-flag-shared-across-pools bug). web2-live-comments uses a module boolean \_tablesReady which is fine for a single pool but would skip ensure on a second pool.; All 7 routers correctly use web2Db||chatDb and the web2: SSE hub; no Web 1.0 SSE topic or TPOS API usage in scope. The only Web 1.0 reads are the documented quick-replies one-time seed (read-only) and the worker's credential tables.; No network/send was triggered during this static review.

### routes:core-data (web2-products.js, web2-variants.js, web2-generic.js, web2-dedicated-entity.js, web2-so-order.js)

Five Web 2.0 Express routers on the Render API server providing core-data CRUD over the web2Db Postgres pool (fallback chatDb): web2-products (kho SP + purchase pipeline status/pending/stock, history audit, cascade snapshot into native_orders + fast_sale_orders), web2-variants (size/color values + locked short_code generation), web2-generic (schema-driven CRUD over one web2_records table for ~78 entities, plus capture-lock CAS and live-hidden-commenters atomic append), web2-dedicated-entity (factory router cloning the generic shape into per-entity tables web2_delivery_zones/web2_printers), and web2-so-order (single JSONB doc per shop with optimistic-concurrency version guard). All use per-pool WeakSet ensureTables, parameterized SQL, and \_notify(topic, payload) on the realtime-sse-web2 hub after DB commit (topics web2:products, web2:variants, web2:<entity>, web2:so-order). Pool isolation is correct (web2Db || chatDb everywhere; cascade writes target native_orders/fast_sale_orders which are Web 2.0 tables, not Web 1.0). No TPOS coupling. Main weaknesses: web2-products and web2-variants writes are completely unauthenticated while WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 is live, and several concurrency/migration edge cases below.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** Service routers loaded at server boot via require() in server.js (web2-products L637, web2-variants L639, web2-generic L640, web2-so-order L892, web2-dedicated-entity L955).; Mounted: /api/web2-products (server.js L764), /api/web2-variants (L766), /api/web2-so-order (L894), /api/web2/deliveryzone + /api/web2/printer dedicated (L959-960), /api/web2 generic catch-all mounted LAST (L965) so dedicated routes win.; initializeNotifiers wired to web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients at boot: web2-products (L1115), web2-generic (L1124), web2-variants (L1127), web2-so-order (L893), dedicated-entity initDedicatedEntityNotifiers (L963). All point at the Web 2.0 SSE hub (correct).; No interval/cron in these files. ensureTables runs lazily on first request per pool (WeakSet-gated), NOT at boot.; web2-products ensureTables additionally runs migration 078 (backfill product snapshots into native_orders + fast_sale_orders) once, self-gated via native_orders_migrations table; and an unconditional ALTER fast_sale_orders ADD COLUMN updated_at on every cold pool.; web2-dedicated-entity ensure() auto-migrates rows from web2_records into the dedicated table once when the dedicated table is empty.

| Control                                                                                   | Handler                                  | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| GET /api/web2-products/list?search&activeOnly&page&limit                                  | web2-products.js:351                     | Parameterized ILIKE search (search wrapped %..%), capped limit 1000, ORDER BY is_active DESC, created_at DESC, code ASC. Returns mapRow products + total/hasMore. No auth.                                                                                                                                                          |
| GET /api/web2-products/pending?supplier                                                   | web2-products.js:411                     | Lists status='CHO_MUA' AND pending_qty>0, optional supplier filter (param). Declared before /:code to avoid catch.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| GET /api/web2-products/usage?codes=A,B                                                    | web2-products.js:444                     | jsonb_array_elements over native_orders.products, returns per-code list of orders INCLUDING customer_name + full phone (PII) in response body. No auth gate.                                                                                                                                                                        |
| GET /api/web2-products/:code/history                                                      | web2-products.js:520                     | Returns web2_product_history rows (user_id/user_name/changes JSONB). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| GET /api/web2-products/batch?codes / /:code                                               | web2-products.js:561 / 583               | Batch by ANY($1::text[]) or single fetch. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| POST /api/web2-products (create)                                                          | web2-products.js:602                     | Requires code+name+supplier; INSERT with $-params; 23505 -> 409; \_notify('create',code) + \_logHistory. NO AUTH MIDDLEWARE despite enforce live.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| PATCH /api/web2-products/:code                                                            | web2-products.js:671                     | Transaction: SELECT FOR UPDATE prev, guard stock<pending (409 unless ?force), UPDATE allowed fields, cascade name/price/imageUrl into native_orders.products + fast_sale_orders.order_lines (parameterized), COMMIT, then \_logHistory + post-commit SSE (web2:native-orders, web2:fast-sale-orders) + \_notify('update'). NO AUTH. |
| POST /api/web2-products/adjust-stock                                                      | web2-products.js:930                     | Transaction loop, stock=GREATEST(0, stock+delta), clamp warning. \_notify('adjust-stock', codes[]) also cross-broadcasts web2:supplier-wallet. NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| POST /api/web2-products/mark-printed                                                      | web2-products.js:989                     | print_count+1 for codes (single non-transactional UPDATE). NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| DELETE /api/web2-products/:code?force                                                     | web2-products.js:1022                    | Atomic DELETE guarded by pending=0 OR force; 0 rows -> re-SELECT to distinguish 404 vs 409 pending. \_notify('delete') + \_logHistory. NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| POST /api/web2-products/adjust-pending                                                    | web2-products.js:1085                    | Transaction, SELECT FOR UPDATE (3 match branches code/variant/name+supplier), ghost-delete when pending=0&stock=0&created_by='so-order', status transition. NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                |
| POST /api/web2-products/upsert-pending                                                    | web2-products.js:1223                    | so-order draft flow: match name+variant+supplier FOR UPDATE, INSERT new (CHO_MUA) or pending+=qty; resolveOnly mode resolves code only. NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| POST /api/web2-products/confirm-purchase                                                  | web2-products.js:1412                    | Single UPDATE WHERE status IN (CHO_MUA,MUA_1_PHAN) [+codes/supplier], stock+=pending, pending=0. NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| POST /api/web2-products/confirm-purchase-partial                                          | web2-products.js:1489                    | Transaction, FOR UPDATE per item, qtyR=min(req,pending), stock+=qtyR. NO AUTH.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| POST /api/web2-products/backfill-supplier                                                 | web2-products.js:1602                    | requireWeb2Admin. Maps code prefix->supplier for rows missing supplier.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| POST /api/web2-variants (create) / PATCH /:id / DELETE /:id                               | web2-variants.js:318 / 381 / 447         | shortCode auto-suggest+validate /^[A-Z0-9]{1,20}$/, 23505->409. NO AUTH on any of create/patch/delete.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| GET /api/web2-variants/suggest-short-code                                                 | web2-variants.js:252                     | Loads ALL locked short_codes into memory Map, computes collision-free code. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| POST /api/web2-variants/backfill-short-codes                                              | web2-variants.js:269                     | requireWeb2Admin. Assigns short_code to NULL rows (N+1 queries, each calls \_suggestShortCode which re-scans all rows).                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| GET /api/web2/\_storage                                                                   | web2-generic.js:100                      | Returns pg_total_relation_size + pg_database_size + per-entity breakdown. NO AUTH — leaks DB-level size info.                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| POST /api/web2/capture-lock/acquire \| /release                                           | web2-generic.js:166 / 209                | Atomic CAS upsert on web2_records lock row (TTL-based). NO AUTH (any caller can force-acquire/release the livestream capture lock).                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /api/web2/live-hidden-commenters/hide/:fbId \| unhide/:fbId                          | web2-generic.js:245 / 292                | requireWeb2AuthSoft. Atomic jsonb append/filter of commenters array.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| GET /api/web2/:entity/(health\|list\|get/:code)                                           | web2-generic.js:326 / 346 / 399          | validSlug gate, parameterized. NO AUTH on reads.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| POST /api/web2/:entity/create \| PATCH update/:code \| DELETE delete/:code \| bulk-create | web2-generic.js:421 / 484 / 656 / 682    | requireWeb2AuthSoft (enforced 401 when WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1). update uses transaction+FOR UPDATE history append; bulk-create cap 5000 ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; PROTECTED_DATA_FIELDS strips state-machine fields for purchase-refund.                                                                                                 |
| POST /api/web2/:entity/delete-all \| /\_vacuum                                            | web2-generic.js:591 / 620                | requireWeb2Admin + body.confirm===true. delete-all bulk; \_vacuum runs VACUUM [FULL] ANALYZE web2_records.                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| web2-dedicated-entity create/update/delete                                                | web2-dedicated-entity.js:157 / 204 / 264 | requireWeb2AuthSoft; update transaction+FOR UPDATE history append; delete-all requireWeb2Admin. Table name from hardcoded server map (safe).                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| GET /api/web2-so-order/get                                                                | web2-so-order.js:66                      | requireWeb2AuthSoft. Returns single doc 'main' {data,version,lastUpdated} or empty.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /api/web2-so-order/save {data,baseVersion}                                           | web2-so-order.js:97                      | requireWeb2AuthSoft. Optimistic concurrency: baseVersion<=0 INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (else 409), else UPDATE WHERE version=baseVersion (0 rows -> 409 with server snapshot). \_notify('update',v).                                                                                                                             |
| POST /api/web2-so-order/reset                                                             | web2-so-order.js:177                     | requireWeb2Admin. DELETE doc 'main'.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

**Liên kết:** web2/products/ (Kho SP page) — consumes /list, /usage, /history, PATCH, adjust-stock, mark-printed, confirm-purchase\*, web2/variants/ (Kho Biến Thể) — consumes web2-variants CRUD + suggest-short-code, so-order/ (Sổ Order) — consumes web2-so-order /get,/save and web2-products upsert-pending/adjust-pending/confirm-purchase, native-orders/ and fast-sale-orders route — targets of PATCH cascade (native_orders.products, fast_sale_orders.order_lines) + migration 078, render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js — SSE hub all \_notify calls publish to, render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js — requireWeb2AuthSoft/requireWeb2Admin used by generic/dedicated/so-order, render.com/routes/web2-supplier-wallet.js — receives cross-broadcast web2:supplier-wallet on stock-affecting actions, render.com/routes/purchase-refund.js — PROTECTED_DATA_FIELDS in generic update guards its state machine, render.com/routes/web2-returns.js — shares web2_products.return_qty column, web2/shared/web2-sse-bridge.js (Web2SSE) — client subscriber of all topics below

**SSE:** Published web2:products (web2-products \_notify) — actions create/update/delete/adjust-stock/mark-printed/adjust-pending/upsert-pending/confirm-purchase/confirm-purchase-partial/backfill-supplier; payload {action, code, codes, ts} (mã SP only, no PII).; Cross-published web2:supplier-wallet on stock-affecting actions (adjust-stock/upsert-pending/confirm-purchase\*/adjust-pending) — payload includes from:'web2:products'.; Published web2:native-orders and web2:fast-sale-orders (PATCH cascade, post-commit only) — {action:'product-snapshot-sync', productCode, affected, ts}.; Published web2:variants — {action, id, ts}.; Published web2:<entity> for generic (create/update/delete/bulk-create/delete-all) and capture-lock (acquire/force-acquire/release) and live-hidden-commenters (hide/unhide) — {action, code, ts}.; Published web2:<slug> for dedicated entity (deliveryzone/printer) — {action, code, ts}.; Published web2:so-order — {action:create|update|reset, version, ts}.; All \_notify calls happen AFTER COMMIT (transactional handlers) or after autocommit (single-statement handlers), before res.json — no \_notify-before-commit found. \_notify wrapped in try/catch (failure logged, does not break response). No server-side debounce (client debounces). \_notifyClients is a module-level mutable set once at boot. Publish-only — no subscriptions in this unit, so no listener cleanup concern.

**console.log:** 39 — web2-products.js:663 console.error('[WEB2-PRODUCTS] POST / error:', e) — logs full error object (may include body context but not customer PII).; web2-products.js:287 / web2-variants.js:70 / web2-generic.js:69 — boot-noise '[...] Tables created/verified' logged on every cold pool (acceptable but noisy on Render restarts).; No console call logs tokens/x-web2-token/phone/customer_name. PII (customer_name+phone) is returned in the /usage HTTP RESPONSE body but is NOT logged. web2-auth \_softWarn logs method+path only, no token.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `race` web2-products POST / (create) is NOT transactional/row-locked while the so-order flow inserts the same product via upsert-pending (FOR UPDATE matching on name+variant+supplier). Two paths race: create inserts a NEW code while a concurrent upsert-pending FOR UPDATE finds no row and ALSO inserts -> tw — `web2-products.js:602-661 vs 1270-1340` → Route product creation through a single path, or add a unique partial index on (LOWER(name),LOWER(variant),LOWER(supplier)) so concurrent create/upsert collapse to one row.
- 🟡 `perf` web2-variants backfill-short-codes is O(N^2): each NULL-short_code row calls \_suggestShortCode which SELECTs ALL locked short_codes (full scan) -> N rows means N scans. Worse, an assigned code from iteration i is not visible to i+1's scan (committed separately), so two new rows in the batch can be s — `web2-variants.js:269-300,136-187` → Load locked codes once into a Set, reserve newly-assigned codes in that Set during the loop, batch the UPDATEs.
- 🟡 `race` web2-variants auto-suggested shortCode on create is read-then-insert non-atomically: two concurrent creates of different values can both be suggested the same free code; the second gets 23505 -> 409 surfaced to the USER as a spurious conflict even though a valid code existed. — `web2-variants.js:330-336,346-374` → On 23505 for auto-suggested codes, retry suggestion with numeric suffix instead of returning 409 to the user.
- 🟡 `logic` web2-products migration 078 runs a heavy DO-block (per-product UPDATE of native_orders + fast_sale_orders) inside the lazy ensureTables on the FIRST request to ANY products endpoint per cold pool, blocking that request potentially for seconds. Self-gated by native_orders_migrations so idempotent, bu — `web2-products.js:229-284` → Move 078 to a one-shot boot migration runner instead of request-path ensureTables, or insert the marker first and make the loop resumable.
- 🟡 `race` Stock can be lost across endpoints with different update semantics: PATCH /:code sets stock to an ABSOLUTE value (takes FOR UPDATE at 710 but applies the client's literal), while adjust-stock applies a RELATIVE delta. A delta committed by adjust-stock after the PATCH client read its form value is cl — `web2-products.js:720-743 vs 948-954` → Route all stock changes through the relative adjust-stock path, or re-read stock under the PATCH lock and reject/merge if it changed since the client read.
- ⚪ `logic` Stock-only PATCH emits action 'update', which is NOT in stockAffectingActions, so web2:supplier-wallet is NOT cross-broadcast. Supplier debt pill (qty\*cost) on Ví NCC goes stale after a manual stock/price edit via PATCH. — `web2-products.js:97-110,905` → Detect stock/price change in PATCH and additionally cross-broadcast web2:supplier-wallet.
- ⚪ `logic` web2-dedicated-entity ensure() auto-migrate copies from web2_records only when the dedicated table COUNT=0. If a partial insert previously happened (dedicated had >=1 row), the bulk migrate is permanently skipped and remaining web2_records rows for that slug are never copied — silent divergence betw — `web2-dedicated-entity.js:70-89` → Use a migration marker (like native_orders_migrations) instead of COUNT=0 gating.
- ⚪ `logic` web2-products PATCH cascade triggers whenever req.body has name/price/imageUrl, including price:null. A PATCH explicitly setting price:null coerces Number(null)=0 and writes priceUnit/price=0 into EVERY matching order line across native_orders + fast_sale_orders — zeroing snapshot prices. — `web2-products.js:789-792,841-843` → Validate price is a finite non-negative number before cascading; skip cascade when the value is null/NaN.
- ⚪ `data-loss` adjust-stock GREATEST(0, stock+delta) silently clamps oversell to 0 and only emits a best-effort warning when result==0, without reporting the magnitude of the negative that was absorbed. Inventory drifts positive vs reality and the caller cannot reliably detect the oversell. — `web2-products.js:948-966` → Return requested delta vs applied delta so callers detect oversell, or reject when stock+delta<0 unless force.
- ⚪ `race` web2-so-order /save returns the 409 server snapshot via a SECOND SELECT after the failed UPDATE; another writer can bump version between the failed UPDATE and that SELECT, so the returned snapshot version may already be stale (benign: client just 409s again). — `web2-so-order.js:143-169` → Acceptable; optionally return the conflicting row in a single CTE to reduce churn.
- ℹ️ `race` adjust-pending / upsert-pending name+variant+supplier match uses LOWER() equality without a covering unique constraint; concurrent so-order drafts adding the same item may FOR UPDATE on DIFFERENT rows (base variant NULL vs exact variant) per ORDER BY tie-break, both proceed, double-counting pending — `web2-products.js:1120-1146,1256-1271` → Verify the exact-variant-first ORDER BY deterministically selects the same row under concurrency; consider a unique partial index on (LOWER(name),LOWER(variant),LOWER(supplier)).
- ℹ️ `other` web2-products ensureTables unconditionally runs ALTER TABLE IF EXISTS fast_sale_orders ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS updated_at on every cold pool — a cross-module schema write from the products route. Idempotent but couples products migration to fast_sale_orders schema; surprising ownership. — `web2-products.js:218-220` → Keep but document; ideally own fast_sale_orders schema only in its own ensureTables.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 All mutating endpoints in web2-products.js (POST /, PATCH /:code, DELETE /:code, adjust-stock, mark-printed, adjust-pending, upsert-pending, confirm-purchase, confirm-purchase-partial) and web2-variants.js (POST /, PATCH /:id, DELETE /:id) have NO auth middleware. With WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 live in pr — `web2-products.js:602,671,930,989,1022,1085,1223,1412,1489 ; web2-variants.js:318` → Add requireWeb2AuthSoft to all product/variant write handlers (consider requireWeb2Admin for DELETE), matching web2-generic; or guard at router level for POST/PATCH/DELETE methods.
- 🟡 GET /api/web2/\_storage returns pg_database_size, pg_total_relation_size and per-entity row counts/byte sizes with no auth — information disclosure about DB internals/scale. — `web2-generic.js:100` → Gate with requireWeb2Admin (monitoring is admin-only) or at least requireWeb2AuthSoft.
- 🟡 capture-lock acquire/release have no auth. Any caller can force-acquire (force:true) or release the livestream capture leader lock, hijacking or disabling capture from another machine. — `web2-generic.js:166,209` → Apply requireWeb2AuthSoft so enforce mode blocks anonymous lock manipulation.
- ⚪ GET /api/web2-products/usage returns full customer_name + unmasked phone in the HTTP response, and the endpoint is unauthenticated. PII exposure to any caller. — `web2-products.js:466-467,494-495` → Gate behind auth and/or mask phone (last 4 digits) unless caller is authenticated staff.
- ℹ️ SQL is consistently parameterized; interpolated fragments (sets/where/conds/order/whereParts/valuesSql/tableName/entitySlug) are built from fixed allowed-column maps, literal strings, or hardcoded server args (deliveryzone/printer table names). No user-controlled string reaches a query body. No SQL — `web2-products.js:739,800,852,1130,1270,1449 ; web2-dedicated-entity.js:56-67` → No change; note web2-dedicated-entity table-name interpolation is only safe because makeDedicatedEntityRouter is called with hardcoded constants — never wire it to user input.
- ℹ️ requireWeb2AuthSoft accepts token via query ?token= and body.token in addition to header. ?token= can leak into access logs / referers. — `middleware/web2-auth.js:34-41` → Prefer header-only for tokens; deprecate the query-string path.

**Ghi chú:** Pool isolation is correct across all five files: every handler uses req.app.locals.web2Db || req.app.locals.chatDb, and ensureTables is WeakSet-keyed per pool so cold-start fallback to chatDb cannot make web2Db skip table creation. No Web 1.0 table (customers, customer_wallets, balance_history) is read/written. No TPOS API call. No bare snake_case (Web 1.0) SSE topic published.; Cascade targets native_orders and fast_sale_orders are Web 2.0 tables (legacy non-prefixed names but Web 2.0 per CLAUDE.md), so cross-table writes are within-layer — not a Web 1.0 coupling bug.; \_notify is consistently called after COMMIT/autocommit and before res.json; no \_notify-before-commit found (the stated hunt target is clean here).; Timezone: these routes store epoch millis (Date.now()) in BIGINT columns and do not format/display dates, so no GMT+7 / missing-Z surface in this unit.; Error handling: transactional handlers ROLLBACK in catch with .catch(()=>{}) and release client in finally — no leaked clients. \_logHistory and \_notify are best-effort try/catch and do not poison transactions.; SQL injection: none found — all dynamic SQL fragments derive from fixed allowed-column maps, literal ORDER BY strings, or hardcoded table/slug args; values always parameterized.; Biggest actionable gap is the missing write-auth on web2-products and web2-variants under WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 — fix first.

### routes:fb — render.com/routes/web2-fb-posts.js

Express router mounted at /api/web2-fb-posts (server.js:942) that powers the Web 2.0 "Đăng bài Facebook" page: connect a Facebook account (OAuth dialog or pasted token), store the long-lived user token + per-page access tokens in web2*fb_post_tokens (web2Db), compose/publish/schedule/edit/delete posts on the shop's 2 real FB pages via Graph API (services/web2-fb-graph-service.js), generate AI/template captions, read engagement + ad insights, and keep a manual ad ledger (web2_fb_ad_entries) plus drafts/scheduled store (web2_fb_posts). Correctly uses web2Db pool, web2* table prefix, and the web2:fb-posts SSE hub. The dominant risk is that the ENTIRE router is unauthenticated — no requireWeb2Auth/Soft middleware anywhere — so every state-changing endpoint (publish to live pages, delete posts, disconnect, overwrite token) is reachable without x-web2-token, directly contradicting WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 that gates sibling routes.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** At boot (server.js:937-942): require('./routes/web2-fb-posts'), initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients) wires SSE hub web2:fb-posts, ensureSchema(web2Pool||chatDbPool) creates web2_fb_post_tokens / web2_fb_posts / web2_fb_ad_entries + 2 indexes (idempotent), app.use('/api/web2-fb-posts', router); Module-level: OAUTH_BASE/OAUTH_CALLBACK computed from env WEB2_FB_OAUTH_BASE (default worker URL) — used to build OAuth redirect_uri; Graph service module-level: reads FB_APP_ID / FB_APP_SECRET env (shared with Web 1.0 fb-ads — config only, not W1 data); \_liveCache Map (60s per-page) created; No cron/interval owned by this route; FB-scheduled posts auto-publish on FB side via scheduled_publish_time (no server cron)

| Control                                                          | Handler              | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| GET /status                                                      | web2-fb-posts.js:186 | loadToken (single newest row), returns connected flag, expired flag, user {id,name}, safePages (NO access_token), aiAvailable, oauthAvailable. Leaks user_id + name + page list to ANY unauthenticated caller.                                                                                                    |
| GET /auth/login-url?return&scope                                 | web2-fb-posts.js:223 | Builds FB OAuth dialog URL with SCOPES_FULL (or SCOPES_POST if scope=min), state=b64url({r:safeReturn,t}). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| GET /auth/callback?code&state                                    | web2-fb-posts.js:238 | exchangeCodeForToken→exchangeLongLivedToken→getMe→getPages→saveToken (plaintext user+page tokens), \_notify('connect'), returns HTML that location.replace(back). No auth; CSRF on state only loosely (timestamp not validated).                                                                                  |
| POST /connect {token}                                            | web2-fb-posts.js:285 | Accepts arbitrary FB user token, exchanges long-lived, saves tokens, \_notify. UNAUTHENTICATED — anyone can overwrite the stored shop FB token.                                                                                                                                                                   |
| POST /disconnect                                                 | web2-fb-posts.js:314 | DELETE FROM web2_fb_post_tokens (ALL rows). UNAUTHENTICATED — anyone can sever the shop's FB connection (DoS).                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| POST /refresh-pages                                              | web2-fb-posts.js:326 | Re-fetches pages with stored user_token and re-saves. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| POST /caption {product\|products,style,ai}                       | web2-fb-posts.js:348 | Generates caption via caption service (template free or AI). No auth; ai=true may consume paid/free LLM quota for anonymous callers.                                                                                                                                                                              |
| POST /publish {pageIds,message,media,link,scheduledTime,draftId} | web2-fb-posts.js:371 | For each page: findPage→fb.publishToPage (Graph /feed\|/videos\|/photos), 1.5s spacing, stop on FB rate-limit codes 80001/32/4. Persists/updates web2_fb_posts row, \_notify(status). UNAUTHENTICATED — anyone can publish/schedule to the shop's real FB pages. media url + link forwarded to FB (SSRF surface). |
| GET /list?pageId&limit&after                                     | web2-fb-posts.js:491 | fb.listPagePosts + getLiveVideoMap + listScheduledPosts (first page only). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| GET /post-detail?pageId&postId                                   | web2-fb-posts.js:521 | fb.getPostDetail (rich w/ comments fallback basic). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| POST /delete {pageId,postId}                                     | web2-fb-posts.js:541 | fb.deletePost via page token, \_notify('delete'). UNAUTHENTICATED destructive — anyone can delete any post on the connected pages.                                                                                                                                                                                |
| POST /post-edit {pageId,postId,message?,scheduledTime?}          | web2-fb-posts.js:563 | fb.updatePost (caption and/or reschedule), \_notify('edit'). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| GET /engagement?pageId&limit                                     | web2-fb-posts.js:587 | getPageBasic+getEngagementPosts+getPageInsights+enrichPostsWithInsights (parallel, resilient). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| GET /insights-probe?pageId&postId                                | web2-fb-posts.js:633 | Diagnostic probePostMetrics per-metric. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| GET /ad-accounts                                                 | web2-fb-posts.js:657 | fb.getAdAccounts(user_token) — exposes shop ad accounts (id, spend, currency) to anonymous callers.                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| GET /ad-insights?actId&preset                                    | web2-fb-posts.js:671 | fb.getAdInsights(user_token) — exposes ad spend/campaign data. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| GET /ad-entries?pageId&from&to                                   | web2-fb-posts.js:688 | Parameterized SELECT from web2_fb_ad_entries (LIMIT 1000). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| POST /ad-entry {id?,...}                                         | web2-fb-posts.js:718 | INSERT or UPDATE manual ad ledger row, num() sanitizes numerics, \_notify('ad-entry'). No auth — anyone can write/overwrite ledger.                                                                                                                                                                               |
| DELETE /ad-entry/:id                                             | web2-fb-posts.js:798 | DELETE FROM web2_fb_ad_entries WHERE id=$1, \_notify. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| GET /drafts?status                                               | web2-fb-posts.js:811 | SELECT drafts (LIMIT 200). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| POST /draft {id?,...}                                            | web2-fb-posts.js:829 | INSERT/UPDATE draft row, \_notify('draft'). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| DELETE /draft/:id                                                | web2-fb-posts.js:863 | DELETE FROM web2_fb_posts WHERE id=$1, \_notify. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |

**Liên kết:** web2/fb-posts/ (consumer page — Sale Online menu), services/web2-fb-graph-service.js (all Graph API calls, token usage, SSRF media forwarding), services/web2-caption-service.js (template/AI caption, hasAnyAiKey), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (SSE hub web2:fb-posts via initializeNotifiers/notifyClients), render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js (requireWeb2Auth/Soft — NOT used here, this is the gap), cloudflare-worker/modules/config/routes.js:158,283 (worker route WEB2_FB_POSTS → web2-api; OAuth callback also routes through worker), web2Db pool (server.js app.locals.web2Db)

**SSE:** Publishes web2:fb-posts via \_notify(action,id) at web2-fb-posts.js:56-63 with actions: connect, disconnect, published/scheduled/failed, delete, edit, draft, draft-delete, ad-entry, ad-entry-delete.
