Payload is {action, id, ts} — no PII/token leakage (id is a DB row id or FB postId, safe).; No subscriber/EventSource in this server-side file (route is publisher only). \_notifyClients guarded with try/catch + console.warn on failure (web2-fb-posts.js:60). No debounce here — debounce is the client's responsibility on the page side.; \_notify failures swallowed to a warn (acceptable — SSE is best-effort).

**console.log:** 3 — web2-fb-posts.js:61 console.warn \_notify failed (e.message only — safe); web2-fb-posts.js:122 console.log schema ready (no data — safe); web2-fb-posts.js:124 console.error ensureSchema failed (e.message only — safe). No FB tokens, no customer PII logged.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `logic` loadToken ignores user_id and always returns the single newest row: SELECT ... ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1. The table is PRIMARY KEY (user_id) and saveToken upserts per user, so if two FB accounts ever connect, only the most-recently-updated account is ever usable. findPage(row.pages, pageId) f — `web2-fb-posts.js:129-139 loadToken; used by every page-token lookup` → Either enforce single-account semantics explicitly (DELETE others on connect), or pass the relevant userId / search across all rows' pages for the requested pageId.
- 🟠 `race` /publish with draftId is not idempotent and has no lock. Two concurrent requests (double-click / retried POST / two tabs) both load the same draft, both call fb.publishToPage (Graph create is NOT idempotent — each call creates a new FB post), then both UPDATE the same row. Result: duplicate live pos — `web2-fb-posts.js:371-466` → Take a pg advisory lock keyed on draftId (or a per-token mutex) around publish, or pre-mark the draft status='publishing' with a conditional UPDATE ... WHERE status<>'publishing' and bail if 0 rows af
- 🟡 `logic` Schedule-time unit mismatch between route and service. Route stores scheduled_at = toMs(scheduledTime) (ms, web2-fb-posts.js:443/459) while graph-service.normalizeScheduleSec independently re-parses scheduledTime to seconds for FB. If the client sends seconds vs ms inconsistently, toMs (n>1e12?n:n*1 — `web2-fb-posts.js:483-487 toMs; graph-service.js:164-180 normalizeScheduleSec` → Normalize once (server) to a canonical epoch-seconds, pass that to both FB and the DB (store as ms = sec*1000 from the same value).
- 🟡 `logic` GMT+7 / scheduling UX: normalizeScheduleSec compares against Date.now() and requires delta >= 10 min. If the client builds scheduledTime from a naive local datetime string without timezone, new Date(string) on the Render server (TZ=Asia/Saigon, +7) interprets it as +7 — which happens to match the in — `graph-service.js:168-179; web2-fb-posts.js:483-487` → Require client to send an explicit epoch (ms/sec, UTC) or an ISO string with timezone; never parse naive local strings server-side.
- 🟡 `logic` draftId branch in /publish does not verify the row exists. If draftId is stale/deleted, UPDATE ... WHERE id=$9 affects 0 rows but the code still returns success with id=draftId, and the FB post was already created — so a real post exists with no DB record. Also createdBy is accepted from the body un — `web2-fb-posts.js:432-467` → Check rowCount on the UPDATE; if 0, INSERT a new record instead so the published post is always tracked. Derive createdBy from req.web2User after adding auth.
- ⚪ `other` /publish error handling: if fb.publishToPage throws for a non-rate-limit reason on page i, the loop continues to page i+1, but a thrown error inside the broader try (e.g. normalizeScheduleSec throwing for a bad scheduledTime) is caught per-page only via the inner try; a malformed scheduledTime makes — `web2-fb-posts.js:402-426` → Validate scheduledTime via normalizeScheduleSec once before the loop and return 400 on failure.
- ⚪ `perf` \_liveCache (graph-service.js:354) is an unbounded module-level Map keyed by pageId; with only 2 pages this is fine, but it never evicts stale entries beyond the 60s freshness check (entries persist forever). Minor; not a leak in practice given fixed page count. — `graph-service.js:354-375` → Bound the map or rely on the fixed 2-page set; low priority.
- ⚪ `logic` DELETE /ad-entry/:id and DELETE /draft/:id pass req.params.id straight to a $1 parameter without validating it is numeric (BIGSERIAL). A non-numeric id yields a Postgres cast error caught as a 500 rather than a clean 400; not an injection (parameterized) but poor input validation. — `web2-fb-posts.js:801, 866` → parseInt + validate id before querying; return 400 on NaN.

**Security (audit):**

- 🔴 ENTIRE router is unauthenticated. No requireWeb2Auth/requireWeb2AuthSoft/requireWeb2Admin used anywhere; server.js:942 mounts it with no gate. With WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 active for sibling routes, this route bypasses auth entirely. Any anonymous caller (through the public worker/proxy) can publish & s — `web2-fb-posts.js (whole file); server.js:937-942` → Apply requireWeb2Auth (hard) on all mutating endpoints (/connect, /disconnect, /refresh-pages, /publish, /delete, /post-edit, /draft*, /ad-entry*) and at minimum requireWeb2AuthSoft on reads. Mount as
- 🔴 POST /connect accepts an arbitrary FB user access token from an unauthenticated request body and persists it as the shop's connection (saveToken overwrites by user_id, and loadToken always reads the newest row). An attacker can inject their own token, or simply call /disconnect to wipe the real conn — `web2-fb-posts.js:285-311; /disconnect 314-323` → Gate behind requireWeb2Admin. Reject token paste in production (prefer OAuth-only). Treat token store as admin-only state.
- 🟠 FB user token and per-page access tokens are stored AT REST IN PLAINTEXT in web2_fb_post_tokens.user_token and pages JSONB (p.access_token). A DB dump yields fully usable, long-lived FB credentials for the shop pages (post/delete/ads). Note the project already hashes session tokens (middleware/web2- — `web2-fb-posts.js:140-149 saveToken; graph-service getPages:144-159` → Encrypt tokens at rest (AES-GCM with a KMS/env key) or at minimum a column-level secret; never store page access_token unencrypted. Decrypt only when calling Graph.
- 🟠 SSRF / outbound-fetch abuse via media URLs and link. publishToPage forwards media[].url to FB as file_url (videos) / photo url, and link to /feed, with no allowlist/validation. Combined with the missing auth, an anonymous attacker controls what FB fetches (and the worker/Render may proxy image fetch — `graph-service.js:217 (photo url), 245-251 (file_url), 264-272 (link); _decodeIma` → Validate media url/link against an allowlist of trusted hosts (Kho SP / studio / imgbb CDN), require https, block private/loopback ranges; cap decoded base64 size.
- 🟡 OAuth state is not bound to the caller and the timestamp (t) is never validated, so the state value provides only weak CSRF protection on the callback. Because /connect and the whole flow are unauthenticated, an attacker can also drive the OAuth login flow. — `web2-fb-posts.js:231 (state build), 242-244 (state parse — only r read, t ignore` → Bind state to the authenticated web2User session and verify a server-stored nonce + max-age on callback.
- ⚪ safeReturn allows redirect to any *nhijudy.github.io / nhijudy.store / localhost host via regex; the regex [\w.-]*nhijudy\.(store|github\.io) also matches lookalike subdomain prefixes (e.g. evilnhijudy.store) because of the leading [\w.-]\* with no boundary. — `web2-fb-posts.js:44-49` → Anchor host matching to exact allowed origins (parse URL, compare hostname against a fixed set) instead of substring regex.

**Ghi chú:** Pool usage is CORRECT: getDb returns web2Db||chatDb (web2-fb-posts.js:22); all tables are web2\_ prefixed; SSE uses the web2 hub (web2:fb-posts). No TPOS coupling, no Web 1.0 table/pool/SSE reads — clean layer separation on data, the only coupling is the shared FB_APP_ID/SECRET env which is config, not W1 data (acceptable).; All SQL is parameterized ($n) — no SQL injection found. The dynamic WHERE in /ad-entries (688-710) builds clauses with incrementing $params, values bound separately — safe.; The HTML returned by /auth/callback escapes the error message (replace /[<>&]/g) and JSON.stringify's the redirect URL into the script — reasonable XSS hygiene for the failure/success pages, though safeReturn's loose regex (see security low) is the weak link.; Worker correctly routes /api/web2-fb-posts/\* → web2-api (routes.js:283) and forwards x-web2-token via CORS allowedHeaders (server.js:129) — so the missing-auth issue is purely that the route never CONSUMES the token, not that the header is stripped.; Token is never returned to the browser: safePages strips access_token (web2-fb-posts.js:167-178) and /status omits user_token — this part is implemented correctly; the at-rest plaintext storage is the remaining token-exposure gap.

### routes:misc (web2-jt-tracking, web2-ai-script, web2-cutout, web2-pancake-refresh, web2-vieneu-registry)

Five small Web 2.0 Express routers on the Render web2-api service. web2-jt-tracking (1018 lines) is the heavyweight: it scrapes jtexpress.vn server-side HTML for J&T waybill status, ingests 12-digit codes from Zalo group messages (live listener + history + pasted text + manual), stores state in web2_jt_tracking (web2Db), auto-purges approved rows after 7 days, and broadcasts SSE topic web2:jt-tracking. web2-ai-script proxies Gemini (WEB2_GEMINI_API_KEY) to write a sales video script. web2-cutout proxies paid background-removal APIs (PhotoRoom/withoutbg/fal BiRefNet) with soft auth + per-IP rate limit. web2-pancake-refresh stores encrypted Pancake login creds and auto-renews JWT (cron every 6h) into pancake_accounts on chatDb (shared web1/web2 token store). web2-vieneu-registry is an in-memory heartbeat registry of shop VieNeu TTS machines with an optional shared secret. Notable: the JT router and the vieneu registry have NO Web 2.0 auth middleware, and JT /clear wipes the whole table with only a magic-string confirm.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** server.js:767 mounts web2-cutout at /api/web2/cutout BEFORE the generic /api/web2 catch-all (path-specificity matters).; server.js:773-779 mounts web2-ai-script at /api/web2/ai-script unless WEB1_ONLY=1 (try/catch so a load error won't crash boot).; server.js:927-932 requires web2-jt-tracking, wires initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients), runs ensureSchema(web2Pool||chatDbPool) fire-and-forget, mounts at /api/web2-jt-tracking with NO auth middleware.; web2-jt-tracking ensureSchema(): ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS (idempotent), CREATE TABLE web2_jt_tracking + web2_jt_bc_tags + 5 indexes, then a one-time backfill UPDATE of src_at WHERE src_at IS NULL (self-terminating).; server.js:945 mounts web2-vieneu-registry at /api/web2-vieneu-registry inside try/catch (no auth, no schema).; server.js:1011-1044 requires web2-pancake-refresh, calls .init(chatDbPool) (ensureColumns ALTER pancake_accounts), initializeNotifiers, and .startCron(chatDbPool) at line 1044.; web2-pancake-refresh module-load: setInterval cleanup of rate-limit buckets every 15m, .unref() so it doesn't keep the process alive.; web2-pancake-refresh.startCron: setTimeout(run,60000) then setInterval every 6h calling refreshExpiring(pool,{withinDays:5}).; web2-cutout module-load: in-memory \_cutoutHits Map for per-IP rate limiting (no interval; cleared wholesale when size>1000).; web2-zalo.js:341 fire-and-forget calls jt.autoIngestFromZalo(\_pool,msg) on every new inbound GROUP Zalo message, which inserts pending rows + spawns a detached async loop fetching jtexpress.vn per code.

| Control                                                | Handler                                       | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| POST /api/web2-jt-tracking/clear                       | router.post('/clear') web2-jt-tracking.js:430 | DELETEs ALL rows from web2_jt_tracking if confirm==='YES-CLEAR'. NO auth, NO admin secret — destructive wipe gated only by a magic string. \_notify('clear').                                                                      |
| POST /scan                                             | web2-jt-tracking.js:443                       | Reads up to 5000 web2_zalo_messages matching a 12-digit + 'shop nhi judy' regex, extracts order codes, upserts pending rows (GREATEST on src_at), \_notify('scan'). No auth.                                                       |
| POST /scan-history                                     | web2-jt-tracking.js:500                       | Calls zca.getGroupHistory for each tracked group (live Zalo read), extracts codes within a days window, upserts. No auth.                                                                                                          |
| POST /scan-text                                        | web2-jt-tracking.js:598                       | Parses pasted text line-by-line for order codes, upserts tracking rows, and may INSERT synthetic 'paste:<code>' rows into web2_zalo_messages (dedup by ILIKE), publishing web2:zalo:messages + web2:zalo:thread:<id> SSE. No auth. |
| POST /add                                              | web2-jt-tracking.js:728                       | Extracts 12-digit codes from body.text/codes[], inserts pending rows ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, \_notify('add'). No auth.                                                                                                             |
| POST /track                                            | web2-jt-tracking.js:760                       | Validates billcode is 12 digits, fetchJt() scrapes jtexpress.vn, \_upsertTracked, \_notify('track'). No auth.                                                                                                                      |
| POST /refresh                                          | web2-jt-tracking.js:801                       | \_rederiveStored (re-classify from stored events) then bounded batch (<=15) fetchJt with CONC=3, retry-once, sleep pacing; \_notify('refresh'). No auth, no per-IP limit on the outbound scrape burst.                             |
| GET /list                                              | web2-jt-tracking.js:888                       | Calls \_purgeApproved (DELETE approved>7d) then returns filtered/sorted rows + KPI counts. Search builds parameterized ILIKE clauses (safe). No auth — returns src_message containing customer name/phone/notes.                   |
| GET /:billcode                                         | web2-jt-tracking.js:951                       | Validates 12 digits, returns row; auto-fetches from J&T if never fetched. No auth.                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /:billcode/approve \| /unapprove                  | web2-jt-tracking.js:978 / :998                | Sets/clears approved_at (approve starts the 7-day auto-delete clock). No auth.                                                                                                                                                     |
| GET/POST /bc-tags /bc-tag                              | web2-jt-tracking.js:692 / :704                | Mirror of tag state keyed by raw phone string; upsert/delete web2_jt_bc_tags. No auth, no phone validation.                                                                                                                        |
| POST /api/web2/ai-script/generate                      | web2-ai-script.js:61                          | Validates topic+products, builds prompt, POSTs to Gemini generateContent with key in URL query, parses JSON, returns {narration,scenes}. NO auth middleware — anyone reaching the endpoint burns the Gemini quota.                 |
| GET /api/web2/ai-script/status                         | web2-ai-script.js:115                         | Returns {configured:!!key, model} without leaking the key.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| POST /api/web2/cutout/{withoutbg\|birefnet\|photoroom} | web2-cutout.js:51/69/87                       | requireWeb2AuthSoft (enforces only when WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1) + per-IP rate limit (20/min) + 16MB json; decodes base64 image, calls 3rd-party paid API, returns PNG dataURL.                                                        |
| GET /api/web2/cutout/status                            | web2-cutout.js:47                             | Returns which engines are configured (booleans + key count). No auth.                                                                                                                                                              |
| GET /api/web2/pancake-refresh/status                   | web2-pancake-refresh.js:189                   | requireWeb2AuthSoft; lists accounts with has_creds boolean (does NOT leak identity/password). chatDb pool.                                                                                                                         |
| PUT /:accountId/credentials                            | web2-pancake-refresh.js:207                   | requireWeb2AuthSoft; encrypts password (creds.encrypt), UPDATE pancake_accounts login_identity/login_password_enc/auto_refresh. 404 if account missing.                                                                            |
| DELETE /:accountId/credentials                         | web2-pancake-refresh.js:233                   | requireWeb2AuthSoft; nulls creds + disables auto_refresh.                                                                                                                                                                          |
| POST /:accountId (refresh now)                         | web2-pancake-refresh.js:252                   | requireWeb2AuthSoft + per-IP 5/min + per-account 5-fail/15min; logs into Pancake (pure-node), upserts token into pancake_accounts, \_notify web2:pancake-accounts. realId derived from token uid.                                  |
| POST /api/web2-vieneu-registry/register                | web2-vieneu-registry.js:37                    | Optional x-vieneu-secret check (env, empty=open); validates name+http(s) url, prunes stale, caps 50, sets \_servers Map entry with ts. In-memory.                                                                                  |
| GET /list                                              | web2-vieneu-registry.js:62                    | Public (no secret) — returns all online machine URLs+notes+ageSec.                                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /unregister                                       | web2-vieneu-registry.js:77                    | Optional secret; deletes machine by name from \_servers.                                                                                                                                                                           |

**Liên kết:** web2/jt-tracking/ (frontend page consuming /api/web2-jt-tracking + SSE web2:jt-tracking), render.com/services/web2-zalo-zca.js (zca.getGroupHistory used by /scan-history), render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js (caller of autoIngestFromZalo on inbound group messages; also publishes web2:zalo:\* topics), render.com/services/web2-cutout-service.js (PhotoRoom/withoutbg/fal BiRefNet engines), render.com/services/web2-pancake-login.js (loginPancake pure-node) and web2-pancake-creds.js (AES encrypt/decrypt), render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js (requireWeb2AuthSoft used by cutout + pancake-refresh), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (notifyClients hub for web2:jt-tracking + web2:pancake-accounts), vieneu-tts/ shop-machine server + web2/video-maker (video-vieneu.js) consuming the registry, web2/photo-studio (cutout consumer), web2/video-maker (ai-script consumer), pancake_accounts table (chatDb) shared with Web 1.0 pancake token consumers

**SSE:** PUBLISH web2:jt-tracking (actions: auto-add, refresh, purge, clear, scan, bc-tag, bc-untag, add, track, approve, unapprove) via \_notify — fire-and-forget, errors swallowed with console.warn. Payload {action, code/count, ts} (no PII).; PUBLISH web2:zalo:messages and web2:zalo:thread:<thread_id> from /scan-text when synthetic paste messages are inserted (web2-jt-tracking.js:669-684).; PUBLISH web2:pancake-accounts {action:'refresh', accountId, ts} after a successful token refresh (web2-pancake-refresh.js:179).; No EventSource/subscriptions are opened by these server routes (publish-only). autoIngestFromZalo emits \_notify('auto-add') then a SECOND \_notify('refresh') after the detached background fetch completes — two SSE bursts per inbound message batch (client must debounce).

**console.log:** 16 — web2-cutout-service.js: console.warn logs withoutbg key INDEX + HTTP status on rotation (key#<idx> <status>) — index only, not the key value; low risk but noisy.; web2-pancake-refresh.js:338 cron summary logs scanned/refreshed/failed counts only (no identity/password) — acceptable.; web2-jt-tracking.js:47,115,117,118,120,260 — operational logs only, no customer PII in log text (src_message is stored in DB, not logged).; web2-ai-script.js:109 console.error logs the full error object on generate failure — may include a Gemini upstream error body but not the key.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `race` autoIngestFromZalo spawns a DETACHED async IIFE (lines 246-258) that loops over codes calling fetchJt + \_upsertTracked with no concurrency bound and no overall guard. On a busy Zalo group, many inbound messages fire many overlapping detached loops, each hammering jtexpress.vn — the throttling the /r — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:246-258` → Funnel auto-ingest fetches through a shared bounded queue (reuse /refresh CONC/sleep pacing), or just mark rows pending and let the existing /refresh path fetch them. Do not spawn unbounded detached f
- 🟡 `race` Double SSE burst per ingest: autoIngestFromZalo emits \_notify('auto-add') immediately AND \_notify('refresh') when the detached fetch finishes. Combined with /scan and SSE-driven client reload, a client reloading on every web2:jt-tracking event with insufficient debounce will refetch /list (which its — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:244,257` → Coalesce to a single \_notify after the background pass and ensure the client subscribes with a debounce window.
- 🟡 `perf` GET /list calls \_purgeApproved (a DELETE) on every read. A frequently-polled or SSE-triggered list turns a read endpoint into a write endpoint, adds write load, and means the 7-day TTL only fires when someone opens the page. Concurrent /list calls also race on the same DELETE (harmless but wasteful) — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:891,265-275` → Move purge to a scheduled job (or throttle to at most once per N minutes via an in-memory timestamp), not inside GET /list.
- 🟡 `data-loss` In /refresh, a code that times out twice is counted as fail and its row stays 'pending' with last_fetched_at UNCHANGED (because \_upsertTracked is only called on success in the chunk map). Since the 'active'/'pending' queries ORDER BY last_fetched_at ASC NULLS FIRST, a permanently-failing code keeps — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:856-872,827-848` → On fetch failure, still bump last_fetched_at (or a separate last_attempt_at) so failing codes round-robin to the back of the queue instead of monopolizing every batch.
- 🟡 `tpos-coupling` web2-pancake-refresh deliberately reads/writes pancake_accounts on chatDb (Web 1.0 pool) — server.js:1013 .init(chatDbPool). Comment says intentional (shared web1/web2 token store), but per CLAUDE.md Web 2.0 must not write Web 1.0 tables/pools. This is a documented cross-layer coupling: a bug in thi — `render.com/routes/web2-pancake-refresh.js:8,153-177; server.js:1013` → Keep this explicitly flagged; ideally access the shared token store via a single owner module rather than a Web 2.0 route writing a Web 1.0 table directly. If it must stay, add a comment-enforced inva
- 🟡 `race` vieneu-registry \_servers is unguarded shared mutable state with a check-then-act gap: \_prune() then `if(!_servers.has(name) && _servers.size>=MAX_SERVERS)` then \_servers.set. Two concurrent register calls for new names can both pass the size check and exceed MAX_SERVERS. More importantly the dedupe — `render.com/routes/web2-vieneu-registry.js:48-57` → Key the registry by a stable machine id (or name+secret), not free-text name; treat MAX_SERVERS as best-effort.
- ⚪ `logic` \_parsePasteDate returns the FIRST in-range date in a line and stops. A line whose only date is a far-future hẹn-giao (delivery-promise) date that happens to fall within now+<=2 days could be picked as src_at. The comment claims it avoids ghi-chú dates, but it only does so if an order date precedes t — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:148-170` → Anchor the date parse to the position right after the billcode rather than the first date in the whole line.
- ⚪ `perf` \_cutoutHits map is cleared WHOLESALE when size>1000 (line 40) rather than evicting stale entries, so at the moment of clearing every IP's window resets — a burst right after a clear is briefly unthrottled. — `render.com/routes/web2-cutout.js:40` → Evict only entries with no hits in the last 60s (like the pancake-refresh setInterval pattern) instead of clearing the whole map.
- ⚪ `other` ensureSchema backfill of src_at uses a correlated subquery content LIKE '%'||t.billcode||'%' over web2_zalo_messages with no index support; on a large messages table this one-time UPDATE WHERE src_at IS NULL can scan/lock heavily on the first boot after deploy. Self-terminating but potentially slow. — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:104-117` → Run it as a deferred job after boot rather than inline in ensureSchema during startup.
- ⚪ `logic` deriveStatus/parseJtEvents are fragile against J&T markup changes: parsing splits on the literal string 'result-vandon-item' and matches a bare <div>...</div> after the date. Any markup change on jtexpress.vn silently yields 0 events → status 'not_found', which the 'active' refresh query keeps retry — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:302-347` → Log/alert when a 200 response yields 0 parsed events across many codes (parser likely broke); consider excluding long-stale 'not_found' from the active retry loop.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 web2-jt-tracking router has NO auth middleware on ANY route. Every endpoint (scan, scan-text, add, track, refresh, approve, list, bc-tag, /clear) is reachable unauthenticated. GET /list returns src_message containing customer names, phone numbers and order notes harvested from Zalo — unauthenticated — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js (whole file; server.js:932 mounts with no ` → Add requireWeb2AuthSoft/requireWeb2Auth to the router or per-route. At minimum gate /list, /scan\*, /clear so customer PII and the table wipe are not public.
- 🟠 Destructive POST /clear truncates the entire web2_jt_tracking table guarded only by the static string confirm='YES-CLEAR' and no auth/admin secret. Any unauthenticated caller can wipe all tracking + customer context. — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:430-441` → Require x-admin-secret (CLEANUP_SECRET) or requireWeb2Admin in addition to the confirm string, matching other destructive Web 2.0 endpoints.
- 🟠 AI-script /generate has NO auth and NO rate limit. It is an open proxy to a paid Gemini key; an attacker can drain WEB2_GEMINI_API_KEY quota / run up cost. Unlike cutout (requireWeb2AuthSoft + IP limit), this route is wide open. — `render.com/routes/web2-ai-script.js:61 (mounted server.js:775 with no middleware` → Add requireWeb2AuthSoft + a per-IP rate limiter mirroring web2-cutout's cutoutRateLimit.
- 🟡 cutout/pancake-refresh use requireWeb2AuthSoft, which only blocks when WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE==='1'. If that env is unset on web2-api, all of these (incl. saving/decrypting Pancake credentials and draining paid cutout APIs) fall through to next() after a throttled warn. Credential write/refresh should no — `render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js:132-146; web2-pancake-refresh.js:207/233/252;` → Use requireWeb2Auth (hard) for credential write/refresh; keep soft only for read-only status. Verify WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 is actually set on web2-api.
- 🟡 vieneu-registry GET /list is public and returns shop machine tunnel URLs. If VIENEU_REGISTRY_SECRET is empty (the documented default), register/unregister are also open: anyone can register a malicious url so the video-maker page points operators at an attacker-controlled TTS server (could harvest u — `render.com/routes/web2-vieneu-registry.js:21-29,37-59,62-74` → Require the secret for register/unregister by default (fail closed); consider requireWeb2AuthSoft on /list, or document that the secret MUST be set in production.
- 🟡 \_clientIp / cutoutRateLimit trust the LAST hop of x-forwarded-for when cf-connecting-ip is absent. On the real deploy CF sets cf-connecting-ip, but if hit directly or via a proxy appending client-controlled XFF, the limiter keys on a spoofable value, defeating brute-force/quota protection on both cu — `render.com/routes/web2-cutout.js:25-32; web2-pancake-refresh.js:66-78` → Prefer req.socket.remoteAddress when not behind the known CF proxy, or pin to cf-connecting-ip and reject when missing in production.
- ⚪ Gemini API key is passed in the URL query string (?key=K). If any intermediate logs request URLs, the key can leak. Header-based auth (x-goog-api-key) is safer. — `render.com/routes/web2-ai-script.js:74` → Send the key via the 'x-goog-api-key' header instead of the URL.
- ⚪ bc-tag stores an unvalidated raw phone string as PRIMARY KEY (no /^0\d{9}$/ check), unlike the rest of Web 2.0 which enforces 10-digit VN phones. Garbage/fb_id strings can pollute web2_jt_bc_tags. — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:704-725` → Validate phone with the shared 10-digit regex before insert.
- ℹ️ All five routers use parameterized queries; search ILIKE clauses in /list build the SQL fragment from $-placeholders only (no string concatenation of user input). No SQL injection found. — `render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js:902-934` → No change; noted as a positive.
- ℹ️ SSRF surface is bounded: fetchJt sanitizes billcode to digits and uses a fixed JT_BASE host; cutout/ai-script call fixed upstream hosts; only birefnetCutout fetches a response URL (j.image.url) but from the trusted fal.ai host, not user input. — `render.com/services/web2-cutout-service.js:118-125` → Optionally allowlist the fal response URL host before the second fetch as defense-in-depth.

**Ghi chú:** Pool usage is correct for the Web 2.0 routers: web2-jt-tracking uses req.app.locals.web2Db||chatDb (web2Db); ensureSchema seeded with web2Pool||chatDbPool. web2-pancake-refresh intentionally uses chatDbPool (shared token store) — flagged as coupling, not a pool mixup.; Timezone handling in web2-jt-tracking is correct: \_toEpoch appends +07:00 to J&T's naive 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' (matches the GMT+7 rule and the Pancake missing-Z pattern). \_parsePasteDate uses Date.UTC(y,mo-1,d,5,0,0) = 12:00 GMT+7, also correct.; SQL is fully parameterized across all files; search ILIKE built only from $-placeholders. No injection found.; No hardcoded secrets — all keys read from env (WEB2_GEMINI_API_KEY, FAL_KEY, PHOTOROOM_API_KEY, WITHOUTBG_API_KEY(S), VIENEU_REGISTRY_SECRET, PANCAKE_CREDS_KEY).; Rate-limit cleanup interval in pancake-refresh is .unref()'d (good). vieneu-registry and cutout use lazy prune/wholesale-clear instead of intervals (acceptable, no leak).; ensureSchema/ensureColumns are idempotent (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS / CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) — safe across Render restarts.; Highest-priority fixes: (1) add auth to web2-jt-tracking router + admin-gate /clear; (2) auth + rate-limit web2-ai-script/generate; (3) bound/queue autoIngestFromZalo's detached fetch loop; (4) stop calling the purge DELETE inside GET /list.

### routes:users-auth (render.com/routes/web2-users.js)

Web 2.0 user-account + auth core, fully separate from Web 1.0 (tables web2_users, web2_user_sessions in web2Db; bcrypt passwords; opaque 256-bit hex session tokens, not JWT). Provides CRUD on users (admin-gated), login (issues token), /me (token→user), logout, password change, permission editing, plus a static permission registry (WEB2_PAGES/ACTION_LABELS/ROLE_DEFAULTS) consumed by the frontend matrix editor. Hardens with in-memory per-IP login rate-limit, atomic last-active-admin guards, token-hash-at-rest (C7: SHA-256 stored, raw token only to client) with zero-lockout fallback for legacy plaintext sessions and a 42703 boot-window fallback. Broadcasts web2:users SSE after each mutation. Critically: the role/permission model it defines is returned to clients but is NOT enforced per-action on any server route — only role-level (requireWeb2Admin) and login-level (requireWeb2Auth) gating exists.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** module require: express, bcryptjs, crypto, ../middleware/web2-auth (requireWeb2Auth, requireWeb2Admin, hashWeb2Token); Boot constants: ROLES, TOKEN_TTL_MS=7d, LOGIN_MAX_FAILS=8, LOGIN_WINDOW_MS=15min; setInterval cleanup of \_loginFails every 15min, .unref() so it doesn't keep process alive (line 94-99); WEB2_PAGES / ACTION_LABELS / ROLE_DEFAULTS permission registry built at module load; ensureTables(pool) runs lazily on first request (gated by tablesReady flag): CREATE web2_users + web2_user_sessions, ADD COLUMN token_hash, indexes, two idempotent permission-migration UPDATEs (tpos-pancake->live-chat, drop loadTpos), and seeds default admin (username admin / password admin@@) when table empty (line 394-405); server.js:793 mounts router at /api/web2-users; server.js:1139-1142 wires initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients)

| Control                        | Handler                                | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| GET /pages                     | router.get('/pages') web2-users.js:455 | Returns WEB2_PAGES, ROLES, ACTION_LABELS. NO auth gate — exposes full internal page/action map to anonymous callers.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| GET /role-defaults/:role       | web2-users.js:464                      | Returns computed default permission matrix for a role. NO auth gate.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| GET /list                      | requireWeb2Auth web2-users.js:471      | Any logged-in user (incl viewer/staff) lists ALL users with username/email/phone/role/permissions. limit clamped <=500, offset>=0. Gate is login-only, NOT admin — a viewer can enumerate all accounts + PII.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| GET /:id(\d+)                  | requireWeb2Auth web2-users.js:496      | Any logged-in user reads any user record by id (PII). Login-only gate, not admin/self.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| POST /                         | requireWeb2Admin web2-users.js:513     | Create user. Validates username (regex 3-40 [a-z0-9_.-]), password (>=8), role, displayName required; bcrypt cost 10; parameterized INSERT; 23505->409. \_notify('create'). email/phone NOT format-validated.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| PATCH /:id(\d+)                | requireWeb2Admin web2-users.js:558     | Update displayName/email/phone/role/isActive/note (NOT password). Parameterized dynamic SET. Last-admin guard folded into single atomic UPDATE w/ EXISTS subquery; rowCount=0 re-checked to distinguish 404 vs guard-block. \_notify('update').                                                                                                                                                                         |
| PUT /:id(\d+)/permissions      | requireWeb2Admin web2-users.js:633     | Set custom permissions JSON or null (revert to role defaults). Validates slug in known set and each action in page's allowed set. Parameterized UPDATE. \_notify('update-permissions').                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /:id(\d+)/password        | requireWeb2Admin web2-users.js:677     | Admin-only password reset for any user. bcrypt hash, parameterized UPDATE, then DELETE all that user's sessions (forced logout). \_notify('change-password'). NO self-service password change for non-admins.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| DELETE /:id(\d+)               | requireWeb2Admin web2-users.js:701     | Soft delete (is_active=FALSE) with atomic last-active-admin guard; deletes user's sessions; \_notify('deactivate'). No hard delete.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| POST /login                    | web2-users.js:737                      | Per-IP rate-limit (429 if blocked); lowercases username; SELECT active user; bcrypt.compare; on fail \_loginRecordFail; on success issues crypto.randomBytes(32) hex token, stores SHA-256 hash in BOTH token(PK) and token_hash, updates last_login_at, opportunistic DELETE expired sessions, returns raw token + mapRow(user). Generic 'Sai thông tin' for both unknown-user and bad-password (no user enumeration). |
| GET /me?token= or x-web2-token | web2-users.js:797                      | Resolve token->user. Accepts token from query OR header. Matches token_hash=hash(t) OR (token_hash IS NULL AND token=t); 42703 fallback to plaintext-only. Returns mapRow(user). Token accepted in URL query string (leakage surface).                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| POST /logout                   | web2-users.js:833                      | DELETE session WHERE token_hash=hash(t) OR token=t. No auth required (idempotent). Returns success even with no token.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| \_notify(action,id)            | web2-users.js:30                       | Broadcasts {action,id,ts} on web2:users via injected notifyClients; swallows errors with console.warn. No PII in payload (id only).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |

**Liên kết:** render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js (requireWeb2Auth/requireWeb2Admin/hashWeb2Token — shares token-hash + zero-lockout logic verbatim), render.com/server.js:793 (mount) + :1139-1142 (SSE notifier wiring), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (notifyClients hub for web2:users), web2/shared/web2-auth.js (frontend client: login storage, verify() calls /me?token=, can(slug,action) client-side gate, authHeaders), web2/users/js/users-app.js (admin UI consuming list/create/patch/password/permissions), cloudflare-worker/modules/handlers/proxy-handler.js:552 handleCustomer360Proxy (forwards /api/web2-users/\* to Render via WEB2_GENERIC route in config/routes.js:391)

**SSE:** PUBLISHES web2:users with eventType 'update' after create/update/update-permissions/change-password/deactivate. Payload {action,id,ts} — no PII. No debounce on publish side (each mutation fires once). No subscription here (route, not page). \_notify is best-effort (try/catch warn) and only fires if initializeNotifiers was called (server.js:1141).

**console.log:** 12 — web2-users.js:404 console.log seeds-admin message includes username 'admin' (not the password) — low risk, but the default password admin@@ is hardcoded at line 396; All other console.\* (35,491,507,552,625,671,695,731,791,827,846) log only e.message — no tokens/passwords/PII leaked. NOTE: token leakage to logs happens upstream in the WORKER (proxy-handler.js:562 logs targetUrl incl ?token= query), not in this file.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟡 `race` ensureTables single-flight uses a module-level boolean tablesReady with no in-flight promise lock. On cold start, two concurrent first requests both see tablesReady=false and run the full DDL + permission-migration UPDATE + empty-table admin seed concurrently. CREATE/ALTER ... IF NOT EXISTS are idem — `web2-users.js:339-407 (tablesReady flag + seed 394-405)` → Cache an in-flight promise (let \_ensurePromise; return \_ensurePromise ||= doEnsure(pool)) and use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (username) DO NOTHING for the seed so a concurrent seed is a no-op instead of a
- 🟡 `logic` effectivePermissions silently falls back to ROLE_DEFAULTS.viewer for any unknown role (ROLE_DEFAULTS[role] || ROLE_DEFAULTS.viewer). validateRole rejects unknown roles on write, so a bad role only arises via direct DB edit/migration; the viewer fallback is the SAFE direction. The latent risk is the — `web2-users.js:326, 597-607, 714-715` → Normalize role on read (lower/trim) or add a CHECK constraint role IN (...) on web2_users so invalid roles cannot exist.
- ⚪ `data-loss` Opportunistic expired-session cleanup on login is fire-and-forget with errors fully swallowed (.catch(()=>{})). Harmless for correctness but masks DB issues and lets web2_user_sessions grow unbounded if it persistently fails. — `web2-users.js:783` → Log the catch at warn level, or move expiry cleanup to the existing setInterval / a cron.
- ⚪ `race` Login rate-limit state \_loginFails is per-process in-memory. On Render with multiple instances / after restart, the 8-fail budget resets per instance and per deploy, weakening brute-force protection. JS single-thread means no true data race, but the store is not shared. — `web2-users.js:47-99` → Acceptable now (bcrypt cost 10 + generic errors mitigate); if hardening, back the counter with web2Db or a shared store.
- ⚪ `logic` \_loginClientIp trusts the LAST element of X-Forwarded-For as proxy-written IP, preferring cf-connecting-ip. If the Render origin is reachable directly (n2store-fallback.onrender.com, used elsewhere) without going through Cloudflare, an attacker can spoof cf-connecting-ip / craft XFF to get a fresh r — `web2-users.js:51-66` → Only trust cf-connecting-ip when a shared CF secret header is present, or enforce origin reachable only via the worker; otherwise derive IP from a configured trust-proxy hop count.
- ℹ️ `logic` Password endpoint is admin-only; there is no endpoint for a user to change their OWN password. Means a compromised admin is the only rotation path and users cannot self-rotate. Confirm intended. — `web2-users.js:677 (requireWeb2Admin)` → If self-service is desired, add POST /me/password gated by requireWeb2Auth that verifies the current password before update.
- ℹ️ `web1-coupling` Pool selection falls back to chatDb (req.app.locals.web2Db || req.app.locals.chatDb). web2_users/web2_user_sessions live in web2Db; if web2Db is ever undefined this would silently create/seed the admin user and sessions in the Web 1.0 chatDb pool — a cross-layer write. Repo-wide convention so likely — `web2-users.js:472,497,514,559,634,678,702,738,798,834` → Fail fast (500 'web2Db unavailable') instead of falling back to chatDb for these auth tables, to guarantee no auth data is written to the Web 1.0 pool.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 Permission model is advisory only — never enforced server-side. ROLE_DEFAULTS/effectivePermissions/permissions are computed and returned to the client, and the frontend gates UI via Web2Auth.can(slug,action), but NO server route enforces per-action permissions. Other /api/web2\* routes rely on requir — `web2-users.js:325-336 (effectivePermissions), 471/496 (login-only gates) + middl` → Add a requireWeb2Permission(slug,action) middleware that reads effectivePermissions(role,custom) from req.web2User and 403s when the action is absent; apply to all mutating web2 routes. Treat client-s
- 🟡 GET /list and GET /:id are gated by requireWeb2Auth (any logged-in user), not requireWeb2Admin/self. Any viewer/staff can enumerate ALL user accounts including username/email/phone (PII) and each user's resolved permissions. The header comment claims 'gate CỨNG' but it is login-level, not admin. — `web2-users.js:471, 496` → Use requireWeb2Admin for /list and /:id, or restrict /:id to self-or-admin; strip email/phone for non-admin callers.
- 🟡 Session token accepted via ?token= URL query (extractToken middleware + frontend web2-auth.js:102 calls /me?token=<raw>). Query strings land in access logs, Referer headers, browser history, and the Cloudflare worker logs the full target URL incl ?token= (proxy-handler.js:562). Raw bearer token leak — `web2-users.js:802 (req.query.token), middleware web2-auth.js:34-41, web2/shared/` → Make /me require x-web2-token header only; update frontend verify() to send the header; stop logging url.search in the worker proxy (or redact token param).
- 🟡 GET /pages and GET /role-defaults/:role have NO auth gate, exposing the full internal page/action map and role permission templates to anonymous callers. — `web2-users.js:455, 464` → Gate behind requireWeb2Auth (these feed the admin permission editor; no need to be public).
- ⚪ Hardcoded default admin credentials admin/admin@@ seeded on first boot. If never rotated it is a known-credential foothold; the same string appears in test docs. — `web2-users.js:396, 402` → Seed with a random password printed once to logs (or require env on first boot) and force change-on-first-login.
- ⚪ email/phone are not format-validated on create/update; arbitrary strings stored. Not injection (parameterized) but weak boundary validation. — `web2-users.js:521,536,572-579` → Validate email/phone shape (phone /^0\d{9}$/ per project convention) before persist.
- ℹ️ Token verification uses SQL equality on the SHA-256 hash (not constant-time JS compare). Acceptable: compared value is a hash of a 256-bit random token, so timing leakage of the indexed lookup does not enable practical forgery. — `web2-users.js:807-812, middleware web2-auth.js:55-61` → No action required; noted for completeness.

**Ghi chú:** Token-at-rest (C7) is implemented correctly: login stores SHA-256(token) in both token(PK) and token_hash; raw token returned to client only; /me and middleware verify via hash with a zero-lockout legacy-plaintext fallback and a 42703 boot-window fallback. Logic is duplicated in middleware/web2-auth.js and kept in sync.; Last-active-admin guards in PATCH and DELETE are correctly atomic (single UPDATE with EXISTS subquery); the previous COUNT-then-UPDATE TOCTOU is fixed and rowCount=0 is disambiguated against 404.; All SQL is parameterized; no string-built queries; no SQL injection found.; No innerHTML/XSS surface (pure JSON API).; Timezone: timestamps stored as BIGINT epoch ms (Date.now()) and returned as numbers — no naive-string GMT+7 bug in this file.; This route file is correctly admin-gated for its own mutations; the systemic gap is that the permission MODEL it publishes is not enforced on the OTHER web2 routes — that should be audited at the middleware/route layer project-wide.

### routes:wallet-money

Three Web 2.0 money-path route modules on pool web2Db. (1) web2-supplier-wallet.js: append-only supplier ledger (web2_supplier_ledger UNIQUE tx_id) + meta (web2_supplier_meta.returned_row_ids JSONB) — POST /tx writes one idempotent payment|return tx inside a transaction, locking the meta row FOR UPDATE to serialize returned_row_ids accumulation with a server-side over-refund cap; totals (balance/totalPurchased) are NOT stored, recomputed client-side from the ledger. (2) web2-returns.js: goods-return tickets that credit/debit the customer wallet (processDeposit/processWithdraw) and adjust web2_products stock/return_qty atomically via withTransaction. (3) web2-payment-signals.js: "customer said they transferred" review queue; POST /:id/approve links a SePay tx and credits the wallet via balance-history.linkTransaction inside a single transaction. Ledger is append-only and totals are recomputed not stored — that part is sound. The dominant defect is an AUTH GAP: web2-returns money endpoints have no auth middleware at all.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** SERVICE / Express routers, no per-page load. server.js requires + mounts: app.use('/api/web2-returns', web2ReturnsRoutes) [server.js:765] — NO middleware; app.use('/api/web2/payment-signals', web2PaymentSignalsRoutes) [server.js:861]; app.use('/api/web2-supplier-wallet', web2SupplierWalletRoutes) [server.js:889].; Boot wiring: web2PaymentSignalsRoutes.ensureSchema() called once at boot [server.js:858-860] (detector.ensureSchema); supplierWallet/returns/paymentSignals .initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients) wired to the Web 2.0 SSE hub [server.js:888, 1082, 1122].; Per-request lazy DDL: ensureTables(pool) guarded by a module-level WeakSet \_ensuredPools so CREATE TABLE/ALTER/CREATE INDEX run once per pool object (web2-supplier-wallet.js:50, web2-returns.js:140). web2-returns also creates partial UNIQUE INDEX uq_web2_returns_knh_active in a try/catch (web2-returns.js:197-205).; Lazy requires inside handlers: balance-history.linkTransaction (web2-payment-signals.js:442), web2-msg-send-worker (web2-payment-signals.js:92), fast-sale-orders.applyWalletToUnpaidPbhs (balance-history.js:127) — circular-import avoidance.; supplier-wallet ensureTables also exported as router.ensureLedgerTables and called from purchase-refund.js cold-start (purchase-refund.js:353,525,674).

| Control                                                                      | Handler                                                      | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| POST /api/web2-supplier-wallet/tx (requireWeb2AuthSoft)                      | router.post('/tx') web2-supplier-wallet.js:189               | Validates supplier/type∈{payment,return}/amount>0. BEGIN on dedicated client; INSERT meta ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING then SELECT returned_row_ids FOR UPDATE (serializes). payment without moveName → nextval('web2_supplier_move_seq') → PAY/<yr>/<seq>. INSERT ledger ON CONFLICT(tx_id) DO NOTHING RETURNING — if no row, tx_id already exists → returns {alreadyProcessed:true} (idempotent). For return with rowReturns: ACCUMULATES qty/amount per rowId (delta semantics) under the lock, and rejects (400) if newQty>ordered when client supplies ordered. UPDATE meta.returned_row_ids. COMMIT, \_notify('tx',supplier) → SSE web2:supplier-wallet. |
| POST /api/web2-supplier-wallet/suppliers (requireWeb2AuthSoft)               | router.post('/suppliers') web2-supplier-wallet.js:325        | Single-statement upsert INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(supplier) DO UPDATE (atomic, no RMW). \_notify('supplier').                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| POST /api/web2-supplier-wallet/import (requireWeb2AuthSoft)                  | router.post('/import') web2-supplier-wallet.js:359           | One-time Firestore migration; server-guarded: ROLLBACK+skip if ledger or meta non-empty. Bulk INSERT meta+ledger ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. COMMIT, \_notify('import').                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| DELETE /api/web2-supplier-wallet/supplier/:name (x-admin-secret)             | router.delete('/supplier/:name') web2-supplier-wallet.js:448 | Fail-closed admin gate (CLEANUP_SECRET). DELETE ledger + meta for supplier (two non-transactional statements). \_notify('supplier-deleted').                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| GET /api/web2-supplier-wallet/state                                          | router.get('/state') web2-supplier-wallet.js:100             | Reads all meta+ledger, derives paidAmount/returnedAmount per wallet in-memory (recomputed, not stored). No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| POST /api/web2-returns (NO auth middleware)                                  | router.post('/') web2-returns.js:496                         | van_de_shipper branch: optional processWithdraw (trừ ví) BEFORE insert, then retry-on-23505 \_genCode insert. Main branch: resolves items+walletCredit; withTransaction: pre-check dup active khong_nhan_hang (409), lock source PBHs FOR UPDATE reading FRESH wallet_deducted as credit, \_applyStock(+1), processDeposit(client) into customer wallet, INSERT ticket, zero-out PBH wallet_deducted + stock_restored=TRUE. \_notifyWallet + \_notify('create'). MONEY OP WITH NO AUTH GATE.                                                                                                                                                             |
| POST /api/web2-returns/:code/approve (NO auth)                               | router.post('/:code/approve') web2-returns.js:813            | withTransaction: SELECT FOR UPDATE + state/stock_status guards; move return_qty→stock per item; set stock_status='approved'. \_notify('approve'). No wallet change. No auth gate.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| DELETE /api/web2-returns/:code (NO auth)                                     | router.delete('/:code') web2-returns.js:908                  | withTransaction: SELECT FOR UPDATE; rollback stock (pending→return_qty, else stock); reverse wallet (credited>0 → processWithdraw, 409 if insufficient; credited<0 → processDeposit); set status='cancelled' guarded by AND status='active'; for khong_nhan_hang restore source PBH stock_restored=FALSE + lump wallet_deducted back onto FIRST PBH only. \_notifyWallet+\_notify. No auth gate.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| POST /api/web2-returns/:code/mark-consumed (NO auth)                         | router.post('/:code/mark-consumed') web2-returns.js:885      | Single UPDATE guarded by bill_status='queued' RETURNING; 404 if not queued. \_notify('consumed'). No auth gate.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| POST /api/web2/payment-signals/:id/approve (requireWeb2AuthSoft)             | router.post('/:id/approve') web2-payment-signals.js:384      | Requires phone. BEGIN; SELECT FOR UPDATE guard: confirmed→idempotent COMMIT; any non-pending→409 ROLLBACK. If txId: balance-history.linkTransaction(client,...) credits wallet (or reconciles if alreadyProcessed for same last-9 phone, else 400). UPDATE signal status='confirmed' guarded by status='pending'. \_appendHistory. COMMIT. \_notify('approve') + web2:balance-history SSE. Best-effort sendCkReply via Pancake worker (fire-and-forget).                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| POST /api/web2/payment-signals/:id/confirm \| /dismiss (requireWeb2AuthSoft) | web2-payment-signals.js:268 / :310                           | BEGIN; FOR UPDATE; status='pending' guard; UPDATE + \_appendHistory; COMMIT; \_notify. Idempotent COMMIT when already handled.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| POST /api/web2/payment-signals/:id/link-order (requireWeb2AuthSoft)          | router.post('/:id/link-order') web2-payment-signals.js:348   | NO transaction/lock: UPDATE matched_order_type/code RETURNING, then \_appendHistory(pool,...) as a SEPARATE statement (history append not row-locked → lost-update window).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| GET payment-signals / , /stats, /:id                                         | web2-payment-signals.js:147 / :223 / :240                    | Read + LEFT JOIN enrich native_orders/fast_sale_orders; parameterized. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |

**Liên kết:** render route render.com/services/web2-wallet-service.js (processDeposit/processWithdraw — web2_customer_wallets + web2_wallet_transactions, FOR UPDATE), render route render.com/db/with-transaction.js (single-client BEGIN/COMMIT + \_afterCommit SSE hook queue), render route render.com/routes/v2/web2-balance-history.js linkTransaction (shared credit path, FOR UPDATE on web2_balance_history), render route render.com/routes/purchase-refund.js (second writer to web2_supplier_ledger + returned_row_ids; uses router.ensureLedgerTables), render route render.com/routes/fast-sale-orders.js (applyWalletToUnpaidPbhs side-effect; fast_sale_orders is a Web 2.0 legacy-named table), render table native_orders / fast_sale_orders (Web 2.0 legacy-named, web2Db) read in \_resolveSourceOrder + payment-signals enrich, render route render.com/services/web2-msg-send-worker.js (sendSingleMessage Pancake auto-reply), render service render.com/services/web2-payment-signal-detector.js (ensureSchema for web2_payment_signals), web2 pages: web2/supplier-wallet/, web2/supplier-debt/, web2/purchase-refund/, web2/payment-confirm/ (web2-ck-review), web2/returns + native-orders consumers

**SSE:** PUBLISH web2:supplier-wallet ({action,supplier,ts}) after /tx, /suppliers, /import, /supplier-delete (web2-supplier-wallet.js:32-43). Also published by purchase-refund via app.locals.web2RealtimeSseNotify.; PUBLISH web2:returns + web2:products (action='return-<x>') after create/approve/cancel/consumed (web2-returns.js:38-55).; PUBLISH web2:wallet:<phone> ({action:'return-credit'|... ,phone,ts}) on wallet-affecting return create/cancel (web2-returns.js:56-67). NOTE payload carries raw phone (PII) — see security.; PUBLISH web2:payment-signals ({action,id,ts}) after confirm/dismiss/link/approve (web2-payment-signals.js:36-43), plus web2:balance-history on approve-with-txId (web2-payment-signals.js:501-511).; Wallet-service emits web2:wallet:update + web2:wallet:<phone> via emitAfterCommit registered on client.\_afterCommit so SSE fires only AFTER COMMIT (avoids stale-read) — good. Routes are server-side; no EventSource subscriptions or unmount cleanup here. No debounce on the publish side (debounce is on the client subscribers).

**console.log:** 15 — No PII/token leaks: all 15 console.\* log only e.message or static prefixes. web2-supplier-wallet.js:41,151,313,429(log),436; web2-returns.js:53,65,204,804,876,1054; web2-payment-signals.js:41,217,523,530. The single console.log (supplier-wallet.js:429) prints only aggregate counts (NCC/transaction counts), not customer data.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `logic` Server over-refund cap is conditional on the client sending `ordered`. The accumulation (newQty = prev.qty + delta) is correct and lock-protected, but the guard `if (Number.isFinite(ordered) && ordered>0 && newQty>ordered)` is a no-op whenever the client omits `ordered`. A direct API caller (or a cl — `render.com/routes/web2-supplier-wallet.js:283-290; render.com/routes/purchase-re` → Make ordered authoritative: look up the purchased qty server-side (from so-order/web2_records under the same FOR UPDATE) and always enforce newQty ≤ purchasedQty, treating a missing ordered as 'must f
- 🟠 `data-loss` DELETE /:code restore of source-PBH wallet_deducted on khong_nhan_hang cancel writes the entire credited lump onto only the FIRST locked PBH (lockQ.rows[0].id) when the create path zeroed wallet_deducted across MULTIPLE PBHs. If the original credit spanned N PBHs, later cancel of those PBHs refunds — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:1032-1040 vs create zero-out web2-returns.js:7` → Persist the per-PBH wallet_deducted snapshot at create time (e.g. in the return ticket items/ref) and restore each PBH's original value on cancel, instead of lumping onto rows[0].
- 🟡 `race` native-order khong_nhan_hang credit uses SUM(wallet_deducted) over PBHs with state<>'cancel'. The create path locks rows with `state <> 'cancel' ... FOR UPDATE` but a concurrent PBH cancel (which also restores wallet + restock) can run on a DIFFERENT PBH row not covered, or interleave between \_resol — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:691-711; balance-history.js:125-133` → Verify PBH-cancel and applyWalletToUnpaidPbhs both lock the wallet row and key wallet ops on idempotent reference_id; add a test interleaving return-create + CK-approve for one phone.
- 🟡 `race` payment-signals POST /:id/link-order has no transaction and no FOR UPDATE: it UPDATEs matched_order_type/code, then calls \_appendHistory(pool,...) as a separate statement using `history || $2` read-modify-write. Two concurrent link-order/confirm calls on the same signal can lose a history entry (the — `render.com/routes/web2-payment-signals.js:348-376` → Wrap link-order in pool.connect()+BEGIN, SELECT ... FOR UPDATE, UPDATE, \_appendHistory(client,...), COMMIT — same pattern as confirm().
- 🟡 `logic` supplier-wallet /tx records a 'return' ledger row (increasing returnedAmount in /state) even when rowReturns is absent (returned_row_ids NOT updated). So a return tx can move money/returnedAmount without any per-row accounting, and the over-refund cap (which only runs inside the rowReturns loop) is — `render.com/routes/web2-supplier-wallet.js:264-307` → For type='return' require rowReturns (or at least require it when amount affects refund accounting) and run the ordered cap before inserting the ledger row; reject returns lacking per-row data instead
- ⚪ `logic` DELETE /supplier/:name runs two separate DELETE statements (ledger then meta) outside a transaction. A crash between them leaves ledger deleted but meta orphaned (or vice versa). Admin-only and destructive but non-atomic. — `render.com/routes/web2-supplier-wallet.js:459-460` → Wrap both DELETEs in withTransaction(pool, ...).
- ⚪ `logic` confirm/dismiss/approve call \_notify(...) even on the idempotent already-handled COMMIT branch, broadcasting an SSE 'confirm'/'dismiss'/'approve' for a no-op. Harmless but produces spurious SSE traffic / UI flicker on double-submit. — `render.com/routes/web2-payment-signals.js:286-288,327-329,421-423` → Skip \_notify on the alreadyHandled/alreadyApproved branch (or only notify when a row actually changed).
- ⚪ `race` \_genCode for returns (TV-YYYYMMDD-XXXX via MAX+1) is computed OUTSIDE the transaction and is not atomic; relies on retry-on-23505 (RETRY_MAX=5). Under high concurrency (>5 simultaneous same-day creates) the retry budget can be exhausted → 500. Functional but fragile; same pattern in the van_de_shipp — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:213-231,659-663,549-598` → Use a per-day Postgres sequence or generate the code inside the transaction with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on a counter, eliminating the retry loop.
- ℹ️ `tpos-coupling` No TPOS API coupling and no Web 1.0 pool/table coupling detected: getPool returns web2Db||chatDb consistently; native_orders/fast_sale_orders/web2_balance_history/web2_customer_wallets are all Web 2.0 (web2Db) tables; SSE uses the web2: hub. processDeposit/processWithdraw header comment mentions web — `render.com/routes/web2-supplier-wallet.js:45-47; web2-returns.js:209-211; web2-p` → None — recorded as a clean check.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 web2-returns.js money endpoints have NO auth middleware. POST /api/web2-returns (credits/debits the customer wallet via processDeposit/processWithdraw), DELETE /:code (reverses wallet), POST /:code/approve (moves stock), POST /:code/mark-consumed are all mounted without requireWeb2AuthSoft (server.j — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:496,813,885,908 + server.js:765` → Add requireWeb2AuthSoft (or requireWeb2Auth) to the router or to each mutating route, exactly like web2-supplier-wallet.js:189/325 and web2-payment-signals.js:268/310/384. Prefer router.use(requireWeb
- 🟠 thu_ve_1_phan return credits the wallet with a fully client-supplied amount: walletCredit = totalAmount = Σ(item.price × item.quantity) from req.body.items, with no cross-check against any source order or product price. Combined with the missing auth gate, this is direct unauthenticated balance crea — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:635-647,724-737` → Validate item prices server-side against web2_products / the cited source order, cap walletCredit, and require auth. At minimum reject when sourceOrderCode is absent for thu_ve_1_phan or verify price
- ⚪ SSE payload on web2:wallet:<phone> embeds the raw customer phone in both the topic and the data ({phone}). Topic-as-identifier is by design, but the data field duplicating PII contradicts the project rule 'KHÔNG broadcast PII trong payload'. — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:56-67` → Drop phone from the data object (subscribers already key on the topic); send {action,ts} only.
- ℹ️ \_user() trusts client-supplied userId/userName from body/headers for returns audit history (no token-derived identity), so the history actor is spoofable. — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:128-134` → After adding auth, derive actor from req.web2User like payment-signals \_user() (web2-payment-signals.js:67) instead of body/header.
- ℹ️ All SQL is parameterized (incl. dynamic WHERE building in list/payment-signals). No string-concatenated user values into SQL, no innerHTML (server side). Admin DELETE uses fail-closed x-admin-secret. No hardcoded secrets. Good baseline. — `render.com/routes/web2-payment-signals.js:158-202; web2-supplier-wallet.js:448-4` → No action; recorded as positive.

**Ghi chú:** Append-only + recomputed-totals invariant holds for supplier wallet: ledger rows are insert-only with UNIQUE tx_id idempotency; /state recomputes paidAmount/returnedAmount in-memory and balance/totalPurchased are explicitly NOT stored server-side (web2-supplier-wallet.js:19,109-134). purchase-refund 'reverses' a ledger entry by DELETE of tx-refund-<code> + subtracting returned_row_ids inside a transaction (purchase-refund.js:178-211) — a documented exception to strict append-only, but atomic and idempotent.; Transaction discipline is generally strong: confirm/dismiss/approve (payment-signals), create/approve/delete (returns), and /tx (supplier) all use a single dedicated client with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE before mutation; wallet credits run inside the SAME client via runWithTx(client) so they are atomic with the surrounding ticket/PBH writes. SSE emits are deferred to client.\_afterCommit so subscribers never read pre-commit state.; Biggest exposure is authorization, not concurrency: the returns router is the only money module here with no auth gate. Fixing server.js:765 to attach requireWeb2AuthSoft brings it in line with the other two modules and closes the unauthenticated balance-mint path.; Rounding: amounts are NUMERIC(15,2)/(14,2) in DB and Number() in JS; totalAmount uses Σ price×qty with no explicit rounding — fine for integer VND but could accumulate float artifacts if non-integer prices ever appear. Low risk given VND.; Timezone: \_genCode and PAY/<year> use server-local Date (Render TZ=Asia/Saigon +7) which matches the GMT+7 display requirement; no naive-UTC parsing of Pancake timestamps in these files. No timezone bug found.

### routes:zalo (render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js)

Single-source Express router (mounted at /api/web2-zalo) for all Web 2.0 Zalo functionality: personal accounts via zca-js (QR/cookie login, 2-way chat, lookup) and Official Account (ZNS/CS messages). Persists incoming/outgoing messages, reactions, recalls, seen, and conversations into web2Db tables web2*zalo*\* and broadcasts realtime over the web2 SSE hub (topics web2:zalo:messages / web2:zalo:accounts / web2:zalo:thread:<id>). It owns an in-memory tracked-groups allowlist (firehose filter), a media self-host store, an admin reset endpoint, a 7-day retention job, and boot-time session restore. Pool is correctly req.app.locals.web2Db || chatDb (no Web 1.0 coupling, no TPOS). The file is the data + control plane behind the web2/zalo page and the shared Web2Zalo helper.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** module load: zca.configure({...}) wires persistence callbacks onMessage/onStatus/persistSession/onReaction/onUndo/onSeen/onTyping/onConnected (lines 102-126) — every inbound zca event funnels into DB persisters + SSE; server.js boot: web2ZaloRoutes.ensureSchema(web2Pool||chatDbPool) → sets module-global \_pool, runs ensureWeb2ZaloSchema, \_loadTracked() (lines 2004-2013); ensureSchema starts a setInterval(\_loadTracked, 60s) refresh timer (unref'd) for the tracked-group allowlist cache (lines 2009-2012); server.js boot: web2ZaloRoutes.restoreSessions() → re-login every personal account with a stored session via zca.restoreAll (lines 2016-2026); server.js: web2ZaloRoutes.initializeNotifiers(web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients) wires SSE (line 906) — correct web2 hub; server.js cron (if !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS): runZaloRetention(7) at boot+60s then every 6h (server.js 914-920)

| Control                                                      | Handler                        | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| GET /status                                                  | router.get('/status') (465)    | List active accounts merged with live zca.statusAll(); returns \_safeAccount (strips session/token but exposes zalo_uid, displayName, phone-less). No auth.                                                                                                     |
| GET /accounts                                                | router.get('/accounts') (488)  | List ALL accounts (incl inactive) via \_safeAccount. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| POST /accounts                                               | router.post('/accounts') (505) | Create personal shell account, key=zca\_<uuid>; SSE web2:zalo:accounts create. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| POST /accounts/:key/login-qr                                 | (524)                          | zca.startQrLogin → UPDATE status. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| GET /accounts/:key/qr                                        | (544)                          | Returns QR image/scanned-name/info from in-memory session. No auth — leaks QR + scanned display name to any caller.                                                                                                                                             |
| POST /accounts/:key/reconnect                                | (549)                          | loginWithCredentials(stored session) WITHOUT expectedUid guard.
