No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| POST /accounts/:key/login-cookie                             | (570)                          | Cookie 1-click login; passes expectedUid: rows[0].zalo_uid as wrong-identity guard; persists session; 400 on WRONG_ACCOUNT. No auth — any caller can attach an arbitrary Zalo cookie session to a slot whose zalo_uid is still NULL (first login, guard inert). |
| POST /accounts/:key/disconnect                               | (604)                          | zca.disconnect + UPDATE status. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| POST /accounts/:key/primary                                  | (619)                          | Atomic UPDATE is_primary=(account_key=$1) over whole table → single primary. No auth — anyone can redirect which TK sends 1-1 customer messages.                                                                                                                |
| DELETE /accounts/:key                                        | (645)                          | Destructive: zca.disconnect + DELETE account row. No auth, no admin secret — anyone can delete any Zalo account.                                                                                                                                                |
| GET /accounts/:key/self\|/friends\|/groups                   | (659/667/736)                  | Live zca self/friends/groups. /friends + /lookup expose Zalo PII (names, avatars, uids, phones) with no auth.                                                                                                                                                   |
| POST /accounts/:key/sync-conversations                       | (678)                          | Seeds conversations from roster (users+groups), respecting tracked filter. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                             |
| POST /accounts/:key/repair-group-names                       | (745)                          | Force re-fetch names via \_repairConvNames. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| GET /lookup?accountKey&phone\|uid                            | (759)                          | zca.getUserInfo / findUser → returns full Zalo profile for arbitrary phone/uid. No auth — open PII lookup / enumeration.                                                                                                                                        |
| GET /conversations                                           | (776)                          | Paginated conv list; search ILIKE parameterized; lazily resolves missing group sender names via zca + caches into web2_zalo_members. No auth.                                                                                                                   |
| GET /conversations/:id/messages                              | (935)                          | Reads messages keyset-paginated; lazily heals user/group name+avatar (TTL 6h, 2s timeout); on first page resets unread_count=0 and stamps last_read. No auth.                                                                                                   |
| POST /conversations/:id/backfill                             | (1060)                         | Group-only: pulls zca getGroupHistory, dedupe INSERT, auto-ingest J&T codes. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| GET /conversations/:id/members                               | (1146)                         | Group member list for @mention. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| GET /conversation/:phone                                     | (1215)                         | Lookup conv by phone, prefers primary account. No auth — phone→conversation disclosure.                                                                                                                                                                         |
| POST /conversation/ensure                                    | (1239)                         | Ensure 1-1 conv: findUser via zca, upsert RETURNING. Race: two concurrent ensures both miss SELECT then upsert (idempotent via ON CONFLICT, OK). No auth.                                                                                                       |
| POST /send-message\|/send-image\|/send-file\|/send-sticker   | (1343/1385/1449/1504)          | SENDS to real Zalo customer via zca then \_persistOut + SSE. No auth, no idempotency/rate-limit, no dedupe on null msg_id → double-submit sends duplicate real messages.                                                                                        |
| POST /react\|/recall\|/forward\|/typing\|/seen               | (1530/1553/1567/1580/1592)     | zca side-effects + persist + SSE. /seen sends read receipts to Zalo. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| GET /media/:id                                               | (1654)                         | Serves bytea media with immutable cache, by numeric id only. No auth, no account scoping → any caller can enumerate ids and read any shop-sent image/file.                                                                                                      |
| POST /oa/connect\|/oa/sync-templates\|/send-zns\|/oa/send-cs | (1725/1738/1758/1777)          | OA: exchange app secret/code, send ZNS/CS to real customers. send-zns validation done in oa.sendZNS (phone/template). No auth — anyone can send ZNS billed messages.                                                                                            |
| GET /zns/templates\|/zns/log                                 | (1746/1786)                    | Reads templates/logs; /zns/log exposes customer phones + params. No auth.                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| GET/POST/DELETE /tracked-groups                              | (1821/1834/1856)               | Manage firehose allowlist; reloads \_trackedSet via \_loadTracked; SSE web2:zalo:accounts tracked-changed. No auth.                                                                                                                                             |
| POST /admin/reset-to-tracked                                 | (1874)                         | Destructive wipe of messages/conversations/media/members; GATED by x-admin-secret==CLEANUP_SECRET (the ONLY auth-gated endpoint). dryRun unless confirm=YES-RESET.                                                                                              |
| runZaloRetention(days)                                       | (1978)                         | Cron: DELETE messages+media older than N days, null out stale conv previews.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| restoreSessions()                                            | (2016)                         | Boot: zca.restoreAll for personal accounts with stored session — login WITHOUT expectedUid (trusts stored session).                                                                                                                                             |

**Liên kết:** web2/zalo/ (primary UI page — single source for Zalo management), web2/shared/web2-zalo.js (Web2Zalo helper: getConversation/ensure/sendZNS consume /conversation/:phone, /conversation/ensure, /send-zns), web2/shared/web2-customer-chat.js (Web2CustomerChat Zalo tab → send-message/send-image/conversations), render.com/services/web2-zalo-zca.js (zca-js engine: login, send, listeners, expectedUid guard), render.com/services/web2-zalo-oa.js (OA: exchangeCode, sendZNS, sendCsMessage, syncTemplates — phone/template validation), render.com/db/web2-zalo-schema.js (ensureWeb2ZaloSchema; web2*zalo_accounts/conversations/messages/media/members/tracked_groups, web2_zns*_), render.com/routes/web2-jt-tracking.js (autoIngestFromZalo — J&T 12-digit code ingest from group msgs), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (notifyClients hub for web2:zalo:_ topics), render.com/middleware/web2-auth.js (requireWeb2AuthSoft/Admin — NOT imported here; gap), cloudflare-worker (WEB2_ZALO route → handleCustomer360Proxy → web2-api; forwards x-web2-token/x-admin-secret)

**SSE:** PUBLISH web2:zalo:messages (create on inbound, update on outbound/heal/sync/reset) — \_persistIncoming 334, \_persistOut 1332, heal handlers 974/1007, sync 724, backfill 1129, reset 1960; PUBLISH web2:zalo:thread:<threadId> (message/typing/reaction/recall/seen) — \_notifyThread 352-353, \_persistIncoming 335, \_persistOut 1333; PUBLISH web2:zalo:accounts (create/update/deleted/tracked-changed/reset) — \_saveSession 459, \_updateAccStatus 439, account CRUD 516/611/638/651, tracked 1848/1863, reset 1959; No EventSource/subscribe in this file (server side). No per-request debounce — every persisted event notifies immediately; clients are expected to debounce. SSE notify is fire-and-forget wrapped in try/catch (\_notify 33-40); failures only console.warn.; Cleanup: this router does not own SSE client connections; the only timer is the 60s \_loadTracked refresh interval (unref'd) and the retention cron — neither is cleared on hot-reload (acceptable for a long-lived server process).

**console.log:** 11 — No PII/token leakage in logs — all 11 console.\* log only error.message, counts, or accountKey (an opaque uuid).; routes/web2-zalo.js:122 console.log repaired conv count (benign); routes/web2-zalo.js:1991 console.log retention deletion counts (benign); All others are console.warn of caught error messages (38,58,104,113,114,115,124,1996,2024) — no cookie/session/access_token/phone content is logged.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `race` Dedupe + unread counting breaks when msg_id is NULL. \_persistIncoming relies on `ins.rowCount>0` (isNew) under `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` to add exactly +1 unread, but the only unique index is partial: uq_web2_zalo_msg_id ON (account_key,msg_id) WHERE msg_id IS NOT NULL. Inbound msgs can have null msg — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:274-298; db/web2-zalo-schema.js:185` → Make dedupe deterministic: add a unique index covering (account_key, COALESCE(msg_id, cli_msg_id)) or require msg_id non-null; alternatively skip unread increment when msg_id is null. Use an explicit
- 🟠 `data-loss` Send endpoints have no idempotency/rate-limit. A double-submit of /send-message (or /send-image/file/sticker) calls zca.send twice → the real customer receives duplicate messages. Combined with the no-auth surface and null-msgId dedupe gap, \_persistOut may also insert duplicate rows (sticker out has — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:1343-1527,1306-1335` → Add a client-supplied idempotency key (cli_msg_id) enforced before zca.send (advisory lock or short in-process inflight WeakSet/Map keyed by accountKey+threadId+cliMsgId), like the A1 PBH double-submi
- 🟡 `race` \_trackedSet / \_filterActive are module-global mutable state read in the firehose (\_persistIncoming line 270, sync-conversations 687/711). \_loadTracked() reassigns \_trackedSet/\_filterActive non-atomically and is invoked from a 60s interval, from /tracked-groups mutations, and from /admin/reset — conc — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:46-60,270,1847,1862,1958,2010` → Build the new Set fully in a local then swap both fields together (already mostly the case) and consider double-buffering; acceptable risk but document/snapshot at read time.
- 🟡 `logic` GROUP*NAME_TTL_MS heal compares now() - Number(conv.info_synced_at) where info_synced_at is epoch-ms BIGINT. Consistent ms math here is fine, but the broader file mixes now()=Date.now() ms everywhere while Render runs TZ=Asia/Saigon (+7). No string-date parsing of Zalo timestamps is done here (sent* — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:949,986; sent_at usage` → No change needed server-side; ensure web2/zalo page formats sent_at with timeZone Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh (epoch is unambiguous).
- 🟡 `perf` GET /conversations and GET /conversations/:id/messages perform synchronous per-request zca network calls (getGroupMembersInfo / getUserInfo / getGroupsInfo) inside the request handler, each awaited (2s timeout). On a busy account this serializes list loads behind zca latency and runs N member-resolv — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:810-846,888-908` → Move name-resolution to a background reconcile (already have \_repairConvNames) and batch the member upserts into a single multi-row INSERT.
- ⚪ `logic` \_persistOut sticker path can persist a row with NULL msg_id and NULL cli_msg_id (send-sticker passes only msgId, line 1521); such rows are never deduped and never recall/react-addressable. Also \_persistOut ignores ON CONFLICT for null-msgId duplicates same as the inbound bug. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:1504-1523,1306-1313` → Always thread a generated cli_msg_id through send paths and dedupe on it.
- ⚪ `bug` No partial unique constraint enforces a single is_primary=true. /primary's single full-table UPDATE keeps it consistent, but any other code path (schema seed, manual SQL) could set two; readers (\_getPrimaryKey LIMIT 1) silently pick one arbitrarily. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:619-643,88-97; schema:102` → Add CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... ON web2_zalo_accounts(is_primary) WHERE is_primary=true.
- ⚪ `other` Many fire-and-forget .catch(()=>{}) swallow errors silently (e.g. unread-reset 1042, member upserts 1199, J&T ingest 340/1124), so persistence failures vanish without a log, hindering diagnosis of missing-data reports. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:340,1042,1124,1199, etc.` → Replace empty catches with a throttled console.warn so silent failures are observable.

**Security (audit):**

- 🔴 The entire router applies NO authentication. It never requires ../middleware/web2-auth (no router.use, no requireWeb2AuthSoft/Admin), and server.js mounts it without any auth wrapper. With WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 every other web2 router enforces x-web2-token, but web2-zalo is fully open: unauthenticated — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js (whole file; mount server.js:911); contrast middl` → Add const { requireWeb2AuthSoft, requireWeb2Admin } = require('../middleware/web2-auth') and router.use(requireWeb2AuthSoft) at top so reads/writes are gated under WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE; gate DELETE /acco
- 🟠 Secrets stored plaintext at rest: web2_zalo_accounts.session (Zalo cookie+imei+userAgent — full account takeover material), oa_secret, access_token, refresh_token. A DB dump = silent takeover of the shop's Zalo personal account and OA. No encryption/KMS. — `render.com/db/web2-zalo-schema.js:89-97; written by _saveSession (web2-zalo.js:4` → Encrypt session/oa_secret/access_token/refresh_token with an app key (AES-GCM via crypto, key from env) before INSERT/UPDATE; decrypt only in the zca/oa service. At minimum match the C7 hashing postur
- 🟠 Open PII lookup/enumeration. GET /lookup (759) returns a full Zalo profile for any phone/uid; GET /accounts/:key/friends (667) and GET /conversation/:phone (1215) and GET /zns/log (1786, returns phones+params) all return customer PII with no auth. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:759,667,1215,1786` → Gate behind requireWeb2AuthSoft; consider rate-limiting /lookup to prevent phone→profile enumeration.
- 🟠 GET /media/:id (1654) serves stored image/file bytea by numeric BIGSERIAL id with immutable caching, no auth and no account/conversation scoping. IDs are sequential → trivially enumerable to exfiltrate every media item the shop ever sent. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:1654-1668` → Require auth, or sign media URLs (HMAC token in MEDIA_BASE) and validate; at least scope by account_key the caller is authorized for.
- 🟡 Destructive DELETE /accounts/:key (645) has no admin secret and no auth — anyone can delete any Zalo account row (and the only protected destructive op, /admin/reset-to-tracked, sits right next to it gated by x-admin-secret, highlighting the inconsistency). — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:645-656` → Gate with requireWeb2Admin or x-admin-secret.
- 🟡 MEDIA_BASE falls back to a hardcoded Render host (https://web2-api-kv04.onrender.com) used to build persisted attachment URLs; if the deploy host changes, previously-stored URLs break and the value diverges from the web2-auth.js single-source base-URL convention. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:1686-1687` → Derive from WEB2_CONFIG/env single source; store a relative path or media id and resolve at render time instead of baking an absolute host into DB rows.
- ⚪ expectedUid wrong-identity guard is inert on first login (when stored zalo_uid is NULL) and is bypassed entirely on /reconnect and restoreAll (loginWithCredentials called without opts). A malicious/incorrect cookie pushed to a fresh slot is accepted and persisted. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:592,560,2022; services/web2-zalo-zca.js:322,390` → Combined with adding auth (critical above), and optionally require an explicit confirm/label match on first cookie login.

**Ghi chú:** Pool selection is correct Web 2.0 (web2Db||chatDb); no Web 1.0 table/pool/SSE coupling and no TPOS dependency — clean on layering.; SSE wiring uses the correct web2 hub (web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients) with web2:zalo:_ topics; payloads carry only {action,code,ts} (no PII) — good.; The only auth-gated endpoint is /admin/reset-to-tracked (x-admin-secret); this proves the author knows the pattern, making the total absence of x-web2-token gating on the rest a clear omission rather than intentional public API.; Cloudflare worker forwards x-web2-token and x-admin-secret (only host/cf-_ headers stripped), so adding middleware here will work end-to-end without worker changes.; File is 2033 lines — exceeds the 800-line guideline; consider splitting into accounts/messaging/oa/admin sub-routers.; ensureSchema/\_loadTracked/retention/restoreSessions are exported on the router object and invoked from server.js — boot ordering is ensureSchema → restoreSessions.

## Services

### services:comms-zalo

Server-side comms layer for Web 2.0: (1) web2-msg-send-worker — a setInterval polling queue worker that drains web2_msg_send_items (web2Db) and sends FB messages via the Pancake official API with multi-account PAT rotation, falling back to 'needs_extension' for 24h-window errors so the browser extension can bypass; (2) web2-zalo-zca — in-process zca-js personal-account session manager (QR/cookie login, realtime listener, send/react/recall) with one WS listener per account; (3) web2-zalo-oa — official Zalo OA token refresh + ZNS/CS message sender; (4) web2-pancake-creds (AES-256-GCM helper) + web2-pancake-login (pure-Node Pancake JWT login for token auto-refresh); (5) web2-unread-tracker + web2-unread-reconcile — authoritative unread-count store fed by Pancake update_conversation WS events with a 2-min reconcile safety net; (6) web2-webhook-retry — exponential-backoff retry queue for failed SePay Web 2.0 webhook processing. These run as boot/cron/interval jobs gated by DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS and publish SSE on web2:\* topics. The slice straddles the web2Db/chatDb boundary because Pancake credential tables (pancake_accounts, pancake_page_access_tokens) live in chatDb (Web 1.0 shared infra).

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** web2-msg-send-worker.start(): boot-gated by !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS (server.js:2972). setInterval tick every WEB2_MSG_WORKER_INTERVAL_MS=4000ms (worker:431) + setImmediate first tick (worker:435); interval.unref(). Reads JOB_POOL = web2Db||chatDb (worker:27).; web2-msg-send-worker module-level caches initialized at require time: \_patMem Map, \_accountsCache (60s TTL), \_recomputeTimers Map, inFlight Set (worker:41-46).; web2-zalo session manager: zca-js required defensively at module load (zca:23-31); restoreSessions() called at boot from web2-zalo route → zca.restoreAll(rows) re-logins every personal account with stored session (zca:765, route:2022).; web2-zalo-oa: stateless; \_refreshInFlight Map created at module load (oa:62). No boot job — invoked on demand by route/ZNS sends.; web2-pancake-login/creds: stateless modules, no boot side effects.; web2-unread-tracker.ensureSchema(pool) called at boot from server.js (creates web2_unread_messages + index, idempotent) (tracker:27).; web2-unread-reconcile: reconcileAll scheduled at server.js:2949 — setTimeout 60s after boot then setInterval every 2min (server.js:2950), gated !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS. Reads chatDb PAT, writes web2Db, publishes web2:unread.; web2-webhook-retry.ensureSchema at boot (server.js:295) + startCron every 2min after 5s delay (server.js:330-345), gated !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS; processFn wraps web2-sepay-matching insert+match on web2Pool.; web2-pancake-refresh.startCron(chatDbPool) every 6h (server.js:1044) → relogin Pancake accounts ≤5 days to expiry, writes new JWT into pancake_accounts (chatDb) consumed by the msg-send worker.

| Control                                                    | Handler           | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| web2-msg-send-worker.tick() (interval)                     | tick (worker:392) | Guards reentrancy with \_ticking flag. Calls \_recoverStuck (revert stuck sending/ext_inflight), computes free slots = MAX_CONCURRENT(8) - inFlight.size, \_claimPending(slots), then for each claimed item \_processItem fire-and-forget with .finally(inFlight.delete). Errors logged, never rethrown. |
| \_claimPending(limit)                                      | worker:350        | Atomic claim: CTE SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED then UPDATE state='sending', attempts=attempts+1 RETURNING. Correct concurrency-safe claim across processes.                                                                                                                                         |
| \_processItem(item)                                        | worker:200        | Tries cached PAT send; on 24h error → needs_extension; on rate-limit → \_maybeRetryOrExt; on token/permission/unknown → rotate accounts minting fresh PATs; exhausted → needs_extension (default extension fallback). Calls \_finishItem to write terminal state + debounced recompute.                  |
| \_sendPancake(...)                                         | worker:159        | POST via CF worker /api/pancake-official/.../messages?page_access_token=PAT with action reply_inbox. Returns ok / transient(>=500) / FB e_code+e_subcode. THIS IS THE REAL SEND — sends to live FB customer.                                                                                             |
| \_recoverStuck()                                           | worker:371        | UPDATE sending→pending where updated_at<NOW()-90s; ext_inflight→needs_extension where <5min. Re-queues for resend (see bugs: resend risk).                                                                                                                                                               |
| sendSingleMessage(pageId,convId,custId,msg)                | worker:447        | Single best-effort send used by CK auto-reply (server.js:1060). Same PAT cache+rotation path; returns {ok,needsExtension,error}, never throws.                                                                                                                                                           |
| web2-zalo-zca.startQrLogin(key,label)                      | zca:347           | Creates new Zalo({selfListen}), loginQR with QR-event callback updating \_sessions status, then \_afterLogin on success. Returns immediately {status:qr_pending}; browser polls getQr.                                                                                                                   |
| loginWithCredentials(key,creds,label,opts)                 | zca:390           | zalo.login(creds) then \_afterLogin with expectedUid guard. If session already has api → returns alreadyConnected (idempotent).                                                                                                                                                                          |
| \_afterLogin(...)                                          | zca:287           | getContext→credentials, fetchAccountInfo→profile; WRONG_ACCOUNT guard (zca:322) rejects+deletes session if expectedUid mismatch; persistSession cb writes session+uid to DB; \_attachListener; onConnected cb triggers conv-name repair.                                                                 |
| \_attachListener(key,api)                                  | zca:230           | Stops prior listener, registers message/typing/seen/delivered/reaction/undo + onConnected/onClosed/onError handlers, listener.start(). One listener per account.                                                                                                                                         |
| send/sendMedia/react/recall/forward (zca)                  | zca:438-518       | \_requireApi then api.sendMessage etc. send() degrades to no-quote retry if Zalo rejects quote (zca:459). REAL Zalo sends to live recipients.                                                                                                                                                            |
| web2-zalo-oa.refreshToken(pool,account)                    | oa:64             | Dedups concurrent refresh via \_refreshInFlight Map keyed by account_key\|\|id (oa:62) — prevents parallel refresh invalidating the rotating refresh_token. \_doRefresh persists new access/refresh token to web2_zalo_accounts.                                                                         |
| sendZNS(pool,{phone,templateId,data,...})                  | oa:175            | Normalizes phone→84xxx, logs pending row in web2_zns_log, getValidToken (auto-refresh), POST ZNS template, updates log status sent/failed. Real billed ZNS send.                                                                                                                                         |
| sendCsMessage(pool,{oaRef,userId,text})                    | oa:240            | getValidToken then POST CS message to OA-engaged user. Real send.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| web2-pancake-login.loginPancake({identity,password})       | login:145         | 3-request pure-Node flow (authorize→login→approve) with manual cookie jar + redirect follow to obtain jwt cookie; decodeJwt validates uid. Returns {ok,token,decoded} \| {ok:false,reason}.                                                                                                              |
| web2-unread-tracker.syncFromConversation(pool,data,notify) | tracker:63        | Authoritative: if unreadCount===0 \|\| shopSentLast → DELETE row + SSE clear; else UPSERT message_count=unreadCount (SET not increment, no drift) + SSE upsert on web2:unread.                                                                                                                           |
| web2-unread-reconcile.reconcilePage / reconcileAll         | reconcile:121/131 | For each page with unread rows: read PAT from chatDb, fetch Pancake conversations via worker, \_convToSyncData, replay tracker.syncFromConversation to heal missed WS read-events.                                                                                                                       |
| web2-webhook-retry.enqueue / processQueue / startCron      | retry:38/63/136   | enqueue upserts pending row (2min next_retry); processQueue picks ≤20 due rows FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, runs processFn, marks success / backoff-retry / permanent_failure at 5 attempts; cron every 2min.                                                                                                 |

**Liên kết:** render.com/routes/web2-msg-send.js (job/items CRUD, \_recomputeAndNotify, extension-items/claim-ext/result endpoints; consumed by native-orders bulk-send UI), render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js (mounts zca callbacks, persistSession/\_saveSession, send endpoints, restoreSessions at boot), render.com/routes/web2-pancake-refresh.js (uses web2-pancake-login + web2-pancake-creds; cron writes pancake_accounts JWT), render.com/services/web2-sepay-matching.js (processFn target for webhook-retry: insertWeb2BalanceHistory + processWeb2Match), render.com/routes/sepay-webhook-core.js (initWeb2CkWatcher injects sendSingleMessage for CK auto-reply), render.com/routes/native-orders.js (snapshotKpiBase called by worker \_finishItem on 'Chốt đơn' done), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (notifyClients hub for web2:bulk-send*, web2:unread, web2:zalo:* topics), CF worker chatomni-proxy /api/pancake-official, /api/pancake-official-v2, /api/pancake (PAT mint + send + conversations proxy), chatDb tables pancake_accounts + pancake_page_access_tokens (Web 1.0 shared infra read/written by Web 2.0 worker), web2/payment-confirm page (consumer of web2:unread SSE 'Tin nhắn chưa đọc' tab)

**SSE:** PUBLISH web2:bulk-send:<jobId> and web2:bulk-send via route \_recomputeAndNotify; worker debounces recompute per job 500ms (\_scheduleRecompute worker:335, timer.unref). \_recomputeTimers Map cleared on fire — no leak.; PUBLISH web2:unread (action upsert/clear) from tracker.\_emit (tracker:50) and reconcile replay. Payload {action,psid,pageId,ts} — psid is FB PSID (semi-identifier) but no name/phone. No debounce in tracker (1 emit per conversation event); reconcile can burst-emit one per conversation per page with no debounce.; PUBLISH web2:zalo:accounts and web2:zalo:messages from zca callbacks via route (\_setStatus/onStatus, persistSession→_notify). Status changes fire per event.; SUBSCRIBE: none in these services (server-side publishers only). Cleanup: worker stop() clears interval; webhook-retry stopCron clears interval; zca disconnect() stops listener + deletes session; reconcile setInterval at server.js:2950 is NEVER cleared (no unref, runs forever) — acceptable for a server but note no teardown.

**console.log:** 42 — No PII/token leaks found. All logs use e.message or account_key/sepay_id/item.id only.; web2-zalo-zca.js:773 logs account_key on restore (low-sensitivity identifier, acceptable).; web2-webhook-retry.js:54,92,105,118,120 log sepay_id + error text (sepay_id is a transaction id, not customer PII; error strings could in theory contain bank-memo content if processFn surfaces it — low risk).; Volume note: worker (12) + zca (13) + webhook-retry (10) are all warn/error/boot logs, not leftover debug noise.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `race` Duplicate message send via stuck-recovery. \_claimPending sets state='sending' and increments attempts; \_recoverStuck reverts sending→pending after 90s. If \_sendPancake's underlying Pancake/FB call actually delivered the message but the HTTP response was lost/slow past the worker's own 12s fetch time — `render.com/services/web2-msg-send-worker.js:159-189 (_sendPancake), 350-368 (_cl` → Send a client-side idempotency token (e.g. item.id as a dedupe key / cliMsgId) to Pancake and/or have \_processItem write a 'sent_at marker' before the send so recovery can distinguish 'never attempted
- 🟠 `race` Extension result vs. stuck-recovery double-drain. /:id/items/:itemId/result (web2-msg-send.js:334) updates the item to done/error WITHOUT a state guard. The worker's \_recoverStuck reverts ext_inflight→needs_extension after 5min. If an extension claims (ext_inflight), sends, but reports back later th — `render.com/routes/web2-msg-send.js:308-345; render.com/services/web2-msg-send-wo` → Make /result idempotent and state-guarded: UPDATE ... WHERE id=$ AND job_id=$ AND state IN ('ext_inflight','needs_extension') and ignore if 0 rows. Increase EXT_INFLIGHT_STUCK_MS or have the extension
- 🟡 `logic` web2-webhook-retry.enqueue ON CONFLICT sets retry_count = web2_webhook_retry_queue.retry_count + 0 (a no-op) AND resets status='pending', next_retry_at=2min. If the same sepay_id keeps failing and the upstream keeps re-enqueueing on each failure, retry_count never advances toward the 5-attempt perma — `render.com/services/web2-webhook-retry.js:42-53` → On conflict, do NOT reset next_retry_at downward and either keep the existing backoff schedule or only re-open if status='permanent_failure' was a transient classification. The '+ 0' is almost certain
- 🟡 `web1-coupling` Web 2.0 worker reads AND writes Web 1.0 chatDb tables: \_getAccounts reads pancake_accounts, \_getCachedPAT/\_mintPAT read+UPSERT pancake_page_access_tokens (chatDb). The header comment marks this as 'researched/accepted', but per Web2⊥Web1 rule a Web 2.0 service mutating Web 1.0 credential tables is a — `render.com/services/web2-msg-send-worker.js:86-101, 117-156; reconcile:49-60` → Acceptable as shared Pancake infra, but document the write to pancake_page_access_tokens explicitly and ensure the 6h refresh cron and worker PAT-mint use ON CONFLICT (already do) so concurrent writes
- 🟡 `race` PAT cache poisoning under rotation. \_mintPAT stores the freshly-minted PAT in \_patMem (30min TTL) and persists to chatDb even before confirming it can send. If a rotated account mints a PAT that lacks permission for the page, that bad PAT is cached for up to 30min and persisted, so subsequent sends — `render.com/services/web2-msg-send-worker.js:131-156 (_mintPAT caches before send` → Only cache/persist a PAT after a successful send with it; on send failure due to token/permission, invalidate \_patMem.delete(pageId) so the next item re-mints.
- 🟡 `logic` sendSingleMessage (CK auto-reply) reuses \_getCachedPAT but never invalidates a stale PAT and never falls into needs_extension persistence — on 24h error it returns {needsExtension:true} but the CK watcher fire-and-forgets, so the balance-notification message is silently dropped with no retry/queue. — `render.com/services/web2-msg-send-worker.js:447-475; server.js:1059-1065 (fire-a` → If sendSingleMessage returns needsExtension/error for a CK reply, enqueue it into web2_msg_send_items (or a single-send queue) so the extension can drain it, instead of dropping.
- ⚪ `race` Zalo re-login race on boot vs. manual login. restoreAll iterates accounts sequentially (await each loginWithCredentials), guarded by existing?.api early-return — good. But a manual POST login arriving during boot restore for the same accountKey could both pass the !existing.api check before either s — `render.com/services/web2-zalo-zca.js:390-402 (loginWithCredentials existing chec` → Set a 'connecting' sentinel (s.api = pending promise or s.connecting=true) in \_sessions before awaiting zalo.login so a concurrent caller short-circuits; or serialize per-accountKey logins with an in-
- ⚪ `logic` Unread reconcile timestamp parsing: \_convToSyncData parses conv.updated_at / last_message.inserted_at. Pancake string timestamps lack a trailing Z (UTC), but here Date.parse(t) is used directly (reconcile:91). On the Render box (TZ=Asia/Saigon +7) Date.parse of a naive 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS' string i — `render.com/services/web2-unread-reconcile.js:87-93` → Normalize naive timestamps by appending 'Z' before Date.parse (the parseUtcTs pattern used elsewhere) so reconcile and WS agree.
- ⚪ `perf` Reconcile reconcileAll has no concurrency cap and no debounce on the per-conversation SSE emits: for every page with unread rows it fetches all INBOX conversations and replays syncFromConversation, each potentially emitting a web2:unread event. A page with many conversations produces a burst of SSE — `render.com/services/web2-unread-reconcile.js:109-118, 131-151` → Only emit when a row actually changed (tracker already gates clear on rowCount>0 but upsert always emits); suppress upsert emit when the row is unchanged, or coalesce reconcile emits.
- ⚪ `logic` \_isChotDonTemplate/\_jobMetaCache never invalidates; if a job's template_name were updated mid-run (unlikely) the cache would be stale. More relevant: \_jobMetaCache and \_patMem and \_accountsCache grow unbounded over process lifetime (Maps never pruned for completed jobs/pages). — `render.com/services/web2-msg-send-worker.js:44-45, 297-310` → Minor memory creep on a long-lived process; add periodic prune or cap sizes. Low priority.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 Zalo personal-account session (cookie + imei + userAgent = full account takeover material) is stored as PLAINTEXT JSON in web2_zalo_accounts.session. web2-pancake-creds.js provides AES-256-GCM at-rest encryption but it is only wired to Pancake login passwords, never to Zalo session or OA secrets. — `render.com/routes/web2-zalo.js:451 (JSON.stringify(creds)); zca restoreAll reads` → Encrypt session JSON with web2-pancake-creds.encrypt() before persist and decrypt() in restoreAll/loginWithCredentials, same as the Pancake password path. Reuse PANCAKE_CREDS_KEY or a dedicated ZALO_C
- 🟠 Zalo OA secrets stored plaintext: oa_secret, access_token, refresh_token written in cleartext to web2_zalo_accounts. refresh_token is a ~3-month rotating credential; DB/backup compromise = OA hijack + billed ZNS abuse. — `render.com/services/web2-zalo-oa.js:97-110 (refreshToken UPDATE), 135-156 (excha` → Encrypt oa_secret + refresh_token at rest via the existing AES-GCM helper; decrypt just-in-time in \_doRefresh/getValidToken.
- 🟡 msg-send routes that trigger real customer sends and state mutations have NO x-web2-token auth gate (no web2-auth middleware mounted). POST /, /result, /claim-ext, /cancel are open. With WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 elsewhere, these are an unguarded write surface (anyone reaching Render can enqueue a bulk se — `render.com/routes/web2-msg-send.js (whole router — no router.use(web2Auth); moun` → Apply the SOFT-then-enforce web2-auth middleware (like pbh-reports/web2-generic) so writes require x-web2-token when WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1.
- 🟡 PANCAKE_CREDS_KEY is the only protection for stored Pancake passwords; if env unset, encrypt() returns null and refresh route refuses (good), but there is no startup assertion that the key exists in production — silent degraded mode where auto-refresh cannot run. — `render.com/services/web2-pancake-creds.js:18-27` → Log a single prominent boot warning when isConfigured() is false in production so the missing key is visible.
- ⚪ web2-pancake-login hardcodes Pancake OAuth CLIENT_ID and a STATE blob in source (login:17-25). Not a secret per se (public client id) but couples to a specific app registration. — `render.com/services/web2-pancake-login.js:17-25` → Move CLIENT_ID to env to ease rotation; acceptable as-is since it is a public identifier.
- ⚪ web2:unread SSE payload includes psid (Facebook PSID). PSID is page-scoped and not directly PII, but it is a stable customer identifier broadcast to all subscribers. — `render.com/services/web2-unread-tracker.js:77,108` → Acceptable (client re-fetches detail); keep payload minimal (already {action,psid,pageId,ts}).

**Ghi chú:** Concurrency claim path (_claimPending) is correctly written with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + state recheck; the duplicate-send risk is NOT in claiming but in the recovery→resend of items whose send response was lost (no idempotency key to Pancake).; web2-zalo-oa correctly dedups parallel token refresh via \_refreshInFlight (oa:62-70) — good defense against the rotating-refresh-token footgun. The same pattern is NOT applied to zca per-account login (see bug).; WRONG_ACCOUNT identity guard in \_afterLogin (zca:322) is a solid protection against cookie-login binding the wrong Zalo identity to a slot.; All eight files keep logs free of tokens/cookies/PII — only e.message and ids are logged.; web2-webhook-retry, unread-tracker, unread-reconcile correctly use web2Db for web2_\* tables and only READ chatDb for Pancake PAT (shared infra) — pool usage is correct except the worker's PAT WRITE to chatDb (flagged).; reconcile setInterval (server.js:2950) and webhook-retry/worker intervals have no shared teardown path; only stop()/stopCron() exist and are unused at runtime — fine for a long-lived server but means tests must call stop explicitly.

### services:misc

Seven Web 2.0 Node service modules under render.com/services. web2-caption-service generates Vietnamese FB sales captions (offline templates FREE, optional Groq->DeepSeek->Gemini AI). web2-fb-graph-service is a pure (no-DB) Meta Graph v21.0 wrapper for OAuth, publish/schedule/list/delete posts, page/post insights, and ad insights. web2-cutout-service does background removal via withoutbg/fal-BiRefNet/PhotoRoom with multi-key rotation. web2-ck-watcher auto-reconciles new SePay transactions against pending/confirmed "đã CK" payment signals (two directions) and credits wallets + auto-replies to customers. web2-match-audit logs every match decision into web2*match_audit with a 5-min revert/undo (withdraws wallet in a transaction). web2-blacklist is a DB-backed (60s cache) extraction blacklist. web2-order-customer-service is a thin adapter over web2_customers warehouse. All correctly target web2Db/web2*_ and avoid TPOS; ck-watcher publishes web2:_ SSE topics. The biggest risks are in the routes consuming these services (fb-posts has no auth) and in ck-watcher race/dedup edges.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** server.js boot: matchAudit.ensureSchema(web2Pool||chatDb) + blacklist.ensureSchema (creates web2_match_audit, web2_extraction_blacklist; blacklist seeds '75918' ACB account) — fired with .catch warn, non-blocking (server.js:285-300); server.js boot: web2CkWatcher.initDeps({notify, createNotification, sendMessage}) + sepay-webhook-core.initWeb2CkWatcher(sameDeps) wire SSE notify + auto-reply sendMessage (server.js:1054-1076); ck-watcher runtime triggers: onNewSepayTx called from sepay-webhook-core.js:77 on every inbound SePay tx AND re-invoked from web2-balance-history.js:328 (manual link path); onNewSignal called from server.js:1586 & :1650 on each detected 'đã CK' message via WS handler; fb-graph-service: stateless at boot; module-level \_liveCache Map (60s TTL per page) for live-video classification; reads FB_APP_ID/FB_APP_SECRET env at require time; cutout-service: reads FAL_KEY / PHOTOROOM_API_KEY / WITHOUTBG_API_KEYS env at require; module-level withoutbgIdx sticky key index; caption-service: reads GROQ_API_KEY/DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/GEMINI_API_KEY lazily per call (process.env inside callX); no boot work; blacklist: module-level \_cache Set + \_cacheLoadedAt (60s TTL), getBlacklist loads on first webhook

| Control                                                               | Handler                                    | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| caption.generateTemplate(product,style) / generateMultiTemplate       | web2-caption-service.js:100 / :254         | Offline FREE caption + buildHashtags (max 6). fmtMoney uses Math.random() suffix -> non-deterministic output for same input (intended novelty).                                                                                                                                               |
| caption.generateAI / generateMultiAI                                  | web2-caption-service.js:222 / :272         | aiComplete tries Groq->DeepSeek->Gemini; each callX returns null if no key OR !res.ok (errors swallowed silently); falls back to template. \_friendlyTone post-processes pronouns. No timeout/AbortController on AI fetch -> can hang.                                                        |
| fb.publishToPage({pageId,pageToken,message,media,link,scheduledTime}) | web2-fb-graph-service.js:234               | Publishes/schedules to a real FB page via Graph: video->/videos, photos->upload unpublished then /feed attached_media, else text /feed. Sequential photo upload (await in loop). Returns {postId,scheduled}. Pure fn — caller (route) supplies token.                                         |
| fb.deletePost / updatePost / updatePostMessage                        | web2-fb-graph-service.js:287 / :303 / :293 | DELETE or POST to Graph post-id; mutates real published content.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| fb.normalizeScheduleSec                                               | web2-fb-graph-service.js:164               | Parses ms\|sec\|ISO -> unix sec, validates 10min..30day window. ISO string parsed via new Date() (host TZ=+7) — see timezone bug.                                                                                                                                                             |
| fb.getLiveVideoMap                                                    | web2-fb-graph-service.js:355               | 60s per-page in-memory cache of live video status; errors swallowed (returns {}).                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| cutout svc.withoutbgCutout(buffer)                                    | web2-cutout-service.js:53                  | Rotates WITHOUTBG_KEYS on 401/402/403/429; sticky idx mutated on success/exhaust (shared mutable, concurrency race). 12MB cap. Returns PNG buffer.                                                                                                                                            |
| cutout svc.birefnetCutout / photoroomCutout                           | web2-cutout-service.js:101 / :134          | fal.ai/PhotoRoom segment; birefnet fetches returned image URL (second fetch, no timeout).                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ckwatcher.onNewSepayTx(db,txId,deps)                                  | web2-ck-watcher.js:258                     | Loads tx, resolves identity from web2_payment_qr_codes, scores pending/confirmed signals (72h, matched_tx_id IS NULL), classifies, \_applyMatch on best. Whole body wrapped in try/catch -> errors only warn-logged (best-effort).                                                            |
| ckwatcher.onNewSignal(db,sig,deps)                                    | web2-ck-watcher.js:307                     | Scans up to 200 'in' txs (72h) NOT already claimed; resolves identity per-tx sequentially (N+1 awaits, up to 200 QR lookups); \_applyMatch best. Guards sig.matched_tx_id at entry only.                                                                                                      |
| ckwatcher.\_applyMatch                                                | web2-ck-watcher.js:143                     | Atomic CLAIM signal (UPDATE ... WHERE matched_tx_id IS NULL) -> linkTransaction (idempotent via debt_added) -> append history -> SSE notify web2:payment-signals + web2:balance-history -> auto-reply via deps.sendMessage when credited\|\|reconciled. Returns applied\|lost_race\|not_sure. |
| matchAudit.revert(db,auditId,revertedBy,windowMs)                     | web2-match-audit.js:163                    | In one tx with FOR UPDATE: guards reverted/window(5min)/credit, processWithdraw (rollback on failure), resets web2_balance_history row, marks reverted. Solid.                                                                                                                                |
| matchAudit.list(db,opts)                                              | web2-match-audit.js:105                    | Parameterized filters; LIMIT/OFFSET interpolated but clamped via parseInt/Math.min -> safe.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| blacklist.getBlacklist / add / deactivate / remove                    | web2-blacklist.js:61 / :75 / :90 / :102    | 60s cached Set of active exact patterns (always includes '75918'). add/deactivate/remove invalidate cache. add upserts ON CONFLICT reactivating.                                                                                                                                              |
| orderCustomer.getOrCreateWeb2OrderCustomer / lookupCustomerIdByPhone  | web2-order-customer-service.js:30 / :53    | Delegates to web2-customers-schema getOrCreateWeb2Customer / lookupWeb2CustomerIdByPhone on web2_customers (web2Db). Throws on invalid phone.                                                                                                                                                 |

**Liên kết:** routes/web2-fb-posts.js (consumes caption + fb-graph services; mounts /api/web2-fb-posts), routes/web2-cutout.js (consumes cutout service; /api/web2-cutout), routes/v2/web2-monitoring.js (consumes match-audit + blacklist; /api/web2/monitoring/\*), routes/sepay-webhook-core.js (invokes ck-watcher.onNewSepayTx), routes/v2/web2-balance-history.js (linkTransaction used by ck-watcher; also re-invokes onNewSepayTx), services/web2-sepay-matching.js (consumes match-audit + blacklist in main matcher), services/web2-content-extractor.js (ck-watcher requires extractIdentifier), services/web2-wallet-service.js (processWithdraw/processDeposit for revert + linkTransaction), services/web2-msg-send-worker.js (sendSingleMessage for ck-watcher auto-reply), routes/v2/web2-notifications.js (createNotification dep), db/web2-customers-schema.js (order-customer adapter target), routes/fast-sale-orders.js & routes/native-orders.js (consume order-customer-service), web2 page consumers: web2/fb-posts/, web2/product-card/ & photo-studio (cutout), web2/ck-dashboard/, web2/audit-log/, web2/system/ (monitoring)

**SSE:** ck-watcher PUBLISHES web2:payment-signals {action:'auto-link',id,ts} and web2:balance-history {action:'link',id,ts} via deps.notify (web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients) inside \_applyMatch (web2-ck-watcher.js:204-219). Payload is ID-only (no PII) — good. Wrapped in try/catch ignore.; No SSE subscriptions in these services (server-side). fb-posts route (consumer) publishes connect/disconnect/publish notifications via its own \_notify (not in scope files).; No debounce here (debounce lives in client subscribers). No EventSource/listener cleanup concerns at service layer.

**console.log:** 12 — web2-ck-watcher.js:200-201 console.log auto-link logs only sig#/GD# IDs (no PII) — safe; web2-ck-watcher.js:239 medium-match log IDs only — safe; web2-cutout-service.js:83 logs key index '#0'/'#1' + HTTP status only — no key value leaked — safe; No tokens, phone numbers, or customer names are written to console in any of the 7 files. consoleLogsNotable: none leak PII/secrets.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `logic` Duplicate customer auto-reply: on an inbound SePay tx, sepay-webhook-core runs processWeb2Match FIRST (which can credit + set debt_added=true and may itself reply) THEN runs ck-watcher.onNewSepayTx. ck-watcher's \_applyMatch claims a signal and calls linkTransaction which returns alreadyProcessed=tru — `web2-ck-watcher.js:220-234; ordering routes/sepay-webhook-core.js:63-78` → Only auto-reply when credited===true (a fresh credit this call), not when reconciled (already-credited). Or record a reply-sent flag on the tx/signal and skip if set.
- 🟠 `race` onNewSepayTx and onNewSignal can both run for the same tx<->signal pair near-simultaneously (tiền-về hook fires onNewSepayTx; the 'đã CK' WS message fires onNewSignal). The atomic signal CLAIM (UPDATE ... WHERE matched_tx_id IS NULL) prevents double-link of the SIGNAL, but onNewSignal selects txs wi — `web2-ck-watcher.js:334-365 (NOT EXISTS scan, no SELECT FOR UPDATE on tx)` → After CLAIM succeeds, verify linkTransaction credited (credited===true) before treating as a real match; on reconciled-but-not-credited, roll back the signal claim (set matched_tx_id back to NULL) if
- 🟡 `logic` nameHit uniqueness is computed per-call (nameHitCount over the current scored set) with no cross-process locking. Two concurrent onNewSepayTx invocations for two different txs that both match the same same-named signal can each see nameHitCount===1 and both auto-confirm against the same single name- — `web2-ck-watcher.js:127-137, :295-296 (only 'not_sure' triggers _notifyMedium; 'l` → Treat 'lost_race' as a trigger for \_notifyMedium too (the tx still needs human attention), or re-scan after losing the race.
- 🟡 `data-loss` Both onNewSepayTx and onNewSignal swallow all errors with only console.warn (best-effort). If linkTransaction throws AFTER the signal CLAIM commits (e.g. wallet service error), the signal is permanently marked confirmed+matched_tx_id with NO wallet credit and NO retry — money never reaches the custo — `web2-ck-watcher.js:168-180 (linkTransaction in try, credited stays false) + oute` → If CLAIM succeeded but credited&&reconciled are both false, either rollback the signal claim or enqueue to the webhook-retry queue and emit a staff notification instead of silently leaving an un-credi
- 🟡 `tpos-coupling` Timezone: fb-graph normalizeScheduleSec parses ISO strings with bare new Date(scheduledTime).getTime() (web2-fb-graph-service.js:169). The Render host runs TZ=Asia/Saigon (+7), and Pancake/web2 timestamps frequently lack a trailing Z. A naive 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss' (no Z) from the client is interpret — `web2-fb-graph-service.js:164-179` → Require numeric epoch (ms/sec) from the client for scheduling, or explicitly treat naive strings as Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh wall-clock and convert deterministically; reject ambiguous strings.
- 🟡 `race` cutout withoutbgIdx is shared mutable module state mutated by concurrent requests (line 76 on success, 92 on exhaust). Two parallel cutouts can interleave reads/writes of withoutbgIdx, causing keys to be skipped or quota-exhausted keys to be retried repeatedly within the same burst. Not corrupting, — `web2-cutout-service.js:30,59,76,92` → Snapshot start index locally (already done) and only advance the shared idx atomically; acceptable for low concurrency but document/guard if cutout traffic grows.
- ⚪ `logic` \_score computes 'within24' (web2-ck-watcher.js:114) but it is never consumed by \_classify or sort — dead signal. Intent was likely to gate auto-confirm to within 24h, so a phone-matched signal/tx pair up to 72h apart still auto-credits+replies, which may surprise (old signal auto-resolved by an unre — `web2-ck-watcher.js:114-115 vs _classify :127` → Either use within24 to tighten 'sure' (e.g. require within24 for nameHit), or remove the dead field.
- ⚪ `perf` onNewSignal resolves tx identity sequentially for up to 200 txs (await \_resolveTxIdentity in a for-loop, each doing a web2_payment_qr_codes query when content has QR candidates) — up to ~200 serial DB round-trips per new 'đã CK' message, on the hot WS message path. — `web2-ck-watcher.js:347-358` → Batch the QR lookups (collect all candidates, one ANY() query) or cap the scan tighter / short-circuit once a sure phoneHit/partnerHit is found.
- ⚪ `logic` caption fmtMoney uses Math.random() to pick the price suffix (k/xu/kk/kkk) so the same product yields different captions across template re-renders and the offline template is non-idempotent. For AI prompts the price string is also randomized, which can confuse caching/dedup of generated posts. — `web2-caption-service.js:65-80` → Seed the suffix from a stable product field (e.g. name hash) if reproducibility matters; otherwise document as intentional.
- ⚪ `logic` blacklist getBlacklist only matches type='exact' patterns (\_loadCache filters type='exact', line 48) but add() accepts arbitrary 'type' (e.g. prefix/regex) and the table seeds/stores them; any non-exact pattern added via the monitoring route is silently never enforced. — `web2-blacklist.js:48 vs add :84` → Either implement non-exact matching in the consumer or reject non-'exact' types in add() so admins aren't misled into thinking a prefix is active.

**Security (audit):**

- 🔴 Consumer route web2-fb-posts.js mounts EVERY endpoint with NO auth middleware — including POST /connect (stores FB user+page tokens), POST /publish (posts to real Facebook pages seen by customers), POST /delete (deletes live posts), POST /disconnect (DELETE FROM web2_fb_post_tokens). There is no glo — `routes/web2-fb-posts.js:285,314,371,541,314 (mount server.js:942) vs gated peers` → Apply requireWeb2AuthSoft to all GET and requireWeb2Auth (or requireWeb2Admin for connect/disconnect/delete) to every mutating route in web2-fb-posts.js, matching the cutout/monitoring routers.
- 🟠 FB user + page access tokens stored as plaintext TEXT in web2_fb_post_tokens (user_token TEXT NOT NULL; pages jsonb includes access_token). No encryption at rest. Combined with the unauthenticated /connect endpoint and a single-row table, a DB read or the unguarded route exposes long-lived page toke — `routes/web2-fb-posts.js:72 (schema) + saveToken :140; service consumes page.acce` → Encrypt tokens at rest (app-secret-derived key) or at minimum gate the route; never return user_token to client (safePages already strips page tokens — verify no path returns user_token).
- 🟡 FB image publish accepts arbitrary photo.url / video file_url forwarded straight to Graph and birefnet/photoroom accept caller-supplied bytes; publishToPage media[].url is passed to FB without host allow-listing. Low SSRF impact (FB fetches the URL, not our server) but birefnetCutout does a server-s — `web2-fb-graph-service.js:217 (uploadUnpublishedPhoto url), :251 (file_url)` → Validate media URLs are https and from expected CDN/host before forwarding; prefer bytes (dataUrl) path already supported.
- ⚪ AI caption fetches (Groq/DeepSeek/Gemini) have no AbortController/timeout, unlike fb-graph gfetch (30s) and cutout. A hung provider blocks the /caption request indefinitely. — `web2-caption-service.js:132,153,175` → Wrap each callX fetch with AbortController + ~15s timeout.
- ℹ️ match-audit.list interpolates LIMIT/OFFSET into SQL string but values are sanitized (Math.min(parseInt,500), parseInt) so not injectable; documented for completeness. — `web2-match-audit.js:106-137` → No action; keep clamps.

**Ghi chú:** All 7 services correctly use web2Db (or web2Pool||chatDb fallback at boot) and web2\_\* tables; no Web 1.0 table/pool/SSE contamination and no TPOS API dependency observed — compliant with Web 2.0 isolation rules.; ck-watcher JOINs native_orders/fast_sale_orders — these are Web 2.0-related legacy-named tables that exist as copies in web2Db (per CLAUDE.md), and db passed is web2Db, so reads stay on the correct pool. No pool mixup.; match-audit.revert is well-built: single transaction + FOR UPDATE + withdraw-before-commit (rollback on wallet failure) — the 3H17 fix is correct and addresses prior double-revert/sold-reset-but-money-not-withdrawn bug.; linkTransaction (consumed by ck-watcher) is idempotent (FOR UPDATE + debt_added re-check), which mitigates double-credit; the residual risk is the auto-REPLY/duplicate-message path, not double money.; caption/fb-graph services are pure functions (no DB, no token persistence) — security exposure is entirely in their unauthenticated consumer route web2-fb-posts.js.; Blacklist seed '75918' (ACB shop account) is a hardcoded business identifier, not a secret — acceptable.

### services:realtime-poll

This unit is the Web 2.0 livestream comment relay/fetcher plus SePay-content parsing and a one-time media migration and a reprocess cron. web2-livestream-poller is EVENT-DRIVEN ONLY: start() merely inits pools + config table and schedules a one-shot 25s backfill (reconcileRecentTruncated); the background setInterval/recursive poll loop is intentionally dead (confirmed no poll re-introduced). Comments arrive via the WS relay → POST /ingest → upsert + SSE web2:live-comments, with on-demand pollPostNow/pollNow for warm-up and tier-3 customer lookup, and reconcileFullText to repair truncated snippets. web2-content-extractor + web2-content-parser are pure helpers that extract phone/QR/name candidates and confidence scores from SePay bank content. web2-livestream-media-migrate is an idempotent one-time copy of livestream_snapshots/livestream_images from chatDb to web2Db. web2-reprocess-cron is a 10-min setInterval that re-matches unmatched SePay GD rows server-side.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** server.js:1022 (boot, !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS): web2-livestream-poller.start({web2Pool||chatDbPool, chatDbPool, web2LiveCommentsRoutes}) → ensureConfigTable() seeds 2 default pages into web2_live_poller_pages; schedules ONE setTimeout(25s) reconcileRecentTruncated({hours:48,limit:300}). NO background poll loop is scheduled.; server.js:355 (boot+8s, !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS): web2-reprocess-cron.startCron(web2Pool||chatDbPool, reprocessUnmatched, {intervalMs:600000, limit:200}) → setInterval tick every 10 min with \_running overlap guard.; server.js:1036 (boot+20s, web2Pool && !DISABLE_WEB2_JOBS): web2-livestream-media-migrate.migrate({chatPool:chatDbPool, web2Pool}) → idempotent copy of livestream_snapshots + livestream_images chatDb→web2Db, setval sequence guard, ON CONFLICT(id) DO NOTHING.; web2-content-extractor / web2-content-parser: no boot work — pure modules required lazily by web2-sepay-matching, web2-ck-watcher, web2-balance-history route, and audit script.

| Control                                                       | Handler                                          | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| poller.start({web2Pool,chatPool,liveCommentsModule})          | web2-livestream-poller.js:462 start()            | Stores pool refs into module-level \_web2Pool/\_chatPool/\_liveComments. Guards on \_started. Calls ensureConfigTable() (creates web2_live_poller_pages, seeds 2 FB pages idempotently). Schedules one-shot 25s backfill. Does NOT start any poll loop (event-driven only).                              |
| poller.pollPostNow(pageId, postId, {immediate})               | web2-livestream-poller.js:580 + \_doPollPost:555 | Debounced 1.5s per `${pageId}:${postId}` (coalesces burst via \_pollPostTimers.waiters). On fire: getTokenForPage → fetchPostComments → upsertComments(web2Pool) → \_liveComments.\_notify('poll', postId). Returns {ok,ran,fetched,saved}. NO in-flight guard across debounce windows (race risk).      |
| poller.pollNow()                                              | web2-livestream-poller.js:537 + \_cycle:402      | Runs one full cycle across all enabled pages: getActiveLivePosts → fetchPostComments → upsertComments → \_notify('poll'). Guarded by \_running (returns early if a cycle is in flight). Used by tier-3 customer lookup (web2-customers.js:435) and web2-live-comments routes.                            |
| poller.pollPostNow via POST /api/web2-live-comments/ingest    | web2-live-comments.js:320 ingest                 | WS relay posts conversations; _isSuppressedConv filters boost/page-authored; \_mapWsConvToComment maps to comment with id=`${conv.id}_${message_count}`; upsertComments; \_notify('realtime', postId per post). For truncated snippets (ends with …/...) fires poller.reconcileFullText fire-and-forget. |
| poller.reconcileFullText(pageId,postId,convId,rowId,custUuid) | web2-livestream-poller.js:606                    | In-flight guard by rowId (\_reconcileInFlight Set). Resolves custUuid via \_listPostConversations if missing; fetches conversation messages; UPDATE web2_live_comments SET message WHERE id=rowId AND message<>full; \_notify('reconcile', postId) if rowCount>0. Returns true/false.                    |
| poller.reconcileRecentTruncated({hours,limit})                | web2-livestream-poller.js:662                    | Scans web2*live_comments where message LIKE %… or %... within N hours; reconstructs convId via id.replace(/*[^_]\*$/,'') (only valid for WS-direct ids); calls reconcileFullText per row. Returns {ok,scanned,fixed}. Called once at boot+25s and manually.                                              |
| poller.listLivePostsForAssign()                               | web2-livestream-poller.js:499                    | Fetches livestream posts (14-day lookback) across enabled pages via Pancake /posts; returns sorted list for campaign-parent assignment UI. Read-only, no DB write.                                                                                                                                       |
| extractIdentifier(content)                                    | web2-content-extractor.js:52                     | Pure parse of SePay content → {type:qr_code\|exact_phone\|partial_phone\|none, value, qrCandidates, phoneCandidates}. Strips bank noise, handles MoMo/VCB, prioritizes dash-GD phones, filters test-phone blacklist + isObviousTestPhone.                                                                |
| normalizePhone(rawPhone)                                      | web2-content-extractor.js:239                    | Pure: strips non-digits, 84→0 prefix, last-10, validates 0xxxxxxxxx. Returns 10-digit or null.                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| extractPhoneCandidates / scoreConfidence / decisionTier       | web2-content-parser.js:155/254/302               | Pure SePay parsers: candidate extraction with priority, token_set_ratio name confidence scoring (inline levenshtein, no deps), tiered decision auto_high/auto_safe/review_low/pending_ambiguous. No DB, no logging of content.                                                                           |
| migrate({chatPool,web2Pool})                                  | web2-livestream-media-migrate.js:164             | \_running guard; for each of 2 tables: existence check, ensureSchema on dst via route module, setval sequence over src MAX(id), skip if dstCount>=srcCount, else keyset copy batches of 20 with ON CONFLICT(id) DO NOTHING, final setval. Does NOT drop source.                                          |
| startCron(db, reprocessFn, opts) / stopCron()                 | web2-reprocess-cron.js:30/63                     | setInterval(tick, 10min) with \_running overlap guard; tick calls reprocessFn(db,limit)=reprocessUnmatched → logs only when picked>0 && (matched\|\|pending). stopCron clears interval.                                                                                                                  |

**Liên kết:** render.com/routes/web2-live-comments.js (upsertComments, \_notify, parseUtcTs, /ingest, /poll-now, /boost-mark) — primary consumer/orchestrator of the poller, render.com/routes/v2/web2-customers.js:435 — calls poller.pollNow() for tier-3 live customer lookup, render.com/services/web2-sepay-matching.js — requires web2-content-extractor (extractIdentifier) + web2-content-parser; reprocessUnmatched is the cron's reprocessFn, render.com/services/web2-ck-watcher.js:72 — requires web2-content-extractor.extractIdentifier, render.com/routes/v2/web2-balance-history.js — requires web2-content-parser + web2-content-extractor for badge re-derivation, render.com/routes/livestream-snapshots.js + livestream-images.js — export ensureSchema used by media-migrate to create dst tables on web2Db, render.com/services/web2-pancake-refresh.js — keeps pancake_accounts JWTs (chatDb) fresh; poller reads those tokens, SSE hub realtime-sse-web2 (topic web2:live-comments) via web2RealtimeSseRoutes.notifyClients wired through web2LiveCommentsRoutes.initializeNotifiers, Pancake REST pancake.vn/api/v1 (pages/{id}/posts, conversations, messages, customers) — external read-only fetch

**SSE:** PUBLISH web2:live-comments via \_liveComments.\_notify('poll'|'realtime'|'reconcile'|'poller-pages', postId). Payload is metadata-only {action, postId} — no PII (good). Emitted after upsertComments in \_cycle (poller.js:429), \_doPollPost (572), reconcileFullText (646), and the /ingest route (web2-live-comments.js:359-361).; Debounce: pollPostNow debounces 1.5s per `${pageId}:${postId}` (POLL_POST_DEBOUNCE_MS, poller.js:554) to coalesce WS burst; client-side reload debounce is on the consumer page (not in scope). The poller does not subscribe to any SSE topic — it only publishes.; No EventSource/listener created in these service files, so no unmount cleanup concern here. Timer cleanup: \_pollPostTimers entries are deleted on fire (poller.js:592) but if the process is asked to stop there is no clearTimeout sweep for pending debounce timers or the boot backfill setTimeout (minor leak on shutdown only).

**console.log:** 27 — render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:411 console.warn includes page_id only (no token) — OK; render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:431 console.log prints page_name + post id + fetched/saved counts — operational, no PII (no phone/name/message logged) — acceptable but high-frequency during live, consider gating behind a verbose flag; render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:148 console.warn('[LIVE-POLLER] getToken DB error', e.message) — only error message, token never logged — OK; No console call in this unit logs JWTs, phone numbers, customer names, message text, or SePay content. web2-content-extractor.js and web2-content-parser.js have ZERO console calls (good — they handle PII-bearing bank content). No leftover debug noise found.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `logic` DEDUP-KEY MISMATCH between the two write paths into web2*live_comments. The WS-direct /ingest path stores rows with id = `${conv.id}*${message_count}` (see _mapWsConvToComment, routes/web2-live-comments.js:301), but the poller path (pollNow/pollPostNow → fetchPostComments → _fetchConversationComment — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:275 vs render.com/routes/web2-live` → Unify the dedup key across both paths. Best: derive a single canonical id from (pageId, message_id) which is stable regardless of fetch path, e.g. id = `${pageId}\_${messageId}` in BOTH \_fetchConversat
- 🟡 `race` The \_running overlap guard only protects \_cycle() (web2-livestream-poller.js:403). It does NOT protect \_doPollPost() (line 555). pollPostNow() (debounced) and pollNow() (which calls \_cycle) can run concurrently and both call fetchPostComments for the same post, mutating the shared \_convWatermark Map — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:297-324 (fetchPostComments) + 34-4` → Either (a) serialize per-post work with a per-post in-flight promise map (like \_pollPostTimers but keyed `${pageId}:${postId}` covering the whole fetch, not just the debounce window), or (b) make the
- 🟡 `logic` reconcileRecentTruncated recovers convId via id.replace(/_[^_]\*$/,'') (web2-livestream-poller.js:678). This is only correct for WS-direct rows whose id is `${conv.id}_${seq}`. For poller-origin rows the id is `${postId}_${messageId}`where messageId itself can contain underscores; stripping the trai —`render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:676-682` → Store the conversation id (and customer UUID) explicitly as columns on web2_live_comments rather than reconstructing it from the row id by string surgery. Then reconcileRecentTruncated can SELECT conv
- ⚪ `race` pollPostNow debounce coalescing (web2-livestream-poller.js:580-598): all callers within the 1.5s window share ONE \_doPollPost result via entry.waiters. If \_doPollPost throws synchronously before the await chain (it is wrapped in try/catch so unlikely) the waiters would hang, but more concretely: a N — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:580-598` → Add an in-flight guard per `${pageId}:${postId}` so a second debounce-fire waits for the prior \_doPollPost to settle before starting (chain the promise), preventing concurrent fetches of the same post
- ⚪ `logic` \_mapWsConvToComment falls back to seq = conv.updated_at when message_count is null (routes/web2-live-comments.js:299). updated_at is a timestamp string; if Pancake sends two WS events for the same conversation with the SAME updated_at (e.g. burst within the same second, or a re-delivery), both map t — `render.com/routes/web2-live-comments.js:299-301` → Prefer the actual Pancake message id for seq; only fall back to message_count, and avoid updated_at (a non-monotonic surrogate). If neither is present, skip the row rather than risk silent loss/duplic
- ⚪ `perf` \_custProfileCache (web2-livestream-poller.js:331) grows unbounded — there is a TTL check on read (line 371) but NO eviction of expired/old entries, unlike \_convWatermark which caps at 5000. Over a long-lived process with many distinct customers across livestreams the Map only grows; expired entries — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:331-375` → Add a size cap + oldest-eviction (mirror \_wmSet CONV_WATERMARK_MAX logic) or sweep expired entries periodically. A simple `if(_custProfileCache.size > CAP) evict oldest half` inside \_fetchCustomerProf
- ⚪ `logic` Media-migrate idempotency relies on dstCount >= srcCount to decide 'already migrated, skip' (web2-livestream-media-migrate.js:126). If the source table gains NEW rows after a partial migration (chatDb is still PROD and live-chat may still write there during the cutover window), srcCount rises and th — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-media-migrate.js:126-129` → Track a per-table migration-complete marker (e.g. a row in a web2_migrations table) instead of count comparison, or compare MAX(id) coverage rather than COUNT. At minimum keep the existing setval-befo
- ℹ️ `logic` Dead code: the adaptive-poll loop machinery (\_loop, \_scheduleNext, \_timer, \_hadLiveLastCycle, POLL_INTERVAL_LIVE_MS/IDLE_MS) is fully present but never seeded — start() intentionally does not call \_loop() (background poll disabled per project rule). \_scheduleNext is only reachable from \_loop, and \_l — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:448-460, 478-492` → Either delete the \_loop/\_scheduleNext machinery entirely (event-driven only is the rule) or add an assertion/comment guard so a future edit cannot silently restart background polling. Confirm: as-is,

**Security (audit):**

- 🟡 Pancake account JWT is read from chatDb pancake_accounts and appended to the URL as a query string (?access_token=...) on every Pancake REST call (\_pfm, line 165). Query-string tokens leak into Pancake's server logs, any intermediary proxy logs, and Node's own error stacks if a fetch URL is logged. — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:163-168` → Send the token in an Authorization/Bearer or X-Access-Token header if pancake.vn supports it, instead of the query string. If the query string is mandated by pancake.vn, ensure no code path ever conso
- ⚪ Cross-pool read of Web 1.0 store: the poller reads pancake_accounts via \_chatPool (chatDb, Web 1.0) at web2-livestream-poller.js:126. This is an allowed, documented exception (pancake_accounts is shared), but it is the one place a Web 2.0 service reaches into a chatDb table. Worth flagging because i — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:124-152` → Keep as documented shared store, but isolate the access behind a single helper so a future migration of pancake tokens to web2Db only touches one function. No change required now.
- ⚪ /ingest auth is gated by x-relay-secret === CLEANUP_SECRET, but when CLEANUP_SECRET is unset it falls through OPEN ('cho qua dev only') and merely warns (routes/web2-live-comments.js:322-330). In a misconfigured prod where the env var is missing, the ingest endpoint that writes customer comments (PI — `render.com/routes/web2-live-comments.js:320-330` → Fail closed in production: if NODE_ENV/RENDER indicates prod and CLEANUP_SECRET is unset, return 503 rather than accepting writes. Only allow the open path under an explicit dev flag.
- ⚪ PII in stored payload + SSE blast radius: comments carry phone, fb_id, name, address (web2-livestream-poller.js:274-291) and are upserted then broadcast via \_notify('poll'/'realtime'/'reconcile', postId). The \_notify payload itself only carries {action, postId} (good — no PII in SSE), but reconcileF — `render.com/services/web2-livestream-poller.js:274-291, 427-429` → No change to SSE (already metadata-only). Document that web2_live_comments is PII-bearing and the read endpoint must enforce x-web2-token. Verify the GET path for live comments is auth-gated (out of s
- ℹ️ extractIdentifier / web2-content-parser operate on SePay bank transaction CONTENT which contains customer phone fragments and names. These functions are pure (no logging of the content), and parameterized DB usage lives in the matcher, not here. No SQL injection surface in the two parser files (no s — `render.com/services/web2-content-extractor.js, render.com/services/web2-content-` → No action. Confirmed both parser modules are side-effect-free and do not log PII.
