**console.log:** 2 — web2/report-revenue/index.html:672 console.error('[reports] load error', e) — logs error object only, no PII; benign; web2/report-delivery/index.html:383 console.error('[report-delivery]', e) — logs error only, no PII; benign

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `bug` Customer-360 drill-down modal is broken: it reads summary.native.count / summary.native.totalAmount / summary.pbh.count / summary.pbh.totalAmount, but /api/web2/customer-orders returns a FLAT summary {totalNative, totalNativeAmount, totalPbh, totalPbhAmount, ...} with NO nested native/pbh objects. s — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:639-645 vs render.com/routes/v2/web2-customer-ord` → Make the modal read summary.totalNative/totalNativeAmount/totalPbh/totalPbhAmount, OR add nested native:{count,totalAmount}/pbh:{count,totalAmount} to the route summary. Pick one canonical shape.
- 🟠 `bug` Even with summary fixed, modal order rows render 0đ / wrong state: renderRows('PBH', pbh, 'number','amountTotal','state') reads it.amountTotal but route items are keyed totalAmount (not amountTotal); native uses renderRows('Đơn web', native, 'code','totalAmount','status') but items have no 'code' (k — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:648-649 vs render.com/routes/v2/web2-customer-ord` → Call renderRows('Đơn web', native, 'number','totalAmount','state') and renderRows('PBH', pbh, 'number','totalAmount','state') to match the route item keys.
- 🟡 `bug` customerId resolution off-by-length: web2-customer-orders only treats a numeric param as an ID when raw.length < 10. web2_customers.id is a serial — a 10+ digit id is mis-treated as a phone and never resolved; a 10-digit id also collides with phone format. The modal always passes a numeric customerI — `render.com/routes/v2/web2-customer-orders.js:53` → Disambiguate with an explicit ?by=id param or distinct route, or pass the row's phone (c.phone) into the modal instead of customerId.
- 🟡 `bug` TZ inconsistency in summary aggregation: week_revenue/month_revenue filter date_invoice (timestamptz) >= ((NOW() AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh')::date - INTERVAL) — left side timestamptz vs a plain date cast at session TZ (Asia/Saigon), while today_count/today_revenue correctly convert date_invoice — `render.com/routes/pbh-reports.js:43-44 and WHERE clauses at 47,148,195,248,287` → Wrap date_invoice in (date_invoice AT TIME ZONE 'Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh')::date everywhere it is compared to a +7 date, consistently.
- 🟡 `bug` report-delivery todayLocal() uses BROWSER local timezone (getFullYear/getMonth/getDate), NOT GMT+7. A user in a non-+7 timezone gets a different 'today' than the server's GMT+7 default, so the default view and 'Hôm nay' button can show the wrong day. Violates the Web 2.0 GMT+7 display rule. — `web2/report-delivery/index.html:247-250 (used at 397,409)` → Compute today with Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-CA',{timeZone:'Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh'}).format(new Date()) like the server (render.com/routes/pbh-reports.js:180).
- 🟡 `logic` summary 'today' KPI mixes sets: today_count counts ALL states (no filter) while today_revenue excludes 'cancel'. KPI card shows fmtMoney(today_revenue) with sub '${pbh.today} đơn' — order count includes cancelled orders but the money excludes them, so the two numbers describe different sets. — `render.com/routes/pbh-reports.js:40 vs :42; rendered web2/report-revenue/index.h` → Add FILTER (... AND state != 'cancel') to today_count, or relabel the count.
- ⚪ `bug` Revenue chart new Date(s.day): s.day is a Postgres ::date serialized 'YYYY-MM-DD'; new Date('YYYY-MM-DD') parses as UTC midnight, then getDate()/getMonth() render in browser-local TZ — for users behind UTC the day label can shift one earlier. Dates are already +7 day-bucketed server-side, so client — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:437-438, 447` → Split the 'YYYY-MM-DD' string directly (parts[2]/parts[1]) instead of constructing a Date, or append 'T00:00:00+07:00'.
- ⚪ `perf` loadAll runs 5 fetches in Promise.all; if ANY one rejects (non-abort) the catch wipes ALL six panels to the same error block even though others succeeded — a single slow/failing endpoint blanks the whole dashboard. — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:662-687` → Use Promise.allSettled and render each panel's error independently so partial data still shows.
- ⚪ `race` report-delivery has NO abort/sequence guard (unlike report-revenue). Rapid double-Apply (or Enter spam) can race two fetches whose responses arrive out of order, rendering stale data. loadStats also mutates the date inputs mid-load during swap. — `web2/report-delivery/index.html:356-393` → Add an AbortController + sequence guard like report-revenue, or disable Apply while a request is in flight.
- ⚪ `logic` top-customers-360 combinedTotal mixes gross native total with net PBH total: nw_total uses SUM(total_amount) with NO state filter while pbh_total excludes cancel. If native ever has a cancel/void state, combinedTotal overstates it vs the per-source columns the UI implies. — `render.com/routes/pbh-reports.js:328 (nw_total no filter) vs :341 (pbh_total fil` → Confirm native has no cancel state; if it can, apply the same status filter so combinedTotal is consistently net.
- ℹ️ `bug` Chart max uses Math.max(...d.series.map(s=>s.revenue||1)); the '||1' seeds 1 for zero-revenue points but max>0 guard already handles divide — misleading dead defensiveness; if all revenues are 0 the series.length>0 path renders all min-height bars (harmless). — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:433-436` → Drop the '||1' inside the map.

**Security (audit):**

- 🟠 GET /api/web2/customer-orders/:phoneOrId has NO auth middleware (bare router.get, unlike pbh-reports which uses requireWeb2AuthSoft). It returns customer PII: name, address, phone, full order history with amounts. report-revenue's modal also calls it WITHOUT the x-web2-token header. Any unauthentica — `render.com/routes/v2/web2-customer-orders.js:42; web2/report-revenue/index.html:` → Add router.use(requireWeb2AuthSoft) to web2-customer-orders and make the modal fetch pass AUTHH() headers like the other calls.
- ⚪ WORKER base URL is hardcoded instead of reading window.API_CONFIG.WORKER_URL / WEB2_CONFIG per the single-source convention; if the worker URL changes the page silently breaks. — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:334, web2/report-delivery/index.html:231` → Use window.API_CONFIG?.WORKER_URL || window.WEB2_CONFIG?.WORKER_URL with the literal only as fallback.
- ⚪ Third-party lucide loaded from unpkg CDN without SRI integrity hash on a page rendering customer data — supply-chain risk. — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:18, web2/report-delivery/index.html:13` → Add integrity+crossorigin (SRI) or self-host lucide via shared.
- ℹ️ x-web2-token is read from localStorage('web2_auth').token and sent on GET analytics (revenue/customer PII). Token at-rest plaintext localStorage (project-wide debt). Endpoints soft-gated so they return data without a token unless WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1. — `web2/report-revenue/index.html:346-354; web2/report-delivery/index.html:236-244;` → Acceptable read-only under current model; ensure WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 in prod so anonymous revenue/customer reads are blocked.
- ℹ️ All SQL is parameterized ($1/$2) and all interpolated customer/campaign strings pass through esc()/escapeHtml() before innerHTML. No SQL injection or stored-XSS via report data found; Number coercion guards numeric fields. — `render.com/routes/pbh-reports.js (all queries); web2/report-revenue/index.html:3` → No action; noted clean.

**Ghi chú:** Both pages are READ-ONLY dashboards — no write/mutation handlers, so WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE write-token concerns apply only to gating the GET analytics (soft-gated), not to lost mutations.; Pool usage is correct Web 2.0: web2Db || chatDb on every pbh-reports query; tables read are fast_sale_orders/native_orders/delivery_invoices/refunds/web2_customers (web2Db copies). No TPOS API coupling. No bare snake_case (Web 1.0) SSE topics.; report-delivery cross-loads Web 1.0 shared/js/notification-system.js — allowed by convention (shared/\* is shared), and is its only notification dependency. report-revenue uses window.notificationManager? optionally WITHOUT loading notification-system.js, so its error toast (web2/report-revenue/index.html:673) is a no-op unless the sidebar injects it — verify the sidebar autoloads notification-system or the 'Không tải được' toast never appears.; report-delivery includes web2-sse-bridge.js but never subscribes — stale-data UX bug (no auto-refresh). report-revenue subscribes correctly with 600ms debounce and version-guarded loadAll.; Highest-value fix: align the Customer-360 modal field keys with web2-customer-orders' actual response AND add auth middleware to that PII endpoint.

### page:returns

web2/returns/ ("Thu về" / Goods Return) is a Web 2.0 page that creates return slips and triggers wallet credits/stock changes. It has 3 tabs: Create (pick customer -> pick source order -> choose return method khach_gui/shipper_gui, issue van_de_khach/van_de_shipper, subtype khong_nhan_hang/thu_ve_1_phan, or COD-fix), List, and Pending (approve shipper_gui returns into real stock). Money/stock logic lives in the Render route render.com/routes/web2-returns.js (pool web2Db, table web2_returns, wallet via web2-wallet-service, SSE topics web2:returns + web2:products + web2:wallet:<phone>). The frontend is split into 8 small modules (core/api/customer/order-items/cod/form/tabs/app) coordinated by returns-app.js. It correctly keeps money mutations behind await+loading (not optimistic) and the backend wraps create/approve/delete in a single transaction with FOR UPDATE locks. The most serious issue found is that the entire /api/web2-returns router is mounted with NO auth middleware despite handling wallet deposits/withdrawals — any unauthenticated caller can mint wallet credit or drain stock.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** CSS: web2-sidebar.css, web2-effects.css, web2-theme.css, css/returns.css (index.html:17-20); Google Fonts preconnect + Inter/Manrope (index.html:10-15); lucide UMD icons via unpkg CDN (index.html:22, defer); shared-auth-manager.js + notification-system.js + web2-effects.js in <head> (index.html:26-28); Footer scripts: web2-auth.js, web2-user-info.js, web2-optimistic.js, web2-sidebar.js, web2-sse-bridge.js, web2-wallet-balance.js (index.html:281-286); Page modules in strict load order: returns-api -> returns-core -> returns-customer -> returns-order-items -> returns-cod -> returns-form -> returns-tabs -> returns-app (index.html:287-294); returns-app init() (app.js:131): mounts Web2Sidebar, calls bind() to attach ~20 listeners, autofocuses #custSearch after 80ms, initializes Form.onMethodChange/onIssueChange/onSubTypeChange/buildReasonSelect, calls setupSse(), reads ?tab= to switch tab, reads ?prefillPhone/prefillOrder/prefillName to auto-pick customer+order, lucide.createIcons(); setupSse (app.js:28) subscribes Web2SSE 'web2:returns' with 600ms debounce -> reloads list or pending depending on active tab; No data fetch on enter unless prefill params present; customer search / order list fetched on user interaction

| Control                                            | Handler                                                            | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| #custSearch input (typeahead)                      | Customer.onCustInput (returns-customer.js:12)                      | debounce 300ms, requires >=2 chars, GET /api/web2/customers/search?search=&limit=8, renders #custResults dropdown with esc()-escaped name/phone/id; error -> toast                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| #custResults click on .rt-opt                      | Customer.pickCustomer (returns-customer.js:41 via app.js:46)       | sets STATE.customer={phone,name,customerId}, clears sourceOrder/lines, shows formSections+rightBody, fetches wallet balance async (resets STATE.walletBalance=null first to avoid stale), calls loadCustomerOrders(), attaches wallet-balance pill, renderSummary()                                                                                                  |
| #custClear / Escape key                            | Customer.clearCustomer (returns-customer.js:87; key in app.js:111) | nulls customer/sourceOrder/lines, hides form sections, renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| radio name=method (khach_gui/shipper_gui)          | Form.onMethodChange (returns-form.js:24)                           | sets STATE.method, updates stockHint text, relabels lblCaDon, rebuilds reason dropdown, renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| radio name=issue (van_de_khach/van_de_shipper)     | Form.onIssueChange (returns-form.js:38)                            | sets STATE.issue, toggles panelKhach/panelShipper/subTypeBlock visibility, re-renders order items + cod calc/wallet, renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| radio name=subType (khong_nhan_hang/thu_ve_1_phan) | Form.onSubTypeChange (returns-form.js:56)                          | sets STATE.subType, re-renders order items (checkboxes vs read-only), renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| #reasonSelect change                               | Form.onReasonChange (returns-form.js:75)                           | sets STATE.reason, toggles reasonNoteWrap when 'khac', renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| #reasonSelectShip change                           | Form.onReasonShipChange (returns-form.js:81)                       | sets STATE.reasonShip, re-renders cod wallet panel (tru_cong_no_khach shows wallet deduction), renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| #codReduction number input                         | Cod.onCodInput (returns-cod.js:13)                                 | sets STATE.codReduction=max(0,n), renders cod calc + cod wallet, renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| #orderList click on .rt-order                      | Items.pickOrder (returns-order-items.js:13)                        | sets STATE.sourceOrder skeleton, GET /api/web2-returns/source-order/:type/:code, stale-guard checks STATE.sourceOrder.code still === code before applying, builds STATE.lines from items, renders summary+items+cod, renderSummary()                                                                                                                                 |
| #orderItems checkbox [data-line]                   | Items.toggleLine (returns-order-items.js:110)                      | sets STATE.lines[i].checked, enables/disables qty input, renderSummary() (recomputes wallet credit preview)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| #orderItems #rtSelAll button                       | inline in app.js:80                                                | toggles all STATE.lines.checked, re-renders items + summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| #orderItems qty input [data-lineqty]               | Items.setLineQty (returns-order-items.js:116)                      | clamps qty to [1, maxQty], renderSummary()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| #btnSubmit click (or Enter when create tab)        | Form.submit (returns-form.js:131)                                  | canSubmit() guard, disables btn + 'Đang lưu…', builds payload by issue/subType, awaits api.create() POST /api/web2-returns, toast success, resetForm(), switchTab('list'); error -> toast, finally re-enables btn                                                                                                                                                    |
| #listSearch input                                  | inline debounce 350ms -> Tabs.loadList (app.js:93)                 | GET /api/web2-returns/list?search=, renders table rows with wallet pills                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| #returnsBody click [data-del]                      | Tabs.removeReturn (returns-tabs.js:100)                            | Popup.danger confirm, awaits DELETE /api/web2-returns/:code (rolls back wallet+stock server-side), toast, loadList()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| #pendingList click [data-approve]                  | Tabs.approve (returns-tabs.js:163)                                 | disables btn, awaits POST /api/web2-returns/:code/approve (return_qty->stock), toast, loadPending()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| tab buttons #tab-create/list/pending               | Tabs.switchTab (returns-tabs.js:14)                                | sets STATE.tab, toggles panels, lazy-loads list or pending                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| POST /api/web2-returns (create)                    | web2-returns.js:496                                                | van_de_shipper: optional wallet withdraw (tru_cong_no_khach) before insert + \_genCode retry-on-23505; van_de_khach: single withTransaction inserts slip + \_applyStock + processDeposit (all atomic), khong_nhan_hang locks source PBH FOR UPDATE, reads fresh wallet_deducted, zero-outs it + sets stock_restored=TRUE; emits \_notify+\_notifyWallet AFTER commit |
| POST /api/web2-returns/:code/approve               | web2-returns.js:813                                                | withTransaction + FOR UPDATE lock, re-checks status/method/stock_status, moves return_qty->stock per item, sets stock_status='approved', appends history                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| DELETE /api/web2-returns/:code                     | web2-returns.js:908                                                | withTransaction + FOR UPDATE, idempotent (status<>active -> 409), reverses stock (return_qty or stock), reverses wallet (withdraw if credited>0, deposit if <0, 409 if insufficient), sets status='cancelled', restores PBH stock_restored=FALSE + wallet_deducted for khong_nhan_hang                                                                               |
| POST /api/web2-returns/:code/mark-consumed         | web2-returns.js:885                                                | UPDATE bill_status='consumed' WHERE bill_status='queued' (atomic guard prevents double consume), called by native-orders when 0đ return SP added to a PBH                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| GET /api/web2-returns/queued-by-phone/:phone       | web2-returns.js:411                                                | lists thu_ve_1_phan queued returns for a phone; consumed by web2/shared/web2-return-bill.js NativeReturnBill.collect()                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |

**Liên kết:** Render route /api/web2-returns/\* (render.com/routes/web2-returns.js) — list/pending/get/create/approve/delete/mark-consumed/source-order/queued-by-phone, Render /api/web2/customers/search (customer typeahead), Render /api/web2/customer-orders/:phone (source order list), Render /api/web2/wallets/by-phone/:phone (wallet balance), Render /api/web2-products/list (product search — imported but searchProducts unused on this page), service web2-wallet-service.processDeposit/processWithdraw (wallet mutations), Tables read/written: web2_returns, web2_products(stock/return_qty), fast_sale_orders(wallet_deducted/stock_restored), native_orders, web2_customer_wallets/web2_wallet_transactions, shared modules: web2-auth.js, web2-user-info.js, web2-optimistic.js (loaded but unused), web2-sidebar.js, web2-sse-bridge.js (Web2SSE), web2-wallet-balance.js (Web2WalletBalance pill), Popup (web2/shared/popup.js), web2/shared/web2-return-bill.js (NativeReturnBill) consumed by native-orders to bill 0đ for queued returns, Cloudflare worker WEB2_RETURNS route -> web2-api (cloudflare-worker/modules/config/routes.js:125,257)

**SSE:** SUBSCRIBED: web2:returns (returns-app.js:30) — 600ms debounce, reloads list/pending only for active tab. LEAK: the Web2SSE.subscribe in setupSse() is never unsubscribed and \_sseTimer is never cleared on page teardown; for an SPA-style page this is benign (full page reload), but the returned unsubscribe handle is discarded so repeated init() (not expected here) would stack listeners.; PUBLISHED by backend: web2:returns {action,code,ts,phone} on create/approve/cancel/consumed (web2-returns.js:41) — NOTE payload includes customer phone (PII) broadcast to every returns-page subscriber.; PUBLISHED: web2:products {action:return-\*,ts} to refresh Kho SP badges (web2-returns.js:47).; PUBLISHED: web2:wallet:<phone> {action,phone,ts} only when wallet actually changed (web2-returns.js:56). Phone is the topic key so not an extra leak.; All \_notify calls fire AFTER DB commit (correct ordering, avoids stale-read race).

**console.log:** 9 — Frontend (3, all console.warn, no PII): returns-customer.js:81, returns-app.js:135, returns-app.js:179; Backend (6, console.warn/error with only e.message, no PII/tokens): web2-returns.js:53,65,204,804,876,1054

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `race` Double-submit / concurrent create of a 'thu_ve_1_phan' (partial return) is NOT guarded. The only uniqueness backstop (uq_web2_returns_knh_active, web2-returns.js:199) covers ONLY sub_type='khong_nhan_hang'. For thu_ve_1_phan there is no source-order uniqueness and processDeposit is called with sepay — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:626-647,724-737 (no idempotency for thu_ve_1_p` → Add an idempotency/dedupe key: pass a client-generated requestId (or sourceOrderCode+productCodes hash) as processDeposit reference_id, and/or add a short-window unique guard preventing duplicate acti
- 🟡 `logic` COD-fix (van_de_shipper) withdraw happens BEFORE the slip insert and is NOT in the same transaction as the insert. processWithdraw runs on the pool (its own tx) at web2-returns.js:530, then a separate INSERT loop (with \_genCode retry) runs at 554-598. If the wallet is debited successfully but every — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:528-598` → Wrap the COD-fix withdraw + insert in a single withTransaction(pool, client => {...}) like the van_de_khach path already does (passing client to processWithdraw), so a failed insert rolls back the wit
- 🟡 `logic` Partial-return wallet credit ignores the source order: thu_ve_1_phan credits walletCredit = Σ(price×qty) from the client-supplied items/prices unconditionally (web2-returns.js:645-646), even when the source order was never paid / never deducted the wallet. Unlike khong_nhan_hang (which only credits — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:636-646` → Re-resolve item prices server-side from the source order (when sourceOrderCode present) and cap/condition the credit on what was actually paid; do not trust client-sent price for money.
- 🟡 `race` khong_nhan_hang refund amount race vs. PBH cancel: create() reads fresh wallet_deducted under FOR UPDATE (web2-returns.js:692-710) and then zero-outs it. But the source order resolved for the UI preview (\_resolveSourceOrder, native path, web2-returns.js:286-296) sums wallet_deducted across all non-c — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:1012-1041` → When restoring wallet_deducted on slip cancel for multi-PBH native orders, distribute proportionally to the original per-PBH amounts (snapshot them in the return row at create time) instead of dumping
- ⚪ `race` Stale wallet-balance gating for COD tru_cong_no_khach: pickCustomer fetches walletBalance async and canSubmit() blocks when codReduction>walletBalance (returns-form.js:120-123) using the cached STATE.walletBalance. If the balance changed server-side after fetch, the client gate is stale; the server — `returns-customer.js:71-82, returns-form.js:118-124` → On wallet fetch error, surface a retry instead of silently forcing balance=0; rely on server as source of truth and treat the client gate as advisory only.
- ⚪ `logic` resetForm() after a successful create calls loadCustomerOrders() (returns-form.js:214) which re-fetches the customer's orders, but the just-created return does not remove the source order from the list, so the same order can be immediately picked again to create a second return (especially partial r — `returns-form.js:203-215` → After create, mark/disable the consumed source order in the picker, or re-query queued state to warn the user.
- ⚪ `logic` \_genCode uses MAX(code)+1 lexically (ORDER BY code DESC, web2-returns.js:222) which breaks ordering once the daily sequence exceeds 9999 (5-digit seq sorts incorrectly vs zero-padded 4-digit), and is not atomic — mitigated by the 23505 retry loops, but the retry only loops 5 times; a sustained burst — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:214-231` → Use a DB sequence or insert-then-format-from-id; raise/relativize retry cap if volume grows.
- ℹ️ `logic` Web2Optimistic is loaded (index.html:283) but never used here — correct per project rule (money ops keep await+loading), just noting the unused dependency. searchProducts in returns-api.js:107 is also defined but unused. — `returns-api.js:107, index.html:283` → Optional cleanup; no functional impact.

**Security (audit):**

- 🔴 The /api/web2-returns router is mounted in server.js:765 with NO auth middleware (no requireWeb2Auth / requireWeb2AuthSoft / requireWeb2Admin). The route file imports no web2-auth middleware and never reads x-web2-token. Every endpoint — including POST (mint wallet credit via processDeposit), DELETE — `render.com/server.js:765 + render.com/routes/web2-returns.js (no middleware impo` → Apply requireWeb2AuthSoft (or requireWeb2Auth for writes) — either app.use('/api/web2-returns', requireWeb2AuthSoft, web2ReturnsRoutes) in server.js, or gate the mutating routes (POST '/', POST '/:cod
- 🟡 No authorization role check on approve/delete. Approving a pending return commits real inventory and cancelling reverses wallet money; these are privileged operations but there is no requireWeb2Admin even conceptually. Combined with the missing auth above, any logged-in (or anonymous) user can appro — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:813,908` → Gate approve and delete behind requireWeb2Admin (sibling web2-products uses requireWeb2Admin for privileged ops).
- ⚪ PII (customer phone) is included in the web2:returns SSE broadcast payload and fanned out to every subscriber of that topic. The list page already shows phones so exposure is limited, but per project convention SSE payloads should carry only {action,id,ts} and let clients re-fetch. — `render.com/routes/web2-returns.js:41-44` → Drop phone from the web2:returns notify payload; keep it only on the per-phone web2:wallet:<phone> topic where phone is the key.
- ℹ️ Customer/product/reason data is rendered via innerHTML throughout the frontend but all dynamic values pass through esc() (returns-core.js:12) or are numeric. No unescaped customer-controlled HTML found. SQL is fully parameterized (no string-built queries). — `returns-customer.js:23-34, returns-tabs.js:56-94, web2-returns.js (all queries $` → No action; noted as verified-clean.
- ℹ️ No hardcoded secrets. WORKER_URL falls back to a public proxy literal (returns-api.js:8) which is allowed per project base-URL convention. — `returns-api.js:8-11` → None.

**Ghi chú:** Pool usage is correct: getPool() returns web2Db||chatDb (web2-returns.js:209-211); reads native_orders/fast_sale_orders which are Web 2.0-related tables in web2Db (legacy-named but Web 2.0 per CLAUDE.md), not Web 1.0 chatDb tables. No TPOS coupling found.; GMT+7 is correct: \_genCode uses local new Date() and Render runs TZ=Asia/Saigon, so daily code dates are +7. Frontend date display slices o.date string (returns-customer.js:121) — relies on upstream formatting, not re-parsed, so no Z-suffix bug introduced here.; Transaction discipline for the van_de_khach create, approve, and delete paths is solid: single withTransaction with FOR UPDATE locks, idempotent guards (status checks, unique index, double-approve/double-cancel protection). The 3H2 PBH lifecycle wiring (zero-out wallet_deducted + stock_restored flag) is the documented fix for double-refund across the return<->PBH-cancel boundary.; Stale-guard on pickOrder (order-items.js:31) correctly aborts applying a slow source-order fetch if the user switched orders mid-load.; mark-consumed is atomic (WHERE bill_status='queued') so a queued partial return cannot be double-consumed onto two bills; partial-return billing is all-or-nothing per return code (no leftover-qty accumulation path), so 'partial-return accumulation' risk is low for billing but HIGH for duplicate-create (see bugs).

### page:settings-misc (Printer settings, Payment confirm, Delivery zone)

Three Web 2.0 "settings/misc" pages. (1) printer-settings/index.html — CRUD of POS printers + role assignment (PBH bill / SP label) with all persist logic in shared web2/shared/web2-printer.js: printer LIST stored server-side via generic entity route /api/web2/printer (table web2_printers, SSE topic web2:printer), printer-to-role MAP stored client-only in localStorage web2_printer_roles, default bridge URL in localStorage web2_print_bridge_url. Print path rasters HTML/SVG to ESC/POS or TSPL and POSTs to a localhost Print Bridge. (2) payment-confirm/ — index.html is a meta-refresh REDIRECT stub to ck-dashboard; payment-confirm-app.js (CK confirm + unread tabs over /api/web2/payment-signals and /api/web2/unread) is ORPHANED: no page loads it, ck-dashboard re-implements the feature in its own app file. (3) delivery-zone/index.html — thin wrapper that mounts the generic Web2Page CRUD on entity slug 'deliveryzone' (fee + keywords + manual/fallback flags) used by native-orders auto-detect; persistence goes through shared web2-api.js (auto x-web2-token) and page-builder.js.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** printer-settings: <link> web2-sidebar.css, web2-base.css, web2-components.css, web2-theme.css; Google Fonts Inter (render-blocking external).; printer-settings: <script defer> unpkg lucide@0.294.0 (3rd-party CDN, no SRI).; printer-settings: <script> shared-auth-manager.js, notification-system.js (Web 1.0 shared, OK).; printer-settings: <script> web2-auth.js, web2-sidebar.js, jsbarcode-code128.min.js, qrcode.min.js, receiptline.js, web2-bill-service.js, web2-sse-bridge.js, web2-optimistic.js, web2-printer.js, web2-pos-installer.js.; printer-settings init (inline): Web2Sidebar.mount(activeRoute=printer-settings); lucide.createIcons() on DOMContentLoaded; wireBridgeSetting() seeds #bridgeUrlSetting from localStorage; renderPrinters()+renderRoles() from cache; P.onPrintersChanged(re-render listener registered); async IIFE await P.loadPrinters() then re-render + bridgeAlive() probe to toggle #bridgeWarn.; web2-printer.js module boot (runs on import): seeds \_printers from localStorage web2_printers_cache; \_migrateToServer() (loadPrinters then push legacy local printers if server empty); Web2SSE.subscribe('web2:printer', loadPrinters) if bridge present.; delivery-zone: <link> sidebar/base/components/page-builder/theme CSS + Inter/Manrope fonts; lucide CDN; shared-auth-manager.js, notification-system.js; web2-auth.js, web2-user-info.js, web2-history-timeline.js, web2-db-badge.js, web2-optimistic.js, web2-sidebar.js, web2-api.js, web2-sse-bridge.js, page-builder.js.; delivery-zone init: Web2Sidebar.mount(activeRoute=deliveryzone); lucide.createIcons(); Web2Page.mount('#pageRoot', slug='deliveryzone', columns/fields...) — page-builder fetches list from /api/web2/deliveryzone and subscribes SSE web2:deliveryzone internally.; payment-confirm/index.html: meta http-equiv refresh + location.replace to ../ck-dashboard/index.html (preserves query). Loads only web2-theme.css. Does NOT load payment-confirm-app.js.; payment-confirm-app.js (orphan, not loaded anywhere): init() would mount sidebar, wire tabs/status select/refresh, reloadNow() fetch signals, subscribe web2:payment-signals + web2:unread — but the page never executes it.

| Control                                                                                                 | Handler                                                                         | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| printer-settings: #bridgeUrlSetting input change                                                        | wireBridgeSetting closure (index.html:542)                                      | Trims value; writes localStorage web2_print_bridge_url (removes key if blank/equals fallback); toast. Client-local only, no server/SSE — bridge URL NOT shared across machines (by design: per-POS).                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| printer-settings: #btnAddPrinter click                                                                  | openModal(null) (index.html:638)                                                | Opens modal in add mode; defaults method=bridge, paper=80, port=9100, bridge=defaultBridgeUrl(), gap=2; syncBridge() toggles IP/bridge + label-gap field visibility.                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| printer-settings: #pm_method / #pm_paper change                                                         | syncBridge (index.html:614)                                                     | Shows #pmBridgeFields when method=bridge; shows #pmLabelFields (gap mm) when paper=label.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| printer-settings: #pmSave click                                                                         | inline async (index.html:644)                                                   | Guards double-submit via \_savingPrinter + disabled btn; validates IP required for bridge method; builds printer obj; Web2Optimistic.run{apply:closeModal+renderPrinters+renderRoles, run:P.upsertPrinter(p) -> POST/PATCH /api/web2/printer with x-web2-token then loadPrinters(), rollback:re-render+done, onSuccess:done}. Server publishes SSE web2:printer (action create/update). |
| printer-settings: #prnList [data-act=edit]                                                              | delegated click (index.html:714)                                                | openModal(P.getPrinter(id)) prefilled.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| printer-settings: #prnList [data-act=del]                                                               | delegated click (index.html:720)                                                | Native confirm(); UI-first hide card; Web2Optimistic.run{run:P.removePrinter(id) -> DELETE /api/web2/printer/delete/:id with x-web2-token + strips local roles pointing to id + loadPrinters, rollback:show card again}. SSE web2:printer delete.                                                                                                                                       |
| printer-settings: #prnList [data-act=test]                                                              | delegated click (index.html:758)                                                | \_btnCooldown(2s); P.bridgeAlive(p) then P.testConnection(p) -> POST {bridgeUrl}/tcp-test {ip,port}; toast OK/err. Talks to localhost bridge only.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| printer-settings: #prnList [data-act=print]                                                             | delegated click (index.html:772)                                                | \_btnCooldown(2s); builds sample bill (fake phone 0900000000); Web2Bill.generateHTML -> for bridge: bridgeAlive then P.printHtml (label/tspl) or P.printBillHtml (bill) -> raster -> POST {bridgeUrl}/print {ip,port,b64}; else Web2Bill.openPrint dialog.                                                                                                                              |
| printer-settings: role <select data-role> change                                                        | renderRoles closure (index.html:606)                                            | P.setRole(roleKey, printerId) writes localStorage web2_printer_roles; toast. Client-local only (per-POS role mapping), no server, no SSE.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| printer-settings: #btnDownloadBat / #btnDownloadPs1 / #btnUninstallBat                                  | inline click (index.html:829/837/849)                                           | Web2PosInstaller.downloadInstaller()/downloadUninstaller() build .bat blob download; PS1 fetches ../../scripts/print-bridge.ps1 relative and downloads. Client-side blob, no server mutation.                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| delivery-zone: add/edit/delete row, field save (Ma/Ten/short/fee/keywords/manual/isFallback)            | Web2Page (page-builder.js) generic CRUD on slug deliveryzone                    | Create POST /api/web2/deliveryzone/create, update PATCH /update/:code, delete DELETE /delete/:code via web2-api.js (auto x-web2-token); 'code' field readonly on edit (reference key for saved orders); server publishes SSE web2:deliveryzone; native-orders DeliveryMethodPicker reads this entity as source of truth.                                                                |
| payment-confirm-app.js: confirm/dismiss/link/tab/status/refresh (ORPHAN — never wired into a live page) | doAction/linkOrder/switchTab/reloadNow (payment-confirm-app.js:241/293/330/346) | Would POST /api/web2/payment-signals/:id/{confirm\|dismiss\|link-order} with x-web2-token + Web2Optimistic optimistic status flip; but this file is dead code (page redirects to ck-dashboard which reimplements).                                                                                                                                                                      |

**Liên kết:** web2/ck-dashboard/index.html (payment-confirm redirects here; ck-dashboard-app.js reimplements payment-signals + unread), web2/native-orders/index.html (delivery-zone feeds DeliveryMethodPicker auto-detect; printer roles drive PBH printing; payment-confirm orderLink -> native-orders?code=), web2/web2-pancake/index.html (payment-confirm orderLink for fast_sale/PBH), shared: web2/shared/web2-printer.js, web2-bill-service.js, web2-optimistic.js, web2-sse-bridge.js, web2-auth.js, web2-sidebar.js, web2-pos-installer.js, web2-api.js, page-builder.js, web2-wallet-balance.js, web2-user-info.js, web2-history-timeline.js, render routes: /api/web2/printer (web2-dedicated-entity makeDedicatedEntityRouter on web2_printers), /api/web2/deliveryzone (web2-generic), /api/web2/payment-signals, /api/web2/unread, scripts/print-bridge.ps1, scripts/print-bridge.js (localhost Print Bridge talked to over http://127.0.0.1:17777)

**SSE:** printer-settings (via web2-printer.js): SUBSCRIBE web2:printer -> loadPrinters() (no debounce; each event triggers a full /list fetch). Subscription registered once at module load, guarded by `if (global.Web2Printer) return;` singleton. NO unsubscribe/cleanup — page is full-load nav so acceptable, but listener fires loadPrinters on every event with no debounce (burst -> N fetches).; printer-settings: server publishes web2:printer on create/update/delete/delete-all via web2-dedicated-entity.\_notify (correct web2 hub).; delivery-zone (via page-builder.js): SUBSCRIBE web2:deliveryzone internally; server publishes via web2-generic \_notify. Debounce/cleanup handled inside page-builder (out of scope here).; payment-confirm-app.js (orphan): would SUBSCRIBE web2:payment-signals (reload, 250ms debounce via reload()) and web2:unread (reloadUnread, no debounce). Never active because file is not loaded. NO unsubscribe in either (Web2SSE.subscribe return value ignored).

**console.log:** 1 — web2/payment-confirm/js/payment-confirm-app.js:55 — console.log('[payment-confirm]', msg) fallback toast (only when notificationManager missing; low noise, no PII; and file is dead code anyway).

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟡 `other` DEAD CODE: web2/payment-confirm/js/payment-confirm-app.js (402 lines) is never loaded by any page. payment-confirm/index.html is a redirect stub to ck-dashboard, and ck-dashboard/js/ck-dashboard-app.js reimplements payment-signals + unread. The orphan references DOM ids (#pcSignals, #pcStatus, #pmOv — `web2/payment-confirm/js/payment-confirm-app.js:1-402 (loaded by: none)` → Delete payment-confirm/js + css (and redirect stub if unused), or repoint; document ck-dashboard as the single source. Minimum: add a retired banner comment.
- 🟡 `race` Printer save stuck-button: #pmSave sets \_savingPrinter=true + saveBtn.disabled=true and relies on Web2Optimistic.run calling done() via onSuccess OR rollback. Web2Optimistic.run only fires onSuccess on resolve / rollback on reject (web2-optimistic.js:86/94). If P.upsertPrinter()'s fetch hangs foreve — `web2/printer-settings/index.html:657-701; web2/shared/web2-optimistic.js:83-105` → Reset the flag on settle regardless of outcome (wrap run in a finally) or add AbortSignal.timeout to upsertPrinter fetch and reset \_savingPrinter via a timeout guard.
- ⚪ `other` Optimistic success toast 'Da luu may in (server)' is shown by Web2Optimistic.run at apply() time (web2-optimistic.js:81 via successMsg) BEFORE the server PATCH/POST resolves. On a later server failure the user already saw success, then sees the rollback error toast — confusing for a config persist a — `web2/printer-settings/index.html:687; web2/shared/web2-optimistic.js:81` → For shared-config save/delete prefer awaiting the server or move successMsg to onSuccess (CLAUDE.md already advises await for strict-validation saves).
- ⚪ `race` web2:printer SSE handler calls loadPrinters() with NO debounce (web2-printer.js:700). A burst of create/update/delete events from another machine triggers N concurrent /list fetches; whichever resolves last wins and \_printers/localStorage can flip-flop with stale ordering if responses arrive out of — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:698-701` → Debounce loadPrinters (300-500ms) and ignore out-of-order responses (track a request seq, apply only the latest).
- ⚪ `other` Printer-to-role mapping + default bridge URL stored ONLY in localStorage (web2_printer_roles, web2_print_bridge_url), not server-synced. Intentional (per-POS), but a printer deleted on machine A leaves a dangling role id in machine B's localStorage; getPrinterFor falls back to printers[0], so machin — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:191-202; index.html:606` → On loadPrinters, prune local roles whose printerId no longer exists in the list (mirror removePrinter's local cleanup) so a remote delete doesn't silently re-route to printers[0].
- ⚪ `logic` \_migrateToServer runs on every module load until web2_printers_migrated flag is set and calls loadPrinters(); the page's inline IIFE (index.html:864) also calls loadPrinters() at boot, so two /list fetches race on first load. Harmless but redundant double fetch. — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:205-226,697; web2/printer-settings/index.html:864` → Let the page rely on the module's load, or dedupe concurrent loadPrinters calls with an in-flight promise.
- ℹ️ `other` upsertPrinter MUTATES the caller's printer object in place (p.id = \_genId()) when id missing (web2-printer.js:131), violating the project immutability convention; the inline save handler's local p object is mutated mid-flight. No functional bug but breaks the immutable-update rule. — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:131` → Clone before assigning id: const rec = {...p, id: p.id || \_genId()}.
- ℹ️ `memory-leak` In the orphan payment-confirm-app.js the Web2SSE.subscribe return value is ignored with no unsubscribe; linkOrder() uses a plain fetch (not optimistic) then reload(). Acceptable now (dead code) but would be a listener-leak/inconsistency if revived. — `web2/payment-confirm/js/payment-confirm-app.js:387-394,303-321` → If revived, capture and clean up SSE subscriptions; otherwise delete the file.

**Security (audit):**

- ⚪ printer-settings delete uses native confirm() instead of window.Popup (project convention requires Web2 Popup for confirm). Printer name is interpolated into the confirm() string — confirm() does not render HTML so not XSS, just a convention/UX gap. — `web2/printer-settings/index.html:721` → Use await window.Popup.confirm(...) per CLAUDE.md Web2 Popup rule for consistent UX and to avoid the blocking native dialog.
- ⚪ Printer LIST read is fetched with credentials:'omit' and no x-web2-token (web2-printer.js loadPrinters /list). Printer config (IP/port/bridge URL = internal LAN topology) is exposed read-only without auth if the /api/web2/printer/list route is unauthenticated. — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:109` → Confirm /api/web2/printer/list requires auth server-side; if reads must be public, accept LAN-IP exposure as low risk, else add x-web2-token to the read.
- ⚪ Bridge URL is user-controllable (localStorage / per-printer bridgeUrl) and fetched directly (printEscpos/testConnection/bridgeAlive POST to printer.bridgeUrl). A malicious bridgeUrl value lets the page POST raster bytes / {ip,port} to an arbitrary origin (client-side SSRF-ish). Scope limited because — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:410,650,660` → Validate bridgeUrl against a localhost/LAN allowlist before POSTing; reject external hosts.
- ℹ️ x-web2-token read from localStorage 'web2_auth' in ad-hoc fallbacks (token at-rest plaintext). Consistent with rest of Web 2.0 (known accepted debt). — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:48, web2/payment-confirm/js/payment-confirm-app.js:6` → Track with existing project-wide token-at-rest item; no per-page action.
- ℹ️ 3rd-party CDN scripts (lucide unpkg; html2canvas jsdelivr lazy-loaded in web2-printer.js) without SRI. Standard for this codebase. — `web2/printer-settings/index.html:18; web2/shared/web2-printer.js:363` → Add SRI integrity hashes or self-host for supply-chain hardening.
- ℹ️ delivery-zone and printer-settings writes correctly carry x-web2-token (web2-api.js \_authHeaders, web2-printer.js \_w2Auth), so WEB2_AUTH_ENFORCE=1 will not silently 401 these mutations. — `web2/shared/web2-printer.js:156,172; web2/shared/web2-api.js:24` → No action.

**Ghi chú:** No TPOS coupling and no Web 1.0 pool/table/SSE coupling found in any of the three pages — all writes target /api/web2/\* (web2Db) and the web2 SSE hub (web2:printer, web2:deliveryzone, web2:payment-signals/web2:unread). shared-auth-manager.js + notification-system.js are the legitimate cross-layer shared modules.; No GMT+7 timezone handling is needed in printer-settings or delivery-zone (no timestamp display). payment-confirm-app.js fmtTime relies on Web2Format.dateTime / toLocaleString without forcing Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh, but the file is dead code; ck-dashboard's live version should be audited for the +7 rule instead.; XSS posture is good: printer-settings esc() escapes &<>" before innerHTML; payment-confirm esc() uses Web2Escape/textContent. Printer name/IP and signal customerName/rawMessage are escaped before interpolation.; No hardcoded secrets. Bridge URL fallback http://127.0.0.1:17777 and proxy/worker URL fallbacks are non-secret config.; Print bytes are sent only to a localhost bridge; no Facebook/Pancake/Zalo calls anywhere in this slice.

### page:so-order

Sổ Order (so-order/) is the Web 2.0 purchasing ledger: shop staff record orders bought from suppliers (NCC), organized as tabs (per country/currency) → shipments (lô, by date+batch) → rows (products). Data is a single JSONB doc 'main' in Postgres web2_so_order (web2Db, /api/web2-so-order), mirrored to IndexedDB (Web2IdbStore) as a local-first cache, with optimistic-concurrency push (version) and SSE topic web2:so-order. On "Nhận hàng" (receive) it links to the Web 2.0 product warehouse (Web2ProductsApi.upsertPending + confirm-purchase-partial) and prints QR/barcode labels; product codes for new SP are generated client-side via Web2ProductCode (\_assignKhoCodes) keyed off the active TAB label, not the per-row NCC. It is correctly isolated from Web 1.0/TPOS. The architecture's main weakness is a single shared document + full-state overwrite push, which makes multi-user/multi-tab concurrency lossy.

**Tải khi vào trang / boot:** index.html head: Google Fonts (Inter/Manrope), lucide UMD (defer), shared/js/notification-system.js, web2/shared/web2-effects.js, web2-db-badge.js; CSS web2-sidebar/effects/components, products-print, so-order.css, web2-import.css, web2-theme.css; Body scripts (ordered): web2-auth.js (sets window.API_CONFIG/WEB2_CONFIG base URLs), web2-optimistic.js, web2-sidebar.js (DOMContentLoaded → Web2Sidebar.mount('#web2Aside',{activeRoute:'so-order'}) which auto-loads Lottie/popup/command-palette/notification), web2-products-api, web2-printer, web2-qr, web2-products-print, web2-variants-api, web2-sse-bridge, web2-idb-store, web2-products-cache, web2-variants-cache, web2-variant-multi, web2-suppliers-cache, web2-product-code, web2-deeplink, web2-barcode-scanner, web2-label-ocr, web2-import; so-order data layer: so-order-storage.js then so-order-storage-sync.js (Sync attaches to SoOrderStorage.Sync); so-order app modules (state→format→render→...→app.js orchestrator LAST); SO.init(): renders skeleton; await SoOrderStorage.load() (IDB read + migrateTab heal + 7-day trash purge); applyEditTableModeUi; renderAll; \_applyDeeplink IIFE (reads ?tab=/?supplier= via Web2Deeplink, retries up to 6× / 2.4s re-asserting activeTabId+collapsed and scrolling to row); wireToolbar/wireInlineImageModal/wireModalTotals/wireFooterInputs; lucide.createIcons; init async: Web2ProductsCache.init().then(subscribe → updateRowMeta for open modal rows); Web2VariantsCache.init(); Web2SuppliersCache.init().catch; SoOrderStorage.Sync.init(remoteHandler, conflictHandler) → \_loadFromServer (GET /api/web2-so-order/get) → seed IDB+state → \_subscribeSSE('web2:so-order') → re-load+renderAll+pushSync (push first-visit migration back); adds visibilitychange (visible→pullOnce / hidden→flush), window focus→pullOnce, beforeunload→flush listeners

| Control                                                             | Handler                                                                                                 | Sau đó                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Tab pill click (renderTabStrip)                                     | so-order-render.js:41 inline                                                                            | sets SO.state.activeTabId, SoOrderStorage.save (IDB), pushSync (writes WHOLE state incl activeTabId to server), renderAll. Side-effect: activeTabId is part of the synced doc → broadcasts your tab switch to all machines.                                                                     |
| Shipment header click (toggle collapse)                             | so-order-render.js:94 inline                                                                            | updateShipment{collapsed:!collapsed}, pushSync, renderAll                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Receive button (data-shipment-action=receive / receive-ncc)         | openReceiveShipmentModal so-order-receive.js:136                                                        | builds SO.\_receiveItems from eligible rows, inserts inline panel row, hides other shipments, fire-and-forget \_lookupProductStateForRows (Web2ProductsCache) to patch 'đã nhận/còn chờ', wires Esc/close/confirm/print                                                                         |
| Confirm receive (#soReceiveConfirmBtn)                              | confirmReceiveFromModal so-order-receive.js:531                                                         | await refresh+lookup fresh pending, upsertPending (delta only), confirm-purchase-partial POST (hardcoded worker URL, SO.\_w2Auth header), updateRow status received/partial_received + qtyReceived, pushSync, renderAll, opens barcode print modal. Keeps await+spinner (documented exception). |
| Edit/Add/Delete shipment buttons                                    | so-order-render.js:106 inline → openShipmentModal/openOrderModal/deleteShipment                         | opens edit-shipment modal (loads non-received rows) / opens create modal scoped to shipment / deleteShipment (stock-check confirm → soft-delete to trash if all received else hard delete + adjustKhoPending)                                                                                   |
| Row edit/delete (data-row-action)                                   | so-order-render.js:130 inline → openOrderModal/deleteRow                                                | edit opens single-row modal; delete runs stock check, confirm popup, deleteRow, adjustKhoPending(-qty), pushSync, renderAll. Guarded by SO.\_pendingDeleteKeys against double-click.                                                                                                            |
| Order form submit (#soOrderForm)                                    | handleOrderSubmit so-order-modal-submit.js:9                                                            | branches create / edit-row / edit-shipment; addRow/updateRow/moveRow/setOrderAdjustment in storage, \_explodeVariants multi-variant split, syncRowsToKho/adjustKhoPending (fire-and-forget), hideModal, pushSync, renderAll                                                                     |
| Cell double-click (tbody delegated)                                 | onCellDoubleClick so-order-inline-edit.js:9 → beginInlineCellEdit                                       | replaces td with input/select; commit on change/blur(150ms)/Enter → updateRow + adjustKhoPending(qty delta) + pushSync + renderAll; Esc restores. Guarded by SO.inlineCellEditingKey.                                                                                                           |
| Whole-table edit inputs (tbody change/keydown/focusin delegated)    | onBulkEditChange so-order-bulk-edit.js:9 → commitBulkEditField:45                                       | updateRow{[field]:value}, qty delta → adjustKhoPending, supplier → \_ensureSupplierAsync, pushSync, renderFooterTotals, flashRow. NOTE: no-op skip if r[field]===value.                                                                                                                         |
| Quét mã / Đọc nhãn (#soModalScanBtn/#soModalOcrBtn)                 | so-order-modal-core.js:305/320 → Web2BarcodeScanner.open/Web2LabelOcr.open → \_addRowFromScannedCode:38 | on each scan: findByCode in Web2ProductsCache → push new modal row (prefill name+matchedCode) → renderModalRows → toast. continuous:true for camera.                                                                                                                                            |
| Supplier picker input (modal/inline)                                | attachSupplierPickerOnDemand so-order-inline-edit.js:283                                                | renders dropdown from Web2SuppliersCache.search + current-state suppliers; onPick → set value + onPick callback (\_ensureSupplierWithFeedback creates NCC via cache.ensure → /api/web2-supplier-wallet/suppliers)                                                                               |
| Product-name suggest (portal float panel)                           | showSuggest so-order-modal-suggest.js:9 → applySuggestionToRow:256                                      | findByName in Web2ProductsCache; pick → set name+matchedCode, convert kho VND price → tab currency via fromVnd, autofill variant/image, renderModalRows                                                                                                                                         |
| Tab settings submit / delete (#soTabSettingsForm / #soTabDeleteBtn) | handleTabSettingsSubmit so-order-settings.js:70 / handleTabDelete:98                                    | addTab/updateTab (label/currency/rate/shipMetaFields) or deleteTab (after stock-check confirm + adjustKhoPending for all rows), pushSync, renderAll                                                                                                                                             |
| Import / Tải mẫu (#soImportBtn/#soSampleBtn)                        | so-order-toolbar.js:39 → Web2Import.open(\_soImportConfig) → \_commitSoImport so-order-import.js:153    | groups CSV/JSON rows by supplier+date+batch → addShipment+addRow per group, \_ensureSupplierAsync, syncRowsToKho fire-and-forget, pushSync, renderAll                                                                                                                                           |
| Inline image modal save (#soInlineImageSaveBtn)                     | \_saveInlineImage so-order-image-modal.js:60                                                            | invoiceImage → updateInvoiceImageForGroup (all rows same invoiceGroupId); productImage → updateRow; pushSync, renderAll, flashRow                                                                                                                                                               |
| Trash restore/purge                                                 | handleTrashRestore/handleTrashPurge so-order-delete.js:300/313                                          | restoreFromTrash → tab.shipments.push (or tab[0] if origin gone) / purgeFromTrash; pushSync, renderAll, updateTrashCountBadge                                                                                                                                                                   |
| GET /api/web2-so-order/get                                          | render.com/routes/web2-so-order.js:66 (requireWeb2AuthSoft)                                             | returns {data,version,lastUpdated,empty} for doc 'main' from web2Db; ensureTables first (idempotent via WeakSet)                                                                                                                                                                                |
| POST /api/web2-so-order/save {data,baseVersion}                     | web2-so-order.js:97 (requireWeb2AuthSoft)                                                               | baseVersion<=0 → INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING (else 409+server); >0 → UPDATE WHERE version=baseVersion (else 409+server); on success \_notify('update',v) SSE web2:so-order. Stores ENTIRE state blob.                                                                                         |
| POST /api/web2-so-order/reset                                       | web2-so-order.js:177 (requireWeb2Admin)                                                                 | DELETE doc 'main', \_notify('reset',0). Admin-gated.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |

**Liên kết:** web2/shared/web2-auth.js (base URL + x-web2-token), web2/shared/web2-optimistic.js (loaded but NOT used here — kho-sync intentionally bypasses it), web2/shared/web2-sidebar.js (mount, auto-loads popup/lottie/command-palette/notification), web2/shared/web2-sse-bridge.js (Web2SSE.subscribe web2:so-order), web2/shared/web2-idb-store.js (Web2IdbStore IDB cache), web2/shared/web2-products-cache.js + web2/products/js/web2-products-api.js (warehouse link: upsertPending, adjustPending, confirm-purchase-partial), web2/shared/web2-variants-cache.js + web2-variant-multi.js (variant picker + multi-variant explode), web2/shared/web2-suppliers-cache.js → /api/web2-supplier-wallet/suppliers (NCC source = supplier-wallet page), web2/shared/web2-product-code.js (\_assignKhoCodes SP code generation), web2/shared/web2-printer.js + web2-qr.js + web2/products/js/web2-products-print.js (label/QR print), web2/shared/web2-barcode-scanner.js + web2-label-ocr.js (camera scan / OCR add SP), web2/shared/web2-import.js (CSV/JSON import), web2/shared/web2-deeplink.js (?tab/?supplier deep-link + product link to web2/products), web2/variants/index.html (link from empty variant dropdown), render.com/routes/web2-so-order.js (Postgres web2Db storage), render.com/routes/realtime-sse-web2.js (web2:so-order topic hub)

**SSE:** Subscribes web2:so-order (so-order-storage-sync.js:108) — callback ignores events whose version <= \_localVersion (echo guard) then pullOnce(). NO debounce on the SSE callback itself (each event triggers a GET).; Publishes web2:so-order from server \_notify on create/update/reset (web2-so-order.js:33). Payload is {action,version,ts} only — no PII, good.; Cleanup gap: Sync.teardown() removes the \_sseUnsub but app.js NEVER calls teardown() (no pagehide/unmount). EventSource via Web2SSE bridge persists; acceptable for a full page nav but the SSE subscription is never explicitly released.; pullOnce is also fired on visibilitychange(visible) and window focus; visibilitychange(hidden)+beforeunload call flush().

**console.log:** 25 — so-order-kho-sync.js:186,221 console.warn includes e.message (network error text only, no PII); so-order-storage-sync.js:80,100,180,183 console.warn for init/load/push failures (status/error text only); so-order-storage.js:263,340,361,378 console.warn/error for IDB read/write failures; No console.\* logs customer data, tokens, or SĐT. No leftover console.log debug noise — all are warn/error in catch blocks. Acceptable.

**Bugs (audit):**

- 🟠 `data-loss` Whole-document last-writer-wins via full-state overwrite. Every mutation pushes the ENTIRE state object to the single shared doc 'main'. On a 409 conflict, \_flushPending only updates \_localVersion to the server's version and warns the user — it does NOT merge. The next debounced push then writes the — `so-order-storage-sync.js:151-185 (_flushPending), web2-so-order.js:143-169` → Either (a) make conflict resolution merge per-tab/per-shipment instead of overwriting the whole doc, or (b) split the doc per-tab so concurrent edits to different tabs don't collide, or (c) on 409 for
- 🟠 `race` SSE/focus pullOnce can clobber in-flight local edits. pullOnce applies the server doc whenever loaded.version > \_localVersion AND there is no pending push timer. \_flushPending clears \_pushTimer (line 145) BEFORE awaiting the POST, so a concurrent SSE event or window-focus during the in-flight POST s — `so-order-storage-sync.js:121-137 (pullOnce) vs 144-185 (_flushPending clears _pu` → Guard pullOnce with an explicit \_flushInFlight boolean set true around the await in \_flushPending; do not apply remote state while a push is in flight. Or queue remote updates and apply only after the
- 🟠 `data-loss` activeTabId is part of the synced document. Switching tabs writes activeTabId into state and pushes it; a pullOnce on another machine/tab yanks the active tab to the remote value, and the remoteHandler calls renderAll() which destroys any open inline-cell-edit / modal in-progress on the other machin — `so-order-render.js:42-45 (tab switch pushes), so-order-storage-sync.js:135 (remo` → Treat activeTabId (and per-device editTableMode, already excluded) as PER-DEVICE UI state, not synced. Strip activeTabId from the pushed payload and persist it only in localStorage/IDB locally.
- 🟡 `race` renderAll() during remote/SSE update destroys open modals and inline edits. remoteHandler (so-order-app.js:166) and pullOnce both call SO.renderAll() unconditionally. If a receive panel, order modal, or inline cell edit is open when a remote update arrives, the tbody innerHTML is rebuilt, the open i — `so-order-app.js:166-168, so-order-storage-sync.js:135` → Skip/defer renderAll() when a modal/inline edit/receive panel is open (check SO.inlineCellEditingKey, soOrderModal not hidden, .so-receive-panel-row present); apply after the modal closes.
- 🟡 `memory-leak` Receive panel Esc keydown handler can leak. openReceiveShipmentModal adds a document keydown escHandler removed only in closePanel; but if a remote renderAll() removes the panel row without calling closePanel, the escHandler stays bound to document forever (and would call closePanel on a detached no — `so-order-receive.js:308-320 (escHandler) vs renderTableBody removing .so-receive` → Track the escHandler on the panelRow and remove it whenever the panel is removed (MutationObserver, or remove stale .so-receive-panel-row + listener at the start of renderTableBody).
- 🟡 `logic` \_assignKhoCodes existingCodes dedupe counter is seeded only from Web2ProductsCache.getAll() at call time. Two near-simultaneous syncs (e.g. Lưu Nháp then immediate receive, or two receive panels) each compute codes from the same stale cache snapshot and can generate the SAME code for two different n — `so-order-kho-sync.js:56-123, 173; so-order-receive.js:610; so-order-barcode.js:5` → Let the server own final code uniqueness (client code is a hint); accumulate generated codes across calls within a session and rely on server collision handling (owner-matching already partially prese
- 🟡 `race` Double-submit of order form not guarded. handleOrderSubmit has no in-flight lock or button-disable; rapid double Enter/click on Lưu submits twice → addShipment/addRow runs twice creating duplicate shipments/rows (each gets a fresh invoiceGroupId so they aren't merged). Delete actions ARE guarded (SO — `so-order-modal-submit.js:9 (no guard), contrast so-order-delete.js:33` → Disable the submit button / set an in-flight flag at the start of handleOrderSubmit and clear in finally / after hideModal.
- ⚪ `race` \_receiveLookupCache TTL race: cached pendingQty (5s) may be stale relative to the fresh refresh done inside confirmReceiveFromModal, so the panel can show 'còn chờ M' from cache while confirm computes upsertQty from a fresher number if another machine received the same SP within the 5s window. — `so-order-receive.js:193-228, 570-592` → Acceptable (confirm always refreshes); optionally invalidate \_receiveLookupCache on SSE web2:products events.
- ⚪ `logic` Migration \_metaMigrated check has a confusing dead-code expression: `Number(a.weightKg) || 0 || Number(a.caseCount) || 0 || ...` — the interleaved `|| 0` terms are dead (0 is falsy and chains on). It mostly still computes 'any field nonzero' correctly but is fragile and could mask a future edit. — `so-order-storage.js:200-208` → Rewrite as Number(a.weightKg)||Number(a.caseCount)||Number(a.contractAmount) without interleaved ||0.
- ⚪ `logic` Ship dates use new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) which is the UTC date, not GMT+7. Near midnight Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh the default ship date / 'today' grouping can land on the wrong calendar day (e.g. 06:00 local = 23:00 UTC previous day → ISO date is the previous day), violating the Web 2.0 GMT+7 dis — `so-order-storage.js:114,151,450; so-order-modal-open.js:84; so-order-modal-submi` → Compute 'today'/default ship date in Asia/Ho_Chi_Minh (Intl.DateTimeFormat with timeZone or +7 offset) rather than toISOString.
- ⚪ `other` Closing the order modal via ESC (toolbar global handler) or backdrop [data-so-close] sets hidden directly and does NOT call SO.hideModal('soOrderModal'), so SO.\_hideFloatPanels() never runs → the portal suggest/variant float panels appended to <body> can stay visible/lingering after the modal is dis — `so-order-toolbar.js:75-90 (data-so-close + ESC use setAttribute/hidden=true dire` → In the ESC and data-so-close handlers, if the closed element is #soOrderModal call SO.\_hideFloatPanels() (or route all closes through SO.hideModal).

**Security (audit):**

- ⚪ Backend /save stores the ENTIRE client-supplied state blob with no schema validation beyond typeof==='object'. A malicious/buggy client can write arbitrary JSON (e.g. enormous base64 images) into web2_so_order.data with no size cap, and arbitrary fields persist. Beta data so low impact, but unbounde — `render.com/routes/web2-so-order.js:101-104` → Validate shape (tabs is array, cap doc byte size e.g. reject >5MB), strip unknown top-level keys.
- ⚪ Image URLs from paste/drop are inserted into <img src> via escapeHtml only (attribute-safe). A javascript: URL in img src does not execute, but a data:text/html or external tracker URL could be stored and rendered. base64 data URLs are also stored verbatim in the synced doc. — `so-order-render-cells.js:147, so-order-image-modal.js:52, so-order-modal-suggest` → Optionally validate URL scheme (allow http(s)/data:image only) before storing.
- ℹ️ confirm-purchase-partial and the Sync base URL are hardcoded to chatomni-proxy.workers.dev instead of reading window.API_CONFIG.WORKER_URL (the project's single-source base-URL convention per web2-auth.js). Sync uses API_CONFIG with hardcoded fallback (ok); receive.js:652 is a pure hardcode. — `so-order-receive.js:652, so-order-storage-sync.js:39` → Use window.API_CONFIG.WORKER_URL in receive.js confirm-purchase-partial fetch for consistency.
- ℹ️ Positive: all writes correctly attach x-web2-token (SO.\_w2Auth / \_soAuthHeaders) and server uses requireWeb2AuthSoft; /reset is admin-gated (requireWeb2Admin); queries parameterized; pool is web2Db (correct, not chatDb); no TPOS/Web1 coupling; no hardcoded secrets. Token is read from localStorage we — `so-order-state.js:88-97, so-order-storage-sync.js:42-52, render.com/routes/web2-` → No action for this page; track plaintext-token-at-rest as a project-wide item.

**Ghi chú:** Web 2.0 isolation is clean: uses web2Db pool, /api/web2-\* routes, x-web2-token auth, web2:so-order SSE topic, no TPOS API and no Web 1.0 table/pool/SSE access. web2-optimistic.js is loaded but deliberately bypassed in kho-sync (documented rationale: local Sổ Order is source of truth, kho is derived mirror, rollback would be meaningless).; Single global doc 'main' = the whole shop shares ONE row; there is no per-user or per-tenant partition. Combined with full-state overwrite push, this is the central concurrency risk (high-severity bugs 1-3 all stem from it).; Dropdown [hidden]+display CSS bug mentioned in focus is NOT present for the active dropdown classes: so-supplier-dropdown / so-edit-variant-dropdown use default display:block (no author display override), and so-suggest-dropdown/so-variant-dropdown have explicit `[hidden]{display:none!important}` guards at css/so-order.css:2474; so-ship-adv guarded at 1167.; XSS posture is good: render paths consistently use SO.escapeHtml on supplier/productName/note/variant/code/labels.; File sizes: so-order-storage.js (795), so-order-receive.js (734), so-order-render.js (750) are near the 800-line ceiling but under it.

### page:supplier-debt

Web 2.0 supplier-debt (Công nợ NCC) page: a read-only-ish debt ledger report that aggregates supplier purchases (derived client-side from so-order via Web2SoOrder reader → Postgres /api/web2-so-order/get) against payments+returns (from server ledger GET /api/web2-supplier-wallet/state). It computes per-supplier opening/debit(phát sinh)/credit(đã giảm nợ)/ending in a chosen date period, renders a sortable/paginated table with expandable per-supplier detail (running-balance ledger, purchases, transactions), and supports 3 mutations: create supplier (POST /suppliers), edit note (POST /suppliers), record payment (POST /tx, money op). Realtime refresh via SSE topics. The page was split (2026-06-18 MOVE-only) into state/api/render/actions/filters modules coordinated through internal namespace window.\_\_SupplierDebt. The FOCUS concern is correctly handled: aggregate() only counts rows with status received/partial_received into debit, explicitly excluding draft/cancelled/ordered.
